# Bundle Update Log

## 2026-07-22 (batch 507 — pages 5061-5070)

One day, 14-15 April 2008, a single continuous "onboard entertainment" tangent spun off directly from batch 506's close, its subject line formally retitled partway through from "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox" to "Entertainment onboard Paradox." A laptop was floated as a consolidated stand-in for a stack of individual entertainment gadgets, drawing pushback that it is no substitute for nav lights or a VHF (safety gear, not entertainment); a more conservative, weight-conscious counter-case favored reusing whatever 12-volt gear was already on hand — a bright searchlight doubling as buoy-finder and towboat-pilot deterrent, a full DVD library over a small player's cramped screen, and an old handheld VHF still working plugged into 12V despite a dead battery pack — over buying new. A portable digital picture frame (up to 10 in, built-in speakers, SD-card playback from a camera) was floated as an all-in-one alternative, closed out the next day with a joking correction that there is no "black magic" behind it, only "magic smoke." Set against the electronics case, a minimalist counter-philosophy kept only the safety-relevant items (lights, VHF) and substituted a book and a rarely-played harmonica for everything else, reasoning that films and music are escapism and escaping the boat defeats the point of being aboard it. Where cabin space allows an instrument at all, a compact one won out — harmonica ("blues harp") over guitar (too space-hungry underway) or a ukulele considered as a smaller substitute, with online lesson resources noted — rounded out by a wider onboard-leisure kit (MP3 player, handheld mini-TV, possibly a small laptop-class computer) and closing good-natured exaggeration (a trombone-and-wind-up-gramophone "outfit," the trombone mouthpiece proposed as a stand-in foghorn). No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: new page `operations/onboard-entertainment-and-diversions.md` created in full (all ten of this batch's messages, one continuous thread, narrated in one home file per narrate-once-cross-link) — citations [1]-[10]; `operations/index.md` gained the matching index entry. Correspondents and identity basis: Dennis Mingear (`dennismingear`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 2 msgs, signs "Denny", account+profile, no new trigger met — thread is off his usual outboard/fuel-storage topic); Ray (`knobmaker`/`rhaldridge`, established WATCHLIST section-B non-Paradox-catamaran-owner identity, closed/HOLD at the ~5000 review, 1 msg, signs "Ray", account+profile, not re-evaluated per that closure); Nels (`arvent`/`recree8`, established `people/nels-arvidson.md` identity, 2 msgs, signs "Nels", account+profile, content thin/personal-color so folded into the new topic page rather than his own page, consistent with narrate-once); Alastair (`OpenBoat`/`alopenboat`, established `people/alastair-law.md` identity, 2 msgs, signs "Al, Yeovil, England", account+profile+signature); Pete Martin (`pdmartin`/`randoneur`, established `people/pete-martin.md`+`boats/johanna.md` identity, 1 msg, signs "Pete", account+profile+signature, content is personal-leisure color rather than build technique so not added to his own page, per register-shift's characterization-not-log discipline); Alan (`logicaid`/`logicaidnz`, established WATCHLIST section-B New Zealand identity, 1 msg, signs "Alan", account+profile, no new trigger met — pure joke content); Jim Baltaxe (`Jim.Baltaxe`/`jimbaltaxe`, established `people/jim-baltaxe.md` identity, 1 msg, signs "Jim Baltaxe", account+profile+signature, a one-line joke reply carrying no new characterizing fact so not added to his own page). ## 2026-07-22 (batch 506 — pages 5051-5060)

Two days, 13-14 April 2008, continuing straight from batch 505's close, split across two distinct threads. First, the Cedar Key 2008 invitation from batch 504 (msgnum 5016) finally drew its replies: one of the two invited regular attendees had to decline, a spouse's health having worsened to needing near-constant care, compounded by a daughter with two young children of her own to look after, leaving room at most for a single day's visit; the other confirmed he would still bring "Whisper" to the gathering as planned, so at least one Paradox would be there. Second, the "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox" thread carried from batches 502-505 continued in a new direction — away from fuel storage and into battery/solar/wind power generally. A reply agreeing with the previous batch's sealed-AGM-battery endorsement drew on professional solar/wind-power research experience to debunk both technologies for boat use (real panel output roughly 50% of rated figures; a wind generator that won't turn without beating to weather, and is dockside-blocked besides), illustrated by a field anecdote about the generator needed to power a research microwave, and closed with the recurring thread's own verdict restated: a small petrol outboard and a yuloh remain the soundest choice, storing the fuel the one real problem left. A concrete trolling-motor data point (25 lb battery, 4 hours at 2 knots) followed. The thread then pivoted into a full electrical-system comparison prompted by a non-Paradox correspondent's own catamaran power question: Little Cruiser's 32 Ah SLA battery and three-panel solar array (sufficient for a 100 W HF radio) and Swamp Thing's far more minimal 7.2 Ah gel cell/single-panel system were laid out in full, alongside their respective fuel and cooking-gas consumption figures and the standing "keep it simple" lesson; a motorcycle-battery chemistry caution, a reminder that small devices mostly carry their own batteries, a gel-cell/twin-hull-set recommendation, and a closing flash-memory-over-CD/DVD aside (with a carried harmonica offered half in jest) rounded out the exchange. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: `merits/cedar-key.md` extended "Ahead of the 2008 gathering" — citations [29]-[30] (count/max 28→30); `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md` extended "Continued vane refinement, and a family health setback" with a one-sentence cross-link to the fuller Cedar Key account — citation [278] (count/max 277→278); `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md` gained two new sections, "Real-world solar and wind performance, and a professional's skepticism toward battery-electric propulsion" and "Little Cruiser and Swamp Thing's own electrical systems, and battery choice for continuous small loads" — citations [127]-[134] (count/max 126→134); a stray UTF-8 byte-order-mark at the very start of this same file (the only one found tree-wide, confirmed by a full-tree scan) was also stripped in passing, resolving a `shapecheck.js` NOFRONT false-positive with no content change. Correspondents and identity basis: Glen Maxwell (`ifida`/`ifida2000`, established `people/glen-maxwell.md`+subpages identity, 1 msg, signs "Glen", confirmed via account+profile); Dave Bolduc (`mbolduc`/`boldav38`, established `people/bolduc/dave.md` identity, 2 msgs, signs "Dave", confirmed via account+profile); Nels (`arvent`/`recree8`, established recurring identity, 2 msgs, signs "Nels", confirmed via account+profile); Alastair (`OpenBoat`/`alopenboat`, established identity, 3 msgs, signs "Al, Yeovil, England", confirmed via account+profile+signature); Ray (`knobmaker`/`rhaldridge`, established WATCHLIST section-B non-Paradox-catamaran-owner identity, 1 msg, signs "Ray", confirmed via account+profile, no new trigger met — his own boat, not a Paradox, is the subject of the power question); Alan (`logicaid`/`logicaidnz`, established WATCHLIST section-B New Zealand identity, 1 msg, signs "Alan", confirmed via account+profile, no new trigger met). ## 2026-07-22 (batch 505 — pages 5041-5050)

Two days, 12-13 April 2008, continuing straight from batch 504's close, entirely within the "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox" thread, with its two strands (fuel storage/filler-cap and battery-as-ballast charging) interleaved through the same afternoon before the thread's final message the next morning. On fuel storage: the copper-tank proposal drew skepticism on two counts — a built-in tank's own leak risk versus a portable can's recoverability, and the extra pump-and-pipe plumbing a built-in tank would need — plus an independent "over-engineered" verdict. The underlying problem, a lost filler cap, drew three fixes: drilling a hole and fitting a pipe to pump fuel in without removing the cap; a simpler cord lanyard on the cap (and funnel); and a factory-built answer already on record — a Honda 2.3 hp whose cap ships with an internal chain. A "supply tank"'s own siting was questioned, then resolved: no built-in tank is needed at all — the existing 5-litre plastic can itself is the supply tank, decanted by cap-off-and-pipe from inside the cabin, a method attributed to Dave Bolduc's own practice. On batteries: the sealed-battery (Gel Cell/AGM) answer from batch 504 drew a skeptical follow-up — safer against acid spills, but reportedly needing more careful, costlier charging, illustrated by a sealed battery's short working life on a microlight aircraft — countered with general charging guidance (avoid over/under-charging; below 10.5 V repeatedly degrades a battery; consistent overcharging kills even abuse-tolerant cells) and the blunt observation that an expensive battery needs an expensive charger. A theory for the microlight battery's short life followed: a solid-state regulator's 14.4 V cruising-speed charge, fine for wet lead-acid but potentially lethal for some sealed gel cells — a risk carried over to a boat's own alternator — illustrated by a wet-battery mess cleaned from an aerobatic aircraft's fuselage, with an open question left on record about solar-panel overcharging. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md` gained two new sections, "Reactions to the copper-tank proposal, and refining a cap-and-pipe fueling method" and "Sealed-battery charging care, cost, and a light-aircraft cautionary tale" — citations [117]-[126] (count/max 116→126). Correspondents and identity basis: Jim Hall (`flexwing462`/"james hall", established recurring identity carried on the WATCHLIST's unconfirmed-Faith-owner item, 4 msgs, signs "Jim Hall," confirmed via account+profile+signature, no new identity signal); Dennis Mingear (`dennismingear`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 1 msg, signs "Denny," confirmed via account+profile, no new trigger met); Alastair (`openboat`/`alopenboat`, established identity, 3 msgs, signs "Al, Yeovil, England," confirmed via account+profile+signature); Bill Serjeant (`barnacleid`, established `boats/faith.md`/`people/bill-serjeant.md` identity, 1 msg, signs "Bill," confirmed via account+profile, content generic rather than Faith-specific so folded directly into the topic page per existing precedent for his other appearances there); Adrian (`troller`/`trolladrian`, brand-new one-off correspondent, 1 msg, signs "Adrian," no boat/build mentioned, not added to WATCHLIST per the thin-one-off discipline — captured on the citing page instead). Greg (`corporal_nobbs2003`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity) did not post this batch — his prior message (msgnum 5084, batch 504) was only re-quoted as reply context; his WATCHLIST entry is therefore unchanged. ## 2026-07-22 (batch 504 — pages 5031-5040)

Two days, 11-12 April 2008, continuing straight from batch 503's close, entirely within the "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox" thread. The battery-ballast debate continued: a corrected wind-generator output figure (the 400 W rating only holds above ~28 mph; at 9 mph it delivers closer to 50-70 W), an electric catamaran precedent (large battery bank plus a modest panel array, sustaining about 6 knots on panel power alone in good continuous sun), and a longer reply — drawing on a 30-year electronics background — judging that batteries paired with a wind generator would make small extras (radar, a microwave) realistic, with a genuinely decent electric outboard remaining the real bottleneck, and a stretched or NOLI-sized hull floated as easing the whole concept. A historical baseline was offered against the same proposals: Gerry Spiess's 1979 solo Atlantic crossing in a 10 ft boat of his own design ran on outboard fuel and a gas stove, with batteries carried only for a radio and tape recorder. The hydrogen-gas caution drew a second, independent confirmation naming sealed Gel Cell/AGM batteries as the fix. Separately, the original stern-quarter fuel-bin question drew three more answers: vented bins holding removable cans were preferred over built-in tanks (with an aft-deck bin idea for anchor stowage recalled and rejected on ergonomic grounds); a firsthand data point from years of deck-stowed fuel with no problems, including outboard-only river runs of up to 150 miles at a stretch; a new correspondent's caution that ethanol in modern gasoline degrades epoxy and polyester over time, so a **wet** fuel tank (as opposed to a locker holding removable cans) needs vinylester resin specifically; and a closing metal-tank proposal drawn from the published *Of Yachts and Men* — tanks custom-made from 20-gauge copper sheet to fit the stern's odd shapes, filled from rear-deck filler caps and vented through the stern. A separate, one-message exchange closed a carbon-monoxide caution first raised in batch 501: CO monitors and colour-changing oxygen-test cards were recommended as cheap, readily available supplements to good ventilation and common sense. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md` gained two new sections, "A wind generator's real output, and a historical fuel-versus-battery baseline" and "Further stern-quarter fuel-storage proposals: vented bins preferred over built-in tanks, and a copper-tank design" — citations [108]-[116] (count/max 107→116); `boats/whisper.md` gained a new "Deck-stowed fuel, and years without a problem" section — citation [109] (count/max 108→109); `people/david-beard.md` extended the "Outboards, furling, and sea trials" paragraph with the same deck-stowed-fuel fact — citation [267] (count/max 266→267); `construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md` gained a new "Ethanol's effect on epoxy and polyester fuel tanks" section — citation [30] (count/max 29→30); `operations/cabin-heating.md` extended "A carbon monoxide caution for candle and diffuser heaters" — citation [56] (count 62→63 incl. 7 pre-existing letter-suffixed entries 6a-6g, numeric-max 55→56); `people/john-gilbert.md` extended the "Cabin heating" section with the same CO-monitor follow-up — citation [24] (count/max 23→24). Correspondents and identity basis: joe_mapango/Chris Curtis (`ccurtis`/`joe_mapango`, established WATCHLIST section-A maxi-Paradox-builder identity, 1 msg, signs "Chris Curtis," confirmed via account+profile, resurfaced after a long silence with a purely technical reply, no build-status news, WATCHLIST entry updated in place); Dennis Mingear (`dennismingear`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 2 msgs, signs "Denny," confirmed via account+profile, no new trigger met); John Gilbert (`gilberj55`, established `people/john-gilbert.md` identity, 1 msg, signs "JG," confirmed via account+profile+signature); Greg (`corporal_nobbs2003`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 2 msgs, signs "Greg," confirmed via account+profile, still below the person-page bar, WATCHLIST entry updated in place); Alastair (`openboat`/`alopenboat`, established identity, 2 msgs, signs "Al, Yeovil, England," confirmed via account+profile+signature); David Beard (`dlb`/`buster38801`, established `people/david-beard.md`/`boats/whisper.md` identity, 1 msg, signs "David," confirmed via account+profile — the `dlb@`/`buster38801` = David Beard (Whisper) identity trap checked and confirmed, not David LeBlanc); Michael (`isserfiq`/`michaelsilvius`, brand-new one-off correspondent, 1 msg, signs "Michael," no boat/build mentioned, not added to WATCHLIST per the thin-one-off discipline — captured on the citing pages instead). ## 2026-07-22 (batch 503 — pages 5021-5030)

One day, 11 April 2008, continuing straight from batch 502's close. The "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox" thread ran its full course: a builder already mid-cruise gave his own long-practiced petrol-storage routine (two 5-litre cans in the forepeak, checked for leaks, cabin kept fume-free, never handling fuel near a lit stove) and judged a petrol engine more reliable than electric power over real distances — underscored by his own gale-bound stop at Brighton that made the case directly ("There's no way I would be at Brighton without the engine"). The battery-as-ballast idea then drew a genuine safety and engineering debate: a hydrogen-gas explosion caution (illustrated by a garage battery-charger accident), a clarification that modern sealed cells gas far less than older ones, a chlorine-gas warning from saltwater reaching batteries and wiring, a numbers-based critique of solar/wind power for combined propulsion-and-cooking loads (with a pedal or towed water generator and a resistive immersion heater offered as better bets), a household-microwave cooking-time data point, and a named wind-generator product (Air-X Marine Mx-1, 400 W at 12 V) to pair with batteries replacing lead ballast outright. A prospective builder separately restated his stern-quarter fuel-locker mock-up with a new mainsheet-fairlead routing detail, mirroring another owner's self-steering accommodation. On a parallel "'Faith' cruising" thread: a builder thanked "Jeff UK" for a sailmaker referral and asked two follow-up questions (left unanswered on the list), and a fellow UK builder tracked the cruise's early leg on Google Earth, working out the tidal-gate timing a Roach-and-Middleway passage past Havengore Bridge would have needed. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md` extended "A 2008 recurrence: a published account, quarter-tank proposals, and a pivot to electric power" with four new paragraphs — citations [100]-[107] (count/max 99→107); `boats/faith.md` extended "A spring 2008 cruise begins, weatherbound at Ramsgate" and gained a new section, "Gale-bound at Brighton: a petrol-storage routine, and a case for reliability over electric power" — citations [195]-[196] (count/max 194→196); `people/pete-martin.md` extended "A sailmaker inquiry, and pricing acrylic window sheet" and gained a new section, "Following Faith's spring 2008 cruise" — citations [61]-[62] (count/max 60→62); `boats/johanna.md` extended "A mainsheet question relayed to Glen Maxwell, and the foredeck's stubborn curve" — citation [122] (count/max 121→122). ## 2026-07-22 (batch 502 — pages 5011-5020)

Two days, 10-11 April 2008, continuing straight from batch 501's close: a coda to the NOLI-weather exchange, a plywood-standard thread resolved in full, a new fuel-storage/electric-power thread opened and closed, and a build-cost follow-up. On NOLI: a reply looked forward to more photographs of the boat, joking about digging her out of a snow drift. On plywood: the carried-over "When is marine plywood NOT marine plywood?" thread finally closed — a full read-through of two 1995 magazine issues yielded the BS1088 marine-plywood standard in detail (WBP glue-line requirement, timber-durability classes, veneer-quality and defect limits, moisture content, thickness tolerances, face-veneer-thickness formulas, and the Bruynzeel boil-test exception), endorsed by a second correspondent's own boatbuilding experience. On "Faith" 's cruise blog: the device behind it was named (a mini Asus EEE laptop with WiFi, usable only at hotspots, with an iPod Touch under consideration for future cruises), and a passage-making/navigation-lights question was answered (48 hours judged the practical maximum before preferring a proper night at anchor; a single legal all-round masthead light backed by a powerful flash lamp and white flares). A new thread, "Outboards and fuel storage onboard Paradox," opened at the pre-build stage — prompted by a published solo trans-Atlantic account of cabin fuel odor and a nearby owner's lost outboard filler cap — asking whether sealed fuel bins could be built into the stern quarters; it pivoted into an electric-auxiliary-power argument (batteries replacing lead ballast, charged by a small wind generator, running a radar set, radio, and GPS/nav computer, doubling as house power when docked), drawing interest in a dedicated electric-boats group, an open question over solar-panel charging capacity, and a returning correspondent's own galley-side idea — a miniature battery-powered microwave oven. Separately, reacting to a previously-recorded ~£2,500 total build-cost figure, a prospective builder settled on buying materials incrementally rather than saving up first. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max: `boats/noli.md` extended "Painting begins, and a spring 2008 launch target restated" — citation [62] (count/max 61→62); `boats/faith.md` extended "A spring 2008 cruise begins, weatherbound at Ramsgate" — citations [193]-[194] (count/max 192→194); `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` (the special multi-block, continuously-numbered file per WATCHLIST) gained a new "The BS1088 marine-plywood standard, read in full" section — citations [102]-[103] (count/max 101→103); `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md` gained a new "A 2008 recurrence: a published account, quarter-tank proposals, and a pivot to electric power" section, cross-linking to the established batteries-as-ballast case on `design/weight-and-ballast.md` rather than duplicating it — citations [96]-[99] (count/max 95→99); `construction/build-effort.md` gained a new Cost-section bullet, "Paying as you go, rather than saving up first" — citation [116] (count/max 115→116). Correspondents and identity basis: Greg (`corporal_nobbs2003`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 6 msgs, signs "Greg", confirmed via account+profile, still below the person-page bar); Bill Serjeant (`barnacleid`, established `boats/faith.md` identity, 2 msgs, confirmed via account+profile); John Pertalion (`john@`/`ajmctaggert`, established WATCHLIST section-B identity, 1 msg, signs "John, Boone, NC", confirmed via account+profile, no new trigger met); Dennis Mingear (`dennismingear`, established WATCHLIST section-C identity, 1 msg, signs "Denny", confirmed via account+profile); "Steve"/`g4mjw` (`steve@`, established one-off identity already noted thin/below-bar in `operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md`'s own citations, 1 msg, signs "Steve/G4MJW," confirmed via account+profile, unchanged assessment). ## 2026-07-22 (batch 501 — pages 5001-5010)

One extended sitting, 8-9 April 2008 (with one message composed 8 April but held for moderator approval until 10 April), closing out the "'Faith' cruising" thread's real subject — a UK builder's own lighter Paradox/Enigma hybrid, "Noli" — plus both open questions left over from batch 500's "Plywood for paradox"/"When is marine plywood NOT marine plywood?" threads, and drawing a carbon-monoxide safety caution in reply to batch 500's candles-and-heat-diffuser guess. On Noli: a fellow UK builder asked after the sailmaker cutting Noli's sail, drawing Caws Sails' full trading address and phone (Cowes, Isle of Wight) and a reaffirmation that Matt Layden's own latest thinking on the Mk2 Paradox sail went to that sailmaker with the order; a separate reply added a wry weather aside, and the builder himself reported uncovering Noli on a fine Saturday only to wake to snow the next morning. The asking builder's own facts — planning to make the sail yard first to check its stiffness before ordering, and pricing acrylic window sheet at nearer £100 a sheet than a wished-for £6 — are recorded on his own person page. On plywood: the carried-over epoxy-brand question drew a no-strong-preference answer (SP Systems used only for price, UK Epoxy well-regarded by reputation); the WBP-versus-true-marine-plywood question drew a worst-case 12mm/9mm fallback answer plus a plyfaq link, with the asker reporting he had since found (but not yet read) two 1995 magazine issues on the identification question, still hadn't seen the plywood stack in question, and was still negotiating a price through a hard-to-reach intermediary. On cabin heating: a correspondent warned that candle/heat-diffuser cabin heat is a genuine, insidious carbon-monoxide risk even given Paradox's good ventilation, reinforced by a second correspondent's own firsthand near-fatal account. No new person or boat pages; nothing to index-confirm. Files touched, all count==numeric-max except the one pre-existing anomaly noted: `boats/noli.md` extended "Sail and sailmaker: Graham of CAWS Sails" and "Painting begins, and a spring 2008 launch target restated" — citations [58]-[61] (count/max 57→61); `people/pete-martin.md` gained a new "A sailmaker inquiry, and pricing acrylic window sheet" section — citation [60] (count/max 59→60); `construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md` gained an SP Systems/UK Epoxy brand data point — citation [29] (count/max 28→29); `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` (the special multi-block, continuously-numbered file per WATCHLIST) gained a new "A worst-case fallback answered, and the BS1088 stamp's reliability doubted further" section — citations [99]-[101] (count/max 98→101); `operations/cabin-heating.md` gained a new "A carbon monoxide caution for candle and diffuser heaters" section — citations [54]-[55] (count 60→62 incl. 7 pre-existing letter-suffixed entries 6a-6g, numeric-max 53→55); `people/jim-baltaxe.md` gained a new "A carbon monoxide warning" cross-link section — citation [7] (count/max 6→7). ## 2026-07-20 (source ingest — Bill's Log: a decade-long first-person build, cruise, and second-hand record)

Mirrored and ingested **Bill's Log** (`bills-log.blogspot.com`), the near-daily blog of **Bill Serjeant**, at `source-web/bills-log/` — **443 Paradox-relevant posts** (of 2,380 screened), **1,707 images (~144 MB)**, spanning **2005-02-17 → 2015-12-07**, plus `index.json` and a provenance README. New reference page [Bill's Log](/references/bills-log.md) documents the source, its three eras, and the citation form; new [Builders and Owners of Paradox Sailboats (2014 census)](/references/builders-and-owners-census-2014.md) carries its 29-boat worldwide table.

**Why it matters: this is the archive's only continuous, contemporaneous account of a single builder, and its only complete second-hand life cycle of a Paradox.** Where the mailing list preserves the community's back-and-forth, the blog preserves one owner's day-by-day narrative — written before outcomes were known, so reversals and mistakes survive intact rather than being tidied out of a retrospective.

**Three eras folded in.** *Faith's build (2005–07)* deepened [boats/faith.md](/boats/faith.md) with the plywood cutting schedule off sheet 6, beam lamination, ballast casting, mast building, the winter hibernation, and launch on 10 June 2007 — closing her record with her 2009 sale and the modifications made by her later owner. *Faith's cruising (2008)* added the Crouch-to-Scillies cruise in dated legs, the repeated Honda outboard failures, and the Cobnor DCA week — including the **man-overboard incident, upgraded from the secondhand treatment the tree carried to its primary source**, with the 2012 retelling identified as the same event rather than a second one. *"Minnow" ex-"Enuf" (2013–15)* gave [boats/enuf.md](/boats/enuf.md) an entire third era across nine new sections (628→748 lines, citations [287]–[352]): delivery from Norway, the vent-box leak traced across eight months, an eleven-post yuloh rebuild synthesised as one narrative, what an eight-year-old Paradox looked like on inspection, and the sale at £2,900 against a documented £4,615.18 build cost.

**Corrections made to the record, not propagated into it.** The mirror's own README calls Faith "built from a kit"; the contemporaneous posts show the Jordan Boats kit was abandoned before any part was cut and the uncut plywood passed on — the bundle was already right and was left right. A flagged "12′10″ vs 4.2 m" LOA conflict is a source typo (4.2 m **is** 13′10″). Two retrospectives say "a year and a half"; the dates say **~22 months**. The rename to "Minnow" was made by the **Norway owner**, not the buyer — the pre-delivery post and the census table agree, correcting the reading a reader agent had proposed. Don Elliott's vague "died by March 2014" resolved to **2 December 2008**. A misattribution between two similarly-named builders was corrected. The 2014 census was identified as a **2006 base layer with two named corrections**, not a current snapshot — evidenced by its stale "Bill Serjeant | Faith" row.

**Left explicitly unsettled.** A secondhand report that "Zoë" was destroyed by fire in road transit is recorded on [boats/zoe.md](/boats/zoe.md) as an **unconfirmed** report only, hedged in prose and citation, with the boat's history not rewritten around it. Mark Waters' location (Los Angeles per his own 2006 account; Portland, Oregon per the census) is recorded as a discrepancy rather than silently resolved.

**New pages:** [boats/scout.md](/boats/scout.md), [boats/gorfnik-paraduck.md](/boats/gorfnik-paraduck.md), [people/sean-mulligan.md](/people/sean-mulligan.md) (explicitly distinguished from Sean Herron), plus the two reference pages above — all indexed. Several empty rig and component stubs (clew, sail, mk2-sail, internal-rigging, topping-lift, vent-baffle, bottom, deck-beams, ballast, water-tanks) were built out from placeholders into real articles.

## 2026-07-20 (source ingest — the Mk3 sail's origin document)

Mirrored the Really Simple Sails page on the **larger, higher-peaked Paradox sail** to `source-web/reallysimplesails/` (page HTML, text extraction, both content images, README). [components/rig/mk3-sail.md](/components/rig/mk3-sail.md) went from an empty placeholder to a full article: the sail is **~15% larger than the original** with a **higher-peaked yard**, drawn in **2023** by **Michael Storer** of Really Simple Sails for an owner who found his boat "sticky" in light wind, with the **sail centre deliberately held in place** so it retrofits to most existing Paradoxes on three spar-length changes (yard +100 mm, boom +50 mm, mast +150 mm at the base, no diameter change). The anticipated reefing "belly" and its yoga-mat foam-lens remedy are recorded, including that trials found the remedy unnecessary.

**A false inference caught and reversed.** The Bill's Log fan-out had attached a 2013 one-off replacement sail and a 2014 "latest sail plan" reference to the Mk3 page — both predating the Mk3 by a decade. Both were removed, the 2014 peaked-head material relocated to [mk2-sail.md](/components/rig/mk2-sail.md), and mk3-sail.md now states the constraint in-file so it is not re-introduced. The sailmaker's chine-runner commentary — that on the designer's own authority the runners work **only** in conjunction with a hull deep in the water, with a Falmouth Quay Punt offered as the traditional parallel — was folded into [chine-runners/how-they-work.md](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md) [75].

## 2026-07-20 (source ingest — "Paradox Sea Trials": a known article's substance folded in from a cleaner reprint)

Mirrored and ingested the *Duckworks Magazine* (2004) reprint of **Glen Maxwell's "Paradox Sea Trials,"** the first-person account of the **3–9 December 2001** shakedown of "[Zoë](/boats/zoe.md)" from Cedar Key to Honeymoon Island, Florida. Local capture at `source-web/duckworks/04/s/articles/paradox/` — unmodified article HTML, ten photographs (`ST01`–`ST10`), and a companion `index.md` recording provenance and indexing the contents. Only that page was taken; the rest of Duckworks remains uncrawled.

**Not a new source — a better copy of one already held, whose substance had never been folded in.** The article was already known to the bundle as a *bibliographic fact*: `references/messing-about-in-boats.md` [16] recorded its August 2003 MAIB publication (vol. 21 no. 7, pp. 16–18) in a single summary sentence, and two 2003 list messages discussing it are cited in `boats/zoe.md` [25][26]. The MAIB deep-fold resolved at the ~5000 review earlier the same day landed eight facts, **none of them from this article**. Its content was therefore genuinely unloaded, and `boats/zoe.md` covered the cruise in two sentences derived from list chatter. The Duckworks text was verified **identical** to the MAIB scan `messingaboutinbo217hick_0.md`; it is preferred as the working witness only because it is native digital text where the MAIB copy is OCR. Every citation added names **both** publications, so the fold does not misrepresent a 2003 magazine article as a 2004 web one.

**Six pages folded, one citation each, all at local next-sequential numbers.** `boats/zoe.md` — the frozen "Sea trials route: Cedar Key to Clearwater" section expanded from two sentences to the cruise in full: dates, the declined 57 nm Gulf crossing, daily runs (11.8 / ~33 / 25.1 nm), the archive's densest speed set for the design (2.5–3 kt in ~5 mph; 4 kt in ~8 kt, ¾ kt short of calculated hull speed; 5.5–5.9 kt sustained in 12–15 kt; 6.3 kt GPS maximum never witnessed live; 2.8–3.2 kt over grass-scraping shallows), the ~8 in side-deck margin and the documented growth in deck confidence across the week, the Layden accommodation credit, an early builder-afloat use of "micro-cruising," and the closing punch list — citation [60]. `operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md` — new section on the yuloh coming off its bronze ball and the captive-line "training device" improvised underway, plus the bridge transit stemming a foul tide under scull — [43]. `components/rig/furling.md` — the December 2001 origin of the topping-lift solution prepended to its existing section: the insufficiently stiff boom-end tang binding and furling the mainsheet into the sail, and the sail dropping as the halyard slacked — [82]. `operations/self-steering.md` — the earliest dated tiller-line self-steering account, establishing that the technique's limit is **point-of-sail dependent** (unsatisfactory on a broad reach, satisfactory to windward) and the resulting Autohelm 800 intention — [88]. `components/hatch.md` — new section on the below-decks helm station in a three-foot following sea and the case for a port in the hatch, cross-linked forward to the skylight that answered it — [4]. `chine-runners/how-they-work.md` — new section on roll damping running free — [68]. `references/duckworks-magazine.md` — the site promoted from a one-line tip-reprinter to a documented republisher, with the capture recorded — [3].

**One claim deliberately left unsettled.** The roll-damping observation (4.3 kt running free, only slight oscillation despite **no boom vang**, recorded as a surprise against a construction-long worry) was folded in as the **earliest dated firsthand instance** of a claim the tree previously held only in undated form. Its source offers two explanations and adjudicates neither — chine runners acting as dampers, or a balanced lug simply not being roll-prone — and `design/weight-and-ballast.md` independently advances a third, athwartship ballast distribution by the radius-of-gyration principle. The new section names all three and labels the runner explanation plausible and repeatedly reported but **not demonstrated** against the competing rig and ballast explanations. Where the record does not settle a question, the tree says so.

**Verification.** `anchorcheck.js` clean — 0 broken across **4,596** anchors (up from 4,365 at the MAIB ingest). `citecheck.js` clean — 0 files with issues. A numbering collision was caught before it landed: `chine-runners/how-they-work.md` was initially miscounted as topping out at [66] when its true last citation is [67], and the roll-damping marker was corrected [67]→[68] before the citation was appended. Headings and existing citation blocks untouched throughout. Net tree: **0 new `.md`** in `bundle/` (no new pages; nothing to add to any index), +3 files under `source-web/`.

## 2026-07-20 (source ingest — "Paradox," DCA Bulletin 193 pp. 45-47: the archive's only independent sail trial)

Ingested the **companion article** in the same *Bulletin* issue as the owner's appraisal ingested earlier the same day — a three-page evaluation at pp. 45-47 by a different hand, already held locally at `source-articles/gilchrist/paradox-by-duncan-gilchrist/` and verified byte-identical to the publisher's PDF. New reference page ["Paradox" — DCA Bulletin 193](/references/dca-paradox-gilchrist.md), indexed.

**What makes it a distinct source, not a duplicate.** The author was neither owner nor builder: he approached the design from **declared scepticism** that a keel-less, board-less flat bottom could work to windward, and arranged a day and a half aboard "Little Jim" at Chew Valley Lake SC in October 2006 expressly to test it — with the club president standing a rescue boat by for the launch. This is the **only published independent sail trial of a Paradox** in the archive; everything else on windward performance is owner, builder, or designer testimony. Folded to [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md), [chine runners](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md) (positioned against the page's existing deflector-vs-foil debate, since the article records a **bottom-rocker-lift** theory alongside the chine runners and declines to adjudicate), [pointing and tacking](/operations/pointing-and-tacking-angles.md), [beam and stability](/design/beam-and-stability.md), [sailing characteristics](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md), and [building plans](/references/building-plans.md).

**Two open questions closed on the companion page.** *Dating:* [Sailing The Paradox](/references/dca-sailing-the-paradox.md) recorded *Bulletin* 193 as undated, inferring "2005 or 2006" from a count of seasons. This article dates the issue from inside it — Beale Park Show **2006**, plans arriving **October 2006**, photographs credited to October 2006 — so the issue cannot predate late 2006 and most likely appeared winter **2006-07**, on stated dates rather than inference. *Naming:* the article spells the forename **"Alastair"** in running text throughout and gives the full name in an editorial photo credit, against the **"Alistair"** byline two pages later. A single issue setting the name **both ways** is direct print-side evidence that "Alistair" is a typesetting variant — confirming from the other direction what [Alastair Law](/people/alastair-law.md) had settled on self-identification alone, and adding a third independent print attestation of the surname.

**One transcription fault flagged, not silently fixed.** The printed article sets heel angles and field of view with a **percent sign where a degree sign is meant** ("20%", "35%", "55%", "360% vision"), while other angles in the same article ("45°", "90°") are set correctly — a character-mapping fault. The figures are recorded as degrees on [beam and stability](/design/beam-and-stability.md) with the reading made explicit, and the fault documented on the reference page, so the interpretation stays traceable.

**Scoped deliberately.** The windward verdict rests on a single spell of ~8 mph in an otherwise 0-4 mph day, which the article itself states; that caveat is carried into every page citing the figures rather than dropped. The article's negative finding — she enters a tack fast, drops level onto her flat bottom and slows, her weight only just carrying her round — independently corroborates the slow-tack disadvantage the owner had listed in the companion piece, from a different observer on a different day.

## 2026-07-20 (source ingest — "Sailing The Paradox," DCA Bulletin 193; and the Alastair Law naming resolution)

Ingested a signed two-page owner's appraisal of the Paradox from the Dinghy Cruising Association *Bulletin* 193 (pp. 48-49), transcribed from PDF to `source-articles/dinghy-cruising-association/sailing-the-paradox/index.md`. New reference page ["Sailing The Paradox" — DCA Bulletin 193](/references/dca-sailing-the-paradox.md), indexed, plus a published-appraisal section on [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md).

**Naming resolved, and the page renamed.** `people/alastair.md` → [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md), with 563 links across 31 files rewritten and all anchors preserved. The **surname** is attested in print three times independently — the AYRS talk's YouTube title, this *Bulletin* byline, and *MAIB* July 2009 — where the mailing list records only a first name; two pages previously claiming the AYRS talk was "the only source" for it were corrected. The **forename** is settled against the printed sources rather than by them: both magazines print "Alistair," but the sending account's own `From:` display name reads "Alastair" in 321 archive messages against 0, and every message signs off "Al, Yeovil, England," so self-identification is taken as controlling and the printed spelling recorded as a publication-side variant. In-prose first-name references were deliberately left untouched; only identity-bearing spots (person page, boat page, both indexes) carry the full name.

**Scoped by audit, not by list length.** All 26 of the article's advantages/disadvantages were checked against the existing tree first: 17 were already stated (the same builder had said them on-list), 7 partial, 4 genuinely new — so the article is chiefly corroboration-in-print, and the fold was scoped to 11 targeted edits rather than 26. New: boom clearance over the helmsman's head ([rig](/components/rig/index.md)); the 100 mm (4") stem post ([stem](/components/stem.md), previously a bare stub); drying out level *athwartships*, a separate axis from the documented fore-and-aft beaching trim ([beam and stability](/design/beam-and-stability.md)); and a foredeck-strapped fuel can, against an existing record of aft stowage only ([auxiliary outboard power](/operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md)). Partial additions: ~1 hr car-to-boat rigging ([trailering](/operations/trailering.md)); 4 min anchor-to-pillow and 10 min bed-to-sailing ([ground tackle](/operations/ground-tackle.md)); sailing all points in under 1 ft with the rudder raised ([chine runners](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md)); the named Wareham Channel boundary case ([pointing and tacking](/operations/pointing-and-tacking-angles.md)); and windward passage speed in company ([sailing characteristics](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md)).

**Two contradictions recorded as tensions rather than resolved.** The article's flat "a sculling oar has no reverse and no brake" is not reconcilable with the Bolducs' published re-pitching technique already cited on [sculling and yuloh technique](/operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md); both are kept, with the likely distinction — a maneuver set up over several strokes versus one needed in the seconds before a collision — stated as conjecture. And the article's framing of the 70-litre ballast as a drinking-water reserve "if required" sits against the same owner's on-list statement that he does not drink his own ballast water, having found "interesting slime" in the tank; recorded on [weight and ballast](/design/weight-and-ballast.md).

Also filled a gap in [Messing About In Boats](/references/messing-about-in-boats.md), whose coverage history had no 2009 entry: the July 2009 issue (vol. 27 no. 3) carried Bill Serjeant's abridged Scillies cruise account, sailed in company with "Little Jim," which is where the third surname attestation appears. One discrepancy flagged and left unreconciled: the sail plan reproduced on p. 49 labels the design "4.8m COASTAL CRUISER" against the 4.2 m LOA recorded elsewhere — inherited from Matt Layden's own 1994 drawing, not introduced by the *Bulletin*. Verification: anchor check 4,561 links / 0 broken, citation check 0 issues.

## 2026-07-20 (fix-pass FP5 — folding the MAIB reference pages into the thematic tree)

The two MAIB reference pages (`references/messing-about-in-boats.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`, both ingested and renumbered 2026-07-19) had never had their facts folded into the thematic tree. This pass did that fold: 8 targets, each confirmed on the reference page by TEXT (never trusting a stale quoted citation number), then added to its destination in that page's own voice with a citation taking the destination file's own next-sequential number.

1. **Published design particulars** (prismatic coefficient 0.58, displacement/length ratio 270, from the "Paradox / 4.2 m Coastal Cruiser" specifications box) → new `# Published design particulars` section on `design/hull-scantlings.md`, citation [21].
2. **Dave Bolduc's 1997 published windward validation** (Paradox vs. the centreboard Little Cruiser over two months, "not enough difference... to suggest altering Paradox") → new `## Dave Bolduc's 1997 published windward validation` section on `chine-runners/windward-performance.md`, placed just above the existing `## Pointing angles owners report` and explicitly dated/cross-referenced as the earlier, published form of the same claim microcruising.com gives undated; citation [13].
3. **Vinegar unsafe on skin** (Gougeon Bros./WEST SYSTEM technical advisor, 1997) → new `## An earlier, professional warning against vinegar on skin (1997)` section on `construction/epoxy-coating-technique.md`, cross-referencing rather than replacing the existing 2003 mailing-list warning; citation [112].
4. **The rudder's "NASA airfoil section"** (radiused leading edge, max thickness ~1/3 back, small flat trailing edge that "pulls the water onto the blade's surface") → appended to `## Foil section: not a standard NACA airfoil` on `components/rudder.md`, with the NASA-vs-NACA-0006 discrepancy stated plainly and left unreconciled per the brief; citation [79].
5. **"Designs on old envelopes" / 1982 *Cruising World* Design Competition (pub. July 1983) + *Small Boat Journal*** → new paragraph in the `# Origins and formation` section of `matt-layden/biography.md`, citations [59][60].
6. **The 1983–84 "Layden Sightings" log** (young Matt single-handing "Terrapin of New London, CT," leeboards, fully battened sail, closed-in hull) → new `## The 1983–84 sighting log` section on `matt-layden/terrapin.md`, dating the boat two years earlier than the page's prior c. 1985 sourcing and flagging the unreconciled discrepancy; citation [7].
7. **Sand Flea / 2007 Everglades Challenge kayaker rescue** (Matt as "Wizard," finished <½ day behind a crewed boat) → new paragraph in the race-record section of `matt-layden/sand-flea.md`; citation [2].
8. **Cedar Key sightings cluster** (2004 attending; 2005 new open-topped chine-log design; 2006 sailing-kayak hull approval; 2007 hosting the cookout and introducing Sven Yrvind) → new `## Cedar Key sightings, 2004–2007` subsection on `matt-layden/biography.md` under `# The man himself`, cross-linked against the existing, later, and distinct 2011 Layden–Yrvind consultation; citations [61]-[64].

**Two verified no-ops (left untouched, per the brief):** `components/hatch.md` was already built from and directly cites the "submarine" hatch source — confirmed, no change. `construction/water-tank-lining.md`'s System Three/WEST potable-tank content was already present from secondhand mailing-list recollection — given a **citation-only upgrade**: restored the pre-existing orphaned `[35]` marker and added the new primary MAIB source `[46]` alongside it (System Three's five-coat method/five-day vinegar cure; WEST's stated reason for declining potable-tank use), no new prose beyond the one sentence needed to carry the citation.

**Verification:** `anchorcheck.js` and `anchorcheck2.js` both ran clean on the first pass — 4,559 anchor links checked, **0 broken**, **0 missing-target-file** (all cross-file anchor slugs, including several with digit-adjacent en-dashes in the source headings, computed correctly against the checkers' slug algorithm before running them). `citecheck.js` — **0 files with issues, tree-wide**: no GAP/DUP/DANGLING/NOBLOCK anywhere, confirming the two reference pages' pre-pass renumbering held and that every citation added in this pass took its destination file's correct next-sequential number rather than reintroducing a phantom gap.

## 2026-07-20 (fix-pass FP4 — Jeff UK identity resolution + four new pages)

**Task 1 — closed the archive's longest-running identity question.** Verified raw headers on `source-emails/page_240.eml`, `page_244.eml`, `page_258.eml` (`doryman_uk`, Jan 2002, `X-Yahoo-Profile: roverticket`, `u=54619609`) and `page_880.eml`, `page_951.eml`, `page_1281.eml` (`apcf14`, Mar–Nov 2003, same `X-Yahoo-Profile: roverticket`, `u=117851260`) directly against the source archive rather than trusting the existing citation summaries. The profile handle `roverticket` is constant across both e-mail accounts; only the per-subscription `u=` token changes — the same instability already documented for `joe_mapango`/`ccurtis`. `people/jeff-doryman.md`'s §"Disambiguation: 'Jeff UK' (`roverticket` / 'Jay')" (treating the two accounts as an unresolved open question) rewritten into §"Confirmed identity: doryman_uk and apcf14 are the same person" — a condensed, evidence-based note stating the header proof and retracting the old open-question framing, plus a note on the file-slug naming history (his signature was always "Jeff UK," never "doryman"; the slug is left as-is, not renamed, per its many inbound links). Frontmatter title/description and the page's lead paragraph rewritten to state one continuous biographical arc — 2001 Paradox-derivative intent → 2002 "Paramour" (abandoned) → quiet years → Sept 2006 "PARANIGMA" → Jan 2007 renamed **NOLI** → built 2007–2008 toward a launch — replacing the old two-strands-of-two-people framing; the file's existing chronological section order already matched this arc, so no other headings or citations were touched. Heading-anchor change repointed at 3 inbound links (`operations/self-steering.md` ×2, `people/don-elliott.md` ×1) from the old `#disambiguation-jeff-uk-roverticket-jay` anchor to the new `#confirmed-identity-dorymanuk-and-apcf14-are-the-same-person`. `people/index.md` blurb updated to match.

**Task 2 — new page `people/john-gilbert.md`.** Consolidated "JG"/`gilberj55` (real name John Gilbert, confirmed in `From:` headers on msgnum 4460/4890/4907/4928/5040) — a non-builder technical commentator, Dec 2006–Apr 2008 — from 16 files into one characterisation page: ballast physics/weight/trailering, scaling, rig classification, hull construction/scantlings/finishing, chine runners, sculling and the yuloh (his richest topic, 3 posts), self-steering, cabin heating, and community presence, each a short characterisation cross-linking to where the technique already lives in full (narrate once, cross-link — no re-narration of topic-page content). 23 citations, locally numbered 1–23, each with a "full text and citation at" pointer, matching the `william-longyard.md` consolidation precedent. Disambiguated against **Jack Gardiner** (`alex29`/`tidybowlmann`, owns "Jill," different account/era/subject) in both directions — a note added to `people/jack-gardiner.md` too. Indexed at `people/index.md`.

**Task 3 — new page `people/jim-baltaxe.md`.** `jim.baltaxe`/`jimbaltaxe`, Island Bay, Wellington, New Zealand — promoted on the prospective-builder trigger (plans ordered and confirmed arrived, msgnum 5013) plus a coherent arc: a Derek Kelsall vinylester/vacuum-infusion composite workshop → weighing a foam/glass Paradox build → a foam-core chine-runner proposal → two asides on `people/jeff-doryman.md` (the NOLI-name question, a solar mast-light suggestion) → ordering stock plans instead. 6 citations across 4 files (`chine-runners/construction.md`, `construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md`, `references/building-plans.md`, `people/jeff-doryman.md`). No boat page — no build reported. Indexed at `people/index.md`.

**Task 4 — new pages `people/jimmy-lu.md` and `boats/taiwan-paradox.md`.** `jimmylu88`, Taiwan — a first-time boatbuilder whose rapid, professionally finished mid-build photos (Jul–Aug 2007) drew unprompted praise from Pete Martin, Glen Maxwell, and an unrelated one-off "Bill," and whom Bill Serjeant added to the official UK owners/builders registry (`smallsailboats.co.uk/paradox/names.htm`). Build/launch narrative (registry listing, an anchor-weight/foam-flotation Q&A with Glen Maxwell, continued winter praise, launch 18 February 2008) moved onto the new boat page per the boat-page convention; the person page stays a short characterisation + summary + link. The boat has **no recorded name** — confirmed by an exhaustive search — titled "Jimmy Lu's Paradox (Taiwan), unnamed" per the `boats/aussie-paradox.md` slug precedent, with the missing name stated plainly in the prose rather than hidden. 13 citations on the boat page (locally numbered), 2 on the person page (both pointing into the boat page). Both indexed at `people/index.md` / `boats/index.md`. Source content in `boats/johanna.md`, `people/pete-martin.md`, `people/bill-serjeant.md`, and `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md` left in place (Jimmy Lu is a named third party on those pages' own existing prose, not deleted/renumbered) — the new pages duplicate the underlying archive messages under fresh local citation numbers, matching the established consolidation pattern.

**Task 5 — two HOLDs recorded, no wiki change.** Per the brief, **Chris Ostlind** (`wedgesail`) and **Ray** (`knobmaker`/`rhaldridge`) stay below the cleared-bar precedent (Longyard 32 files, LeBlanc 38) — no pages created; to be logged in the watch-list in a later pass.

**Verification:** `anchorcheck.js` and `anchorcheck2.js` both ran clean — 4,544 anchor links checked, **0 broken**, **0 missing-target-file**. `citecheck.js` — 0 new DUP/DANGLING/NOBLOCK; the only GAP files reported are the same 7 known pre-existing ones (`operations/cabin-heating.md`, `design/blue-water-suitability.md`, `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md`, `references/messing-about-in-boats.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, `people/jack-gardiner.md`) — `jack-gardiner.md`'s own edit here (the John Gilbert disambiguation note) added prose only, no citation markers, so its pre-existing gap is unchanged. All four new pages confirmed listed in `people/index.md` / `boats/index.md`.

## 2026-07-20 (fix-pass FP3 — narrate-once-cross-link: five person→boat moves)

Largest pass of the ~5000 holistic review: enforced the "narrate once, cross-link" rule — a boat's build/launch/cruise narrative lives in full on `boats/<boat>.md`; the builder's `people/<person>.md` keeps a characterisation + contribution catalogue + a 1-2 sentence summary and link. Five person/boat pairs fixed, per the project owner's own example (`boats/johanna.md`) plus the clearest whole-page case (`stephen-yahn.md`/Ironsides).

**Task 1 — new page `boats/ironsides.md`.** `people/stephen-yahn.md` (~180 lines) carried a complete, named Paradox build — "Ironsides" — with no boat page at all, the clearest case in the tree of a person page doing 100% of a boat's narrative duty. Created `boats/ironsides.md` with the full build chronology: a widened, steel-reinforced bulkhead 3 (41 in cabin-sole figure included); a side-mounted outboard fabricated to spare Stephen's hands (motor-mount idea → confirmation → fuel/anticavitation setup → finished construction with photos → the afloat-fitting water-shipping risk); the vent-trunk drain build (copper-pipe lightning ground, grooved-block construction, wheat-flour-thickened-epoxy fill technique); rudder gudgeons machined from Boeing surplus aluminium plus a first attempt at powder coating; and the pendulum self-steering idea. 15 citations, locally renumbered. `people/stephen-yahn.md` rewritten in place: kept the pre-build Q&A, tap-test/tiller-shape/rudder-pivot/furling/steel-Colvin technique catalogue, tiller-rope-lock and Honda-2hp reliability notes, dead-drum proposal, Faith-progress comparison, plywood-handling tip, community-welcome sections, window-tint Q&A, the general pipe-vs-tube sizing explanation (Andrew Graham's cut-in-half alternative kept), Gorilla Glue/acrylic-latex/cold-cure-epoxy/windvane-materials/burgee/cabin-heating/rudder-blade-stop/oil-enamel contributions — removed the five build sections named above, replacing them with a single "Building 'Ironsides'" summary + link; citations renumbered 1-66 → 1-51 (15 removed, no gaps). **`boats/ironsides.md` added to `boats/index.md`.** Three inbound links from `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md` and one from `components/vent-box.md` repointed from the old person-page anchor to the new boat-page anchors.

**Task 2 — `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md` → `boats/zoe.md`.** Six sections moved/split, using the sub-file's own citation numbering (Glen Maxwell is a hub; this is a sub-page). Two verbatim-duplicate sections ("A rudder up-haul safety lapse…", "A full sea trial and course-holding test…") collapsed to one-sentence pointers, headings kept alive (zoe.md's own citations [42]/[43]-[50] already cross-link back to these anchors). Two homeless sections moved in full: "The choice to leave 'Zoë' without foam flotation" and "Foam flotation on 'Zoë' revisited, and window tint" (heading text preserved so the internal self-anchor between them, and six external inbound links from `boats/whisper.md`, `people/nels.md` ×3, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, and `construction/window-glazing-materials.md` ×3 plus `people/stephen-yahn.md` ×2, kept resolving after a path-only repoint to `boats/zoe.md`). "Continued vane refinement, and a family health setback" split — the family health-setback sentence (person-level) stays, the vane-gear refinement (undersized blade, Autohelm coupling) moved. "A 2006 personal website, and Zoe bunk cushions" split — the website-launch fact stays, the bunk-cushion praise and shakedown-cruise-write-up praise moved (repointing two references from `boats/faith.md`). Lightly deduped the "Building 'Zoë'" intro against `boats/zoe.md`'s own "The build" hours/launch-date/plywood-grade figures. `boats/zoe.md` gained 9 new locally-numbered citations [51]-[59]; `build-and-zoe.md`'s citation block left untouched (orphaned definitions kept rather than renumbering a 280-citation mega-file — `citecheck.js` treats orphans as normal post-v3, not a defect).

**Task 3 — `people/pete-martin.md` ↔ `boats/johanna.md` (bidirectional).** "Timber sourcing and ring-nail sizing" cut to a 1-2 sentence pointer (johanna.md already fuller). "Epoxy choice: MAS versus FLAG resin, and fillers" moved in full to a new johanna.md section, "Pricing UK marine plywood, and an MAS epoxy and filler choice," alongside the plywood-shopping-list fact split out of "Before the build…"/"Plywood and epoxy shopping…" (ballast decisions and the shopping list moved; the Enigma-460 relay stayed, being person-level news-relay). New johanna.md section "Ballast: lead against concrete, and a bladder-tank alternative" created for the pre-build ballast thread that previously had no boat-page home (5 new citations). Two **reverse-link violations** fixed — johanna.md was deferring to the person page for "the full account," backwards for a boat page: "Welcoming a new North London builder, and a stem-cutting mistake confessed" — the sourcing recap and stem-cutting mistake moved onto johanna.md (reusing its own pre-existing but previously pass-through citation [50]), leaving "welcoming a new builder" (community conduct) on the person page; "Answering a skin-irritation question, and sheer strakes installed" — the sheer-strakes-installed build fact now lives standalone on johanna.md (dropping its own redirect-out link), the skin-irritation Q&A (community/health conduct) stays on the person page. "The drain drilled, and the hull's wet assembly complete" trimmed to a tight summary + link on the person page. 7 new johanna.md citations total ([111]-[117]); three inbound links from `design/weight-and-ballast.md` and one from `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` repointed to the new johanna.md anchors.

**Task 4 — `people/ian-lang.md` → `boats/irene-may.md`.** The person page's entire substantive body ("Starting 'Irene May'," "A shrinking workspace…," "A half-finished hull as motivation…") was a near-word-for-word parallel of what `irene-may.md` already told — collapsed to one "Building 'Irene May'" summary paragraph + link. "A skin reaction to West System epoxy" kept on the person page essentially as-is, per the brief's own guidance that a personal/health contribution others can learn from belongs there.

**Task 5 — `people/bill-serjeant.md` → `boats/faith.md`.** "A 22 mm vent trunk drain proven in a brief force 6" — near-verbatim duplicate of the identically-headed `faith.md` section — trimmed to one clause + link.

**Net effect:** roughly 250-300 lines of build-chronology prose moved off person pages onto boat pages across the five pairs (heaviest: Ironsides ~90 lines into a brand-new page; Zoë ~110 lines moved/added; JoHanna ~100 lines moved/added). Person pages read measurably closer to the `jack-gardiner.md`/`jill.md` exemplar afterward: characterisation + contribution catalogue + a short build summary, not a re-narration.

**Verification:** `anchorcheck.js` and `anchorcheck2.js` both ran clean after two follow-up fixes (a missed `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md` citation-block reference to the old `stephen-yahn.md` Ironsides anchor, and a missed `stephen-yahn.md` citation-block reference to the old `build-and-zoe.md` window-tint anchor — both caught by the checker, not by grep, since the citation-block copies of these links hadn't been swept in the first pass) — 4,476 anchor links checked, **0 broken**, **0 missing-target-file**. `citecheck.js` — 0 new DUP/DANGLING/NOBLOCK; the only GAP files reported are the same 7 known pre-existing ones (`operations/cabin-heating.md`, `design/blue-water-suitability.md`, `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md`, `references/messing-about-in-boats.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, `people/jack-gardiner.md`) — the two of those touched here (`crew-size-and-fit.md`, `plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`) only had citation-entry link targets edited, no citation numbers added, removed, or renumbered. `boats/ironsides.md` confirmed listed in `boats/index.md`.

## 2026-07-20 (fix-pass FP2 — new page `design/sharpie-lineage.md`)

New page, not a batch of emails: created **`design/sharpie-lineage.md`** ("Sharpie lineage: is Paradox a sharpie?") answering the project owner's question directly. The editorial finding is that the *label* is essentially uncontested — a 1996 magazine construction series produced with the designer's cooperation proposes "Chine Runner Sharpie" as a name for the type outright, and a community build manual's own introduction independently calls the boat "a heavy displacement sharpie type" — so the page does **not** frame this as "some say yes, some say no." What the record actually divides on is narrower: (1) which historical sharpie lineage Paradox descends from (a popular assumption favors Phil Bolger; the record corrects this twice in the same 2006 thread toward Ralph "the Commodore" Munroe's Kingfisher/Egret); (2) whether the larger chine-runner-family hulls (a ~38 ft "Modified Sharpie") still count as sharpies at all, given their designer's own contrast with "traditional sharpies['] low sheers, low centers of gravity"; and (3) whether sharpie-typical weathercocking theory actually explains Paradox's own tacking behavior, where a Bolger-derived momentum theory is directly rebutted ("hardly a lightweight Sharpie" at 1,400 lb/14 ft). The page also lays out traits Paradox shares with the type (flat bottom, narrow beam, hard chines, shallow draft, simple panel-and-chine construction, stores-as-ballast practice, exceeding the hull-speed formula) against where she departs from it (no leeboard/centreboard — the chine runner substitutes, itself derived from Bolger's own chine-batten sharpies; an enclosed cabin instead of an open working deck; heavy ballast for self-righting instead of low-freeboard tenderness; a rig worked from inside instead of a cat-ketch), and closes with the type/literature context already established elsewhere in the tree (Parker's *The Sharpie Book*, Chapelle's *American Small Sailing Craft*, Gardner's *Building Classic Small Craft* "A Sharpie" chapter).

**Sources (15 citations, 15 distinct primary sources, all ≤ page_5000 or already-established non-email sources):** four distinct 1996 *Messing About In Boats* "Building Paradox" installments (Parts 1, 4, 5, 13 — the sharpie-history essay, the cockpit-elimination passage, "The Best Open Beachcruiser" brief, and the beam figure), nine archive emails (page_1447, page_2096, page_2097, page_2513, page_3748, page_3751, page_3771, page_563, page_790), microcruising.com (two pages, one citation), and the May 2007 *Small Craft Advisor* Matt Layden interview. Every quote was checked directly against the source `.eml`/scan files before writing, not taken on trust from existing citation summaries; one genuine spelling discrepancy in the source archive ("Ted Monroe" vs. "Ralph 'the Commodore' M.Monroe," both in the same 2006-07-19/21 thread) was preserved as-is in citations rather than silently merged, with body prose using the tree's existing standardized "Munroe" spelling (matching `chine-runners/how-they-work.md`'s prior convention) only where the fuller, named source itself gives the name.

**Cross-linked** (one-sentence pointers added, no content duplicated) from `design/beam-and-stability.md`, `chine-runners/how-they-work.md`, `chine-runners/origin-and-evolution.md`, `operations/sailing-characteristics.md`, `people/chris-morejohn.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`, `design/blue-water-suitability.md`, and `paradox.md` (one sentence in the existing Overview paragraph, no new section). **Indexed** at `design/index.md` (relative link, placed beside the other "is Paradox X" whole-boat classification entry).

**Verification:** `anchorcheck.js` and `anchorcheck2.js` both ran clean after one fix (a heading-anchor guess to `chine-runners/how-they-work.md` corrected to the actual enclosing heading) — 4,471 anchor links checked, **0 broken**, **0 missing-target-file**. `citecheck.js` — no new DUP/DANGLING/NOBLOCK; the only GAP files reported are the same 7 known pre-existing ones (`operations/cabin-heating.md`, `design/blue-water-suitability.md`, `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md`, `references/messing-about-in-boats.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, `people/jack-gardiner.md`), unchanged by this pass since the two of those files touched here (`design/blue-water-suitability.md`, `references/building-paradox-series.md`) only gained a plain cross-link sentence, no new citations.

## 2026-07-20 (fix-pass FP1 — splitting `design/variants/enlarging-for-a-large-sailor.md`)

Structural restructure, not a new batch of emails: `design/variants/enlarging-for-a-large-sailor.md` was misfiled (a "variant" directory holding designer-sanctioned scaling guidance, which is not a variant) and mixed seven unrelated topics, with its worked 20% dimension figures triplicated verbatim across three files. Split into five destinations, all headings moved byte-for-byte identical to preserve the ~37 inbound anchor links across 18 files, repointed by path only.

**Created:** `design/larger-paradox.md` — "Building Paradox larger: the designer-sanctioned scale-up," now the canonical home for the 10% practical ceiling, the worked 20% scale-up (dimensions/sail-area/displacement), the curvature/beam-widening correction, and naming/workload speculation. `operations/registration-and-legal-length.md` — "Registration and the US 'fourteen foot rule'," with a new lede explaining why the stock boat is 13'10" (moved from a stranded paragraph at the end of the old "Flush cabin sides" section) plus the "A bowsprit, and how state registration rules treat it" section. `design/flush-cabin-sides-idea.md` — a sibling of `glass-bottom-idea.md`/`aluminum-construction-idea.md`, holding the "Flush cabin sides, as an unexplored alternative" section (minus the 14 ft paragraph, which went to the registration page).

**Same-day follow-up, per the project owner's direction to document the 10%-vs-20% conflict fully rather than bury it in a paragraph:** gave `design/larger-paradox.md` a new, standalone section, "How far the designer's sanction actually reaches, and what the record does not settle," placed before "Matt Layden's approval…" (a new heading, not subject to the frozen-heading rule). It lays out the full spread of claims as a table — 10% maximum (Larry Geib relaying a direct question to the designer, 2008-03-02), 20% without penalty (the plans-bundled document relayed 2008-01-22), 20% approved in principle (an independent confirmation, 2008-01-22), 20% "all clear" on the same scantlings (the earliest report on record, 2004-08-31), "apparent, tacit blessing" (an explicitly hedged reading, 2004-11-07), and 33-50% "without difficulty" (a builder's own opinion, not attributed to the designer, formed after one reply from Matt Layden about an unrelated 21 ft design and silence on a follow-up offering to pay for his engineering time, 2007-05-21) — states plainly that no message in the archive is from Matt Layden himself on this question, that every claim is secondhand, and that provenance rather than volume is what distinguishes them. Pulled two further citations across from `stretched-paradox.md`, renumbered locally rather than left as cross-links only: [11] Jason Stancil (page_2222, 2004-08-31) and [12] Chris Ostlind (page_2420, 2004-11-07); [13] Chris Curtis's 33-50% opinion (page_4460, 2007-05-21). The paragraph previously tucked under "Matt Layden's approval…" was removed now that its content lives in the dedicated section above it; that heading's own original two sentences are unchanged.

**Renamed:** `design/variants/enlarging-for-a-large-sailor.md` → `design/variants/alternatives-for-a-larger-sailor.md`, trimmed to only the genuinely-alternative-design content that belongs in `variants/`: Michalak's Caroline (the file's most-linked anchor, 9 inbound links, wording untouched), the seaworthy-counterpoint section, and Paramour trimmed from a full restated spec to a short pointer at `paramour-design.md` (which already covers it in full).

## 2026-07-20 (batch 500 — pages 4991-5000) — closes the ~5000-post cycle

One day, 7-8 April 2008, closing out the "'Faith' cruising" and "Luxurious microcruising" threads and the "Plywood for paradox" cost/epoxy exchange, plus a fresh plywood-grade question left open. On the Faith thread: a fellow reader asks whether a nearly finished UK builder's own boat will be launched in time to cross the cruise's path, drawing a construction-progress answer — hull painting in epoxy high-build, a sail on order with a Cowes sailmaker, Perspex windows bought as offcuts, and a late-June/early-July launch target restated — plus a pointer to further online video (the cruise boat under sail in company, alongside separate footage of Matt Layden's Enigma and of Little Cruiser) and anticipation for photos of that other boat's own build and launch. On luxury: firsthand testimony contrasts a leaky, aptly named open 13-footer from the early 1980s with the wine, good books, and phone connectivity of cruising today's boat in comfort, and a further reply gives a firsthand wood-stove account (small-fire, one-stick-at-a-time management) with a guess that a Paradox needs only candles and a heat diffuser. On plywood/cost: a build's total cost is given at last (~£2500, excluding pre-owned gear and the trailer), with buying epoxy in 25-litre bulk drums as the single best saving; the follow-up on which epoxy brand to buy goes unanswered; a plan-reading thank-you closes with a note that a previously shelved companion booklet only made sense once the plans were actually in hand; and a fresh, unanswered question opens on the WBP-versus-true-marine-plywood distinction, the BS1088 stamp's reliability, and whether full glass/epoxy encapsulation makes the grade question largely moot for a boat that spends most of its life on a trailer. No new pages created; nothing to index-confirm. ## 2026-07-20 (batch 499 — pages 4981-4990)

Two days, 6-7 April 2008, running two parallel threads to a close: a "Plywood for paradox" cheap-9mm-substitution question answered in full and independently corroborated, and the "'Faith' cruising" thread's outside interest continuing into a wine/luxury digression and a heater proposal, plus a Google-Earth-versus-paper-atlas exchange. On plywood: a source of cheap surplus 9mm marine plywood drew a confirming answer — Paradox as drawn is very strongly built, similar boats use 6mm or 4mm ply, so 9mm sides should be fine if the reduced strength is kept in mind in close manoeuvres, with a good bottom-lamination joint the part needing real care — followed by a worked construction method (scarf the first bottom layer, lay the second in 150mm strips at 45 degrees like cold-molded construction to avoid voids at the joints) and a related, unanswered question about tracking a build's full component costs for budgeting purposes; a separate, independent answer reached the same conclusion from a different precedent — a lighter, same-size design ("NOLI") already built stitch-and-glue from 6mm topsides and a 9mm bottom. On the Faith cruise: outside readers debated how best to follow the blog from a distance (satellite imagery precise enough to make out people and boats, versus an ordinary paper atlas for a reader still on dial-up) and drifted into a lighter digression on what luxury means aboard a boat this small — a laptop/DVD wish, a wine-bag-in-a-side-bin suggestion, and an explicit rejection of deliberately spartan cruising in favour of miniature comfort — answered with a firsthand heating precedent (a compressed-coal-briquette cast-iron stove, too large for a Paradox, and a proposed scaled-down 3-4 in tube heater). No new pages created; nothing to index-confirm. ## 2026-07-20 (batch 498 — pages 4971-4980)

Six days, 1–6 April 2008, closing the "paradox plans" FAQ recurrence with the UK questioner's own plans finally arriving, then running a rich plan-reading Q&A thread and a new spring cruise report. On plans arrival: a UK newcomer's plans turn up on April Fools' Day, closing out his own earlier turnaround-time question. On plan-reading: fresh from his plans, the same newcomer asks four separate questions working through the drawings for the first time — the stem piece block's undimensioned back face, the vent-trunk/mast-step area, how the cabin side frame's rails (sheet 10) meet the laminated front/rear deck-framing pieces at the corners, and whether the tack strop's deck-entry tube wears into the deck — all answered in full by two established UK builders: don't chase bevel accuracy before assembly, cut oversize and trim freehand at trial assembly, filling any gap with epoxy; the vent-trunk/mast-step base is built up from offcuts and epoxy with the mast and vent trunk fitted to bulkhead 2 before hull assembly, set up horizontally on trestles for repeated trial fits; the cabin side-frame corners simply butt together and are faired with epoxy; the tack strop's tube does wear the deck, addressed with a flared strut foot and an epoxy or tufnol pad, with wear normally confined to the paint since the tie-down holds the strut clear of the deck except under a deep reef; and a general recommendation to build all of a boat's smaller parts before starting the hull, for a clearer workspace and an early motivational finished piece. Separately, the same newcomer floats a cheap-surplus-plywood substitution scheme (9mm sides/deck and doubled bulkheads/bottom in place of the plans' 12/18mm), unanswered on record. A Cedar Key 2008 gathering invitation goes out to the two other regular attendees. A new spring cruise begins aboard "Faith," weatherbound at Ramsgate under a snow forecast, drawing outside interest from a longtime blog reader and a West Wight Potter owner considering a small-boat cruise of his own, plus a cruise-blogging-device question. No new pages created. Files touched, all count==numeric-max unless a pre-existing anomaly is noted: `references/building-plans.md` closed out the plans-turnaround paragraph — citation [183] (count/max 182→183); `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` extended the Cedar Key narrative with a 2008 invitation — citation [43] (count/max 42→43); `people/bolduc/dave.md` gained a new cruise-blogging bullet — citation [112] (count 112→113 incl. 1 pre-existing letter-suffix [26a], numeric-max 111→112); `construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md` extended "Transom and bulkhead #4 crown, and fitting bevels at trial assembly" and "Recommended order" — citations [64]-[66] (count/max 63→66); `components/rig/mast.md` gained a new "Vent trunk and mast step construction, confirmed again" section — citations [20]-[22] (count/max 19→22); `construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md` gained a new "Cabin side frame corners confirmed: just butted and faired" section — citations [51]-[53] (count 59→62 incl. 9 pre-existing letter-suffixes, numeric-max 50→53); `components/rig/tack-strop.md` fleshed out from a 2-line stub into a real page — citations [1]-[3] (new, count/max 3/3); `boats/faith.md` gained a new "A spring 2008 cruise begins, weatherbound at Ramsgate" section — citations [230]-[233] (count 231→235 incl. 2 pre-existing letter-suffixes [133a]/[191a], numeric-max 229→233); `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` (the special multi-block, continuously-numbered plywood file per WATCHLIST) extended "A 2008 recurrence: the cost gap between a 'top job' and an economy build" with the plywood-substitution idea — citation [108] (file-wide count 105→106, numeric-max 107→108; the file carries its own pre-existing count-vs-max gap, unrelated to this batch, unchanged).
