* **Source ingestion (new source type — YouTube): AYRS meeting talk, May 2020.**
  Ingested a YouTube transcript of [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md)'s May 2020
  talk on "[Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md)" to the Amateur Yacht Research
  Society (imported to `source-youtube/`, kept out of the OKF bundle at the
  user's first request, then ingested on the second). Registered the source as
  a new first-class reference concept
  [AYRS meeting talk](/references/ayrs-meeting-video.md) (+ entry in
  [references/index.md](/references/index.md)) — a retrospective firsthand
  primary source, ~17 years post-launch, that largely corroborates Alastair's
  2001–2004 archive record and independently confirms Matt Layden (Florida) as
  designer. Folded the genuinely new/notable facts into their natural homes,
  each cited to the transcript: a new **"A 2020 retrospective: the AYRS talk"**
  section on [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md) (cites [85]–[87]) covering the
  "old man's boat" low-workload character, a boat-show **spring-balance masthead
  righting-moment test** (>350 ft-lb — the only quantified field measurement on
  record), two self-recovered knockdowns, the centre-of-rotation motion insight,
  yuloh cruising, and the boat's ballast evolution; the righting-moment test and
  the ballast evolution into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (cite [83]);
  the centre-of-pitch-rotation comfort insight (and the Helford two-up seasick
  swap) as a new section on
  [sailing characteristics](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md) (cite [30]);
  the ~2 kn all-day yuloh figure and the DCA Thames-raid 50-of-55-mile row into
  [sculling and yuloh technique](/operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md)
  (cite [26]); and a brief cross-linked note + surname ("Law", the only source
  giving it) on the [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) person page (cite [202]).
  Register-compliant: narrate-once on the boat page, topic pages carry the
  universal facts, citations block carries the source. No email cursor change.

* **Source ingestion (YouTube): Sven Yrvind on Matt Layden's chine runners,
  July 2025.** Imported a 15 July 2025 talk-to-camera from Sven Yrvind's YouTube
  channel (transcript at `source-youtube/sven-yrvind-matt-layden-chine-runners-july-2025.md`)
  in which the chine runner's most authoritative outside champion recounts, from
  memory, how he came to Paradox. Registered it as a new reference concept
  [Sven Yrvind video — Matt Layden's chine runners](/references/yrvind-chine-runner-video.md)
  (+ entry in [references/index.md](/references/index.md)) — a late retrospective
  first-person primary source that largely **corroborates** the existing record
  (the 1997 month sailing Paradox, the towline-release broach, the
  coastal-vs-offshore verdict) and adds genuinely new firsthand detail, each
  folded into its natural home and cited to the transcript. On [Sven Yrvind —
  Paradox and the chine runner](/people/yrvind/paradox-and-the-chine-runner.md)
  (cite [5]): the **1997 return-from-Japan timing**, the **mailed drawings →
  Bolduc → Matt** origin, Matt's boyhood ***Cruising World* subscription since
  age 13** (when Yrvind wrote for it), and the **Jensen Beach / Stuart** home
  stay. On [voyages and boats](/people/yrvind/voyages-and-boats.md) (cite [6]):
  Yrvind's **chine-runner Atlantic crossing** (his own 2025 dating: 2011),
  Ireland → Madeira → Martinique, a stiffer boat (~7 cm more beam, less sail
  area) that heeled far less, with a **gale landfall at Marin**. On [Matt
  Layden's biography](/matt-layden/biography.md) (cite [52]): the boyhood
  *Cruising World* / long-following detail, folded into the existing Yrvind
  paragraph. Treated as authoritative firsthand testimony (per the user's note
  that Yrvind is an authority in his field), stated as fact and folded into the
  prose per the register. No email cursor change.

* **Update (batch page_2081–page_2090, msgnum 2105-2114)**: 13-15 May 2004.
  Three threads, none newly opened. (1) **LEON**'s (`melodymann`) "Paradox
  with a difference" follow-up closed out: having read the AF3
  capsize-recovery link and Alastair's stability comparison from batch 208,
  he judged the Paradox the right boat for him going off others'
  experience, but still too small for his liking, noting someone else had
  suggested a small catamaran instead — folded into the existing "List
  culture" section on
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#list-culture)
  (cite [32]). (2) The "water tight boot on the Paradox tiller
  arm"/series-sea-drogue debate, carried over from batches 206-208, saw
  **Robert** (`boat142`) and **Alastair** trade a further round after the
  thread's rubber-baffle-disc resolution: Robert restated the rudder-stress
  case for a bow-led parachute anchor (a sustained 45° turn over a 14 ft
  moment arm for up to 30 hours) against the series drogue's
  bridle/shock-absorber/~90-drogue rigging, and raised a bow-led-anchor
  flooding scenario (a breaking sea overtopping the main vent trunk faster
  than the aft bilge pump could clear it, flooding the forward stateroom).
  **Alastair** countered with firsthand data: during one of his own "less
  sensible" passages, green water repeatedly swept the whole boat, yet only
  a small quantity reached the vent trunk and its own drain cleared it
  faster than it came in, with no stateroom flooding — and questioned
  whether a drogue's pull could plausibly draw the transom a foot out of
  the water as Robert's scenario implied. Robert disclosed he had not yet
  begun building. Alastair described three of his own methods (one used in
  practice) for anchoring off the bow without leaving the cabin. Added as a
  new section, "Bow-led vs. stern-led ground tackle: the rudder-stress and
  flooding-path case, restated," on
  [construction/transom-deck-plate.md](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md#bow-led-vs-stern-led-ground-tackle-the-rudder-stress-and-flooding-path-case-restated)
  (cites [32]-[36]); cross-link bullet on `people/alastair-law.md` (cites
  [199]-[200]); no cross-link needed on Robert (below-threshold, no person
  page). (3) A new, independent "Storm Tactics Handbook" thread: **Smith**
  recommended the book of that title as further reading once the
  tiller-boot debate wound down, while judging Paradox unlikely ever to
  meet the extreme conditions it addresses; **David Beard**, aboard
  "Whisper," asked whether anyone had tried heaving-to in a Paradox;
  **Alastair** offered three untested ideas (fixed boom position exploiting
  the boat's downwind weathercocking with the rudder raised; a couple of
  feet of sheeted-in sail with the rudder turned toward the wind; and
  deliberately unbalancing the boat before a tack); David Beard asked how
  one would actually go about unbalancing the boat, describing his own
  neutral rudder/sail trim and his separate, still-open light-air tacking
  difficulty. Added as a new section, "Untested heaving-to ideas: boom
  position, minimal sail, and deliberate unbalancing," on
  [operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md#untested-heaving-to-ideas-boom-position-minimal-sail-and-deliberate-unbalancing)
  (cites [28]-[31]); cross-links on `people/david-c-smith.md` (cite [145]),
  `people/david-beard.md` (cites [215]-[216]), and `people/alastair-law.md`
  (cite [201]). No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content
  extended existing pages, consistent with narrate-once-cross-link. No new
  below-threshold correspondents this batch: LEON (`melodymann`) and Robert
  (`boat142`) both already-standing identities from batches 208 and 206
  respectively, not newly flagged. Attribution-trap check: `dlb@.../
  buster38801` u=55757302 (David Beard) confirmed via From:/X-Yahoo-Profile
  on both his Storm-Tactics messages, no confusion with `whisper@.../
  meerkatoz` (David LeBlanc, no traffic this batch); `kd7ixk@.../
  betelgeuserdude` (Smith) confirmed on his one message, no Derek Clark
  (`dclark52001`) traffic this batch; three-distinct-Larrys watch not
  triggered (no Larry traffic this batch). Msgnum run 2105-2114 confirmed
  distinct and fully consecutive, continuing directly from batch 208's
  2104, no gaps. Byte sizes cross-checked via `wc -c` against dispatch
  instructions exactly (1432/7558/7604/9690/5083/1431/2592/2061/3682/4157
  bytes for page_2081 through page_2090 respectively), no stale-read issue.
  Citation-collision check clean across all six touched files
  (`references/mailing-list.md` 32 total, `construction/transom-deck-plate.md`
  36 total, `operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md` 31 total,
  `people/alastair-law.md` 201 total, `people/david-c-smith.md` 145 total,
  `people/david-beard.md` 216 total — no duplicates in any). Cross-link
  anchors verified against actual headings in
  `construction/transom-deck-plate.md` and
  `operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md`. Cursor set to 2090.

* **Update (batch page_2071–page_2080, msgnum 2095-2104)**: 11-13 May 2004.
  Centerpiece was the close of the "watertight boot on the Paradox
  tiller"/series-sea-drogue debate carried over from batch 206-207: **Robert**
  (`boat142`) cited a direct 2003 consultation with **Matt Layden** and USCG
  report CG-D-20-87 (May 1987) as his authorities, restating Matt Layden's
  reported tiller-boot fabric/vent/scupper recommendations and a proposed
  15 ft/sec water-jet stern test before rigging a series drogue. **Smith**
  rejected the USCG report's authority outright (comparing its still-debated
  status to the saccharin-cancer and Iraq-WMD reports, and its advocates to
  sailmakers/canvas workers/inexperienced sailors seeking a panacea),
  contrasting it with the plain, cheap truck tire many bluewater sailors
  actually trust, and declined to re-argue his prior objections. Robert
  conceded the coastal-cruiser framing but maintained "smart" seamanship
  means planning for the worst regardless. The thread then found its one
  concrete resolution: **Alastair** proposed a simpler fix than either a
  sealed boot or a redesign — a second rubber baffle disc fixed to the
  tiller ahead of the transom, deflecting water without blocking
  ventilation — which Smith endorsed as "making a lot of sense" and more
  practical than his own half-formed flange idea. Added as two new sections,
  "Robert's sources, and Smith's closing rebuttal" and "A simpler alternative
  for the tiller opening: a second rubber baffle disc," on
  [construction/transom-deck-plate.md](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md#roberts-sources-and-smiths-closing-rebuttal)
  (cites [27]-[31]); cross-link updates on `people/david-c-smith.md` (cites
  [143]-[144], extending the existing tiller-boot bullet) and
  `people/alastair-law.md` (cites [197]-[198], new bullet). Separately, **David
  Beard** posted two further "New Photos" announcements — a `paradox2`
  upload and a complete `paradoxbuilders2` sailing-photo set shot with a
  chase boat to capture "Whisper" on all points of sail — extending the
  batch-207 photo-storage narrative on
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#the-photo-storage-limit-and-the-founding-of-paradoxbuilders2)
  (cites [27]-[28]), cross-linked on `people/david-beard.md` (cites
  [213]-[214]). A new correspondent, **"LEON"** (`melodymann`), asked how
  Jim Michalak's "AF3" design compares in stability to the Paradox, wanting
  more interior space (room for a dog); Alastair judged the AF3 less stable
  but better suited to lying in the sun, and James (`jcbanik`, an
  already-standing below-threshold identity) linked an AF3 capsize-recovery
  page — recorded on
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#list-culture)
  (cites [29]-[31]), with a cross-link update on `people/alastair-law.md` (cite
  [198]). LEON is a new below-threshold correspondent (no boat, no build)
  appended to `backlog.md`; Robert and James both resurfaced as
  already-standing below-threshold identities (batches 206 and 205
  respectively), not newly flagged. Msgnum run 2095-2104 confirmed distinct
  and fully consecutive continuing from batch 207's 2094, no gaps. Cursor
  set to 2080.

* **Update (batch page_2061–page_2070, msgnum 2085-2094)**: 10 May 2004.
  Administrative centerpiece rather than a single technical thread: with
  founder **Glen Maxwell** away on his Atlantic crossing aboard "Hogfish
  Maximus," the group hit a genuine Yahoo Groups **photo-storage limit**
  head-on. **David Beard** uploaded new "Whisper" photos and then found
  himself locked out of the photo section by a recurring group error,
  independently confirmed the same day by **Bert Vercauteren**. **Smith**,
  filling the moderation gap, diagnosed the storage ceiling, deleted his
  own large-format "Swash" photos as a first step, and — resolving the
  archive's own previously-open question of exactly when and by whom
  `paradoxbuilders2` was founded — formally launched that second group on
  10 May 2004 for photo/file overflow, restricted to Paradox and other
  Matt Layden material, asking members to keep the main discussion on
  `paradoxbuilders` itself. When David Beard still could not get back into
  the photo section, Smith found he had to fully delete, rather than resize,
  three off-topic photos (a steel dinghy, a Bris rudder, a "water in my
  backyard" shot) to restore general access, and proposed reserving
  `paradoxbuilders2`'s files section for photo folders going forward. Added
  as two new sections, "The photo-storage limit, and the founding of
  paradoxbuilders2" and "Nels heads to Europe as a departing co-moderator,"
  on
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#the-photo-storage-limit-and-the-founding-of-paradoxbuilders2)
  (cites [19]-[26]), updating the existing `paradoxbuilders2` history-table
  row to record the resolved founding date/founder; cross-link updates on
  `people/david-c-smith.md` (cites [138]-[142]), `people/david-beard.md` (cites
  [211]-[212]), `people/bert-vercauteren.md` (cite [8]). Separately, **Nels**
  — revealed to have been appointed a `paradoxbuilders` co-moderator at some
  earlier, unrecorded point — announced he was leaving for a multi-month
  trip to Europe and would go quiet on the list; Smith thanked him. Cross-link
  update on `people/nels.md` (cite [89]), plus a lead-paragraph note
  recording his co-moderator role. Independently, the batch-206 "water tight
  boot on the Paradox tiller arm" thread reached its natural close: **Derek
  Clark** reacted to Smith's storm-tactics language with a lighter caution that
  Paradox is a coastal cruiser for which reliable weather forecasts should
  be easy to come by, recalling that even Alastair's own record-setting
  Poole-to-Cowes crossing involved an error of judgement on its fourth
  attempt; **Smith** agreed, adding that he rarely puts to sea when forecast
  conditions already exceed 20 knots/10 ft seas, and that incremental
  exposure to worsening conditions is itself how a sailor builds a genuine
  foul-weather threshold. Extended the existing "A proposed permanent
  tiller boot, and Smith's rebuttal" section on
  [construction/transom-deck-plate.md](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md#a-proposed-permanent-tiller-boot-and-smiths-rebuttal-with-sea-anchor-vs-series-drogue-doctrine)
  (cites [25]-[26]); cross-link update on `people/david-c-smith.md` (cites
  [138],[140], interleaved with the photo-storage cites above since both
  threads ran the same day). No new files, no renames, no prunings — all
  content extended existing pages, consistent with the
  narrate-once-cross-link principle. No new below-threshold correspondents;
  Bert Vercauteren (established person page) and Derek Clark (standing
  below-threshold identity) both resurfaced but are already-known, not
  newly flagged. Standing open-items check (Jeff UK/roverticket,
  botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold Larry/newspipetr3, Derek
  Clark/dclark52001, Fredrik, Jake, Lugger #2, kayaker37/Paul,
  paull01/Paul-Seattle, Paul V., Steve/Kansas City, Howard Welch, Jason
  Stancil, John Hall, Todd, Alf Sawatzky, Shane Hersey, Bengt Genell,
  Bill/wam_53, Craig/crag8, Ron/churo65, Phil/eliallegra(topsail@),
  Shorty/shortypen, LJGeib/second-Larry, Ken Preston/kenpreston46,
  James/jcbanik, Roger Nuffer/rogernuffer): only Derek Clark resurfaced, as
  expected (standing identity, already below threshold, remains so).
  Attribution-trap check: `dlb@...`/`buster38801` (David Beard) confirmed
  via `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:` on both his messages, no confusion with
  `whisper@...`/`meerkatoz` (David LeBlanc, no traffic this batch);
  `kd7ixk@...`/`betelgeuserdude` (Smith) confirmed on all five of his messages;
  `dclark@...`/`dclark52001` (Derek Clark) confirmed distinct from Smith on
  his one message — the Smith/Derek Clark trap is squarely exercised this
  batch since both posted in the same thread, and both were verified
  against `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:` headers directly; no confusion found.
  Three-distinct-Larrys watch not triggered (no Larry traffic this batch).
  Msgnum run 2085-2094 confirmed distinct and fully consecutive, continuing
  directly from batch 206's 2084, no gaps. Byte sizes cross-checked via
  `wc -c` against the dispatch instructions exactly (1098/3375/1125/1399/
  1357/1809/1975/1726/998/2282 bytes for page_2061 through page_2070
  respectively), no stale-read issue. Citation-collision check clean across
  all six touched files (`mailing-list.md` [19]-[26], `dc.md` [138]-[142],
  `david-beard.md` [211]-[212], `nels.md` [89], `bert-vercauteren.md` [8],
  `transom-deck-plate.md` [25]-[26]). Cursor set to 2070.

* **Update (batch page_2051–page_2060, msgnum 2075-2084)**: 9-10 May 2004.
  No single dominant thread — two independent continuations of the same
  overall "big guy in a small boat" exchange, plus a related storm-tactics
  side debate. (1) The **crew-size/fit** thread continued as a new
  correspondent, **Robert** (`boat142`), proposed a systematic scaling
  method for anyone the stock cabin doesn't fit: check comfort in a
  prototype across three postures (helm seat, cushion on the sole, mattress
  on the sole), then, if genuinely too small, build a sized cardboard
  mockup, measure the upscale factors, apply one uniform scaling factor to
  length/beam/height, replot the plywood cutting layout, and rebalance
  ballast to the new design waterline — restating the archive's existing
  16-ft-with-no-rig-penalty finding. **David Beard** clarified his
  "Whisper" removable-rear-window answer as fair-weather-only (hatch closes
  and the boat stays watertight in bad weather; "Little Cruiser" carries
  the same feature without issue), and **Alastair** added a firsthand
  15-hours-sitting/7-hours-standing passage comparison bearing on the
  raised-weight/stability question, plus a reservation that a removable
  rear window forces an all-or-nothing inside/outside choice. Added to the
  existing "very large sailor's fit" section on
  [operations/crew-size-and-fit.md](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md#a-very-large-sailors-fit-68315-lb-and-a-cardboard-mockup-suggestion)
  (cites [41]-[43]), with a new subsection "Series drogue vs. sea anchor
  geometry, and testing a rear window against green water" for Robert's
  hydro-pistoning water-jet test proposal and Alastair's tiller-hole/
  transom water-ingress concern (cites [44]-[45]); cross-link updates on
  `people/alastair-law.md` (cites [195]-[196]) and `people/david-beard.md`
  (cite [210]). (2) That transom water-ingress concern led directly into
  a **new "water tight boot on the Paradox tiller arm"** sub-thread:
  Robert proposed a permanent watertight boot over the tiller port plus a
  dorade/mushroom after-deck vent to replace its lost ventilation (or,
  preferably, a storm-only removable boot), and a series-sea-drogue
  ground-tackle scheme anchored to welded eyes on genoa tracks at each
  transom corner, citing an informal conversation with Matt Layden as not
  having raised any objection. **Smith** rejected the tiller-boot/vent
  proposal point-by-point (transom-slam hydraulic loading on a loose boot,
  an impractical drain arrangement, a storm-vulnerable dorade/mushroom
  vent, and the as-designed system's passive fair-weather/foul-weather
  ventilation and pressure-equalization advantage) and set out the
  fullest case on record for a **bow-led parachute sea anchor at ~45°**
  over a **stern-led series drogue** given Paradox's exposed transom
  fittings (rudder, tiller port, deck plate, after-house plexiglass) —
  added as a new section, "A proposed permanent tiller boot, and Smith's
  rebuttal (with sea-anchor vs. series-drogue doctrine)," on
  [construction/transom-deck-plate.md](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md#a-proposed-permanent-tiller-boot-and-smiths-rebuttal-with-sea-anchor-vs-series-drogue-doctrine)
  (cites [20]-[24]), with a cross-link update on `people/david-c-smith.md` (cites
  [136]-[137]). New below-threshold correspondent **Robert** (`boat142`/

* **Import — two *Small Craft Advisor* Matt Layden interviews** (out-of-band, not
  an email batch): ingested `source-articles/interviews/small-craft-advisor/`,
  Matt Layden's own words. **(A) "Enigma — The Matt Layden Interview"** (SCA May
  2007, interview & photo by Steve Isaac; partial 4-page scan) — Matt's fullest
  first-person account of the chine-runner / lifting-body system. Added to
  `chine-runners/how-they-work.md` (new section "The designer's own account
  (2007): a lifting-body system"; cite [15]; frontmatter description extended)
  and `chine-runners/origin-and-evolution.md` (new section "The conceptual
  origin: Bolger's chine battens and the NASA lifting body"; cite [8]); connected
  to the SCA article Matt announced in April 2006 on `matt-layden/enigma.md`
  (cite [11]); Yrvind's relayed praise + Matt's modest reply on
  `people/yrvind/paradox-and-the-chine-runner.md` (cite [4]). **(B) Doug Cameron,
  "Matt Layden — A Profile"** (SCA May/June 2009; capt-doug.blogspot.com) — new
  biographical facts added to `matt-layden/biography.md` (new "Early life and
  training" section — New England oysterman grandfather, "boat" as first word,
  16' Zip, cabinet-shop apprenticeship, design philosophy, mega-yacht career,
  doesn't drive / seldom wears shoes; enriched Balboa 20 entry; new "Cedar Key
  scene" section — battleship-grey 13½' "Rob Royoid" canoe, Jim Brown design
  talk, WaterTribe sandbag-and-food ballast strategy; cite [51]) and Enigma
  characterization on `matt-layden/enigma.md` ("next iteration of the Paradox
  line… car top"; 2006 solo-record claim; cite [12]). Single-writer import on
  Opus; all citation numbers verified unique per file; no email cursor change.

* **Update (batch page_2041–page_2050, msgnum 2065-2074)**: 5-9 May 2004.
  No single dominant thread — two independent exchanges. (1) The **"Chine
  Runners"** thread carried over from batch 204 reached its true close:
  **Alastair** replied that his own freehand-ground runners were "probably
  ok then," judging it "would be frightening" if runner shape could really
  be refined much further — folded into the existing
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md#chine-runners-rebuilt-after-the-shakedown-cruise)
  section (cite [79]), with a cross-link update on `people/alastair-law.md`
  (cite [192]). A separate correspondent, **Nels**, then reopened the topic
  from a design-theory angle — asking whether Matt Layden had ever published
  a formula for scaling chine-runner size to a larger hull (citing Phil
  Bolger's Martha Jane, ~75% larger, as his example) and whether Matt had
  ever tried bilgeboards or bilge keels instead of chine runners, leeboards,
  or centerboards; neither question drew a recorded answer. Added as a new
  section, [chine-runners/how-they-work.md](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md#no-published-scaling-formula-and-no-bilgeboardbilge-keel-experiment-on-record)
  (cite [16]), with a cross-link summary on `people/nels.md` (cite [88]).
  (2) A new **"big guy in a small boat"** thread — a new correspondent,
  **James** (6'8"/315 lb), asked whether he would be comfortable sailing a
  Paradox, drawing the fullest single round of fit/comfort replies on
  record: **Smith** (inflatable-mattress sleeping fix, cardboard-mockup
  caution), **Dave Bolduc** (comfort is relative; see the boat in person),
  **Alastair** (height distribution, not raw height, is what matters; later
  a convertible-car analogy answering a second newcomer's claustrophobia
  worry), a new correspondent **Roger Nuffer** (6'5"/180 lb, independently
  raising the same worry and floating a speculative 10-15% size increase),
  **Shorty** (a cardboard-cockpit-mockup suggestion, echoing a technique
  already used twice earlier in the archive), and **David Beard** ("Whisper"'s
  removable rear window doubling as a reading/chart-table perch). Added in
  full as a new section on
  [operations/crew-size-and-fit.md](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md#a-very-large-sailors-fit-68315-lb-and-a-cardboard-mockup-suggestion)
  (cites [33]-[40]), with cross-link summaries on `people/david-c-smith.md` (cite
  [135]), `people/bolduc/dave.md` (cite [79]), `people/alastair-law.md` (cites
  [193]-[194]), and `people/david-beard.md` (cite [209]). New below-threshold
  correspondents James (`jcbanik`) and Roger Nuffer (`rogernuffer`) added to
  `backlog.md`; Shorty (`shortypen`) reconfirmed as the same already-flagged
  PDRacer-community identity from batch 202, not newly promoted. No new
  files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing pages,
  consistent with the narrate-once-cross-link principle. Standing
  open-items check (Jeff UK/roverticket, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-
  threshold Larry/newspipetr3, Derek Clark/dclark52001, Fredrik, Jake,
  Lugger #2, kayaker37/Paul, paull01/Paul-Seattle, Paul V., Steve/Kansas
  City, Howard Welch, Jason Stancil, John Hall, Todd, Alf Sawatzky, Shane
  Hersey, Bengt Genell, Bill/wam_53, Craig/crag8, Ron/churo65,
  Phil/eliallegra(topsail@), LJGeib/second-Larry, Ken Preston/kenpreston46):
  only Shorty/shortypen resurfaced as expected; none of the others appeared.
  Attribution-trap check: `dlb@...`/`buster38801` (David Beard) confirmed on
  the Chine-Runners and big-guy-thread messages, no confusion with
  `whisper@...`/`meerkatoz` (David LeBlanc, no traffic this batch); `Smith`
  (`kd7ixk@...`/`betelgeuserdude`) confirmed, no Derek Clark
  (`dclark52001`) traffic this batch. Msgnum run 2065-2074 confirmed
  distinct and consecutive, continuing directly from batch 204's 2064, no
  gaps. Byte sizes cross-checked via `wc -c` against the dispatch
  instructions exactly (3730/5172/1226/3677/1680/2448/2511/4750/2565/3050
  bytes for page_2041 through page_2050 respectively), no stale-read issue.
  Citation-collision check clean across all eight touched files
  (`boats/whisper.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, `chine-runners/how-they-work.md`,
  `people/nels.md`, `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md`, `people/david-c-smith.md`,
  `people/bolduc/dave.md`, `people/david-beard.md`); a pre-existing
  non-sequential citation-number artifact was noted (but not introduced or
  touched) in `people/david-beard.md` ([143] dated earlier than [142],
  inherited from prior batches' interleaving). Cursor set to 2050.

* **Update (batch page_2031–page_2040, msgnum 2055-2064)**: 3-4 May 2004.
  No single dominant thread — four independent exchanges. (1) **Glen
  Maxwell**'s "Gone sailing" announcement — stepping away from the list to
  join **Chris Morejohn** aboard Morejohn's liveaboard sharpie **"Hogfish
  Maximus"** ("basically a 38 foot version of Paradox") for an Atlantic
  crossing to Portugal via Bermuda and the Azores, three months allowed for
  the trip — drew well-wishes from **Jack Gardiner** (his own Paradox about
  three weeks from launch), **Dave Bolduc** (recalling his own boyhood years
  living in Portugal), and **Smith**. Added in full to
  [people/glen-maxwell/matt-layden-eyewitness.md](/people/glen-maxwell/matt-layden-eyewitness.md#an-atlantic-crossing-aboard-hogfish-maximus)
  with a cross-link summary on
  [people/chris-morejohn.md](/people/chris-morejohn.md) and one-line citations
  on `people/jack-gardiner.md` and `people/david-c-smith.md`'s existing pages. (2) **Kjell
  Karlsson**'s "sheet entry" question — reviving a months-old open item about
  Matt Layden changing the mainsheet's transom entry — was answered by **Smith**
  (the group's photos show the original prototype's single-part sheet,
  changed on the plans to a two-part purchase; proposed bungee-tethered cleat
  chocks and a yuloh-mount dome fitting against fouling) and **Alastair**
  (confirmed the two-part purchase's mechanical-advantage rationale and the
  port-side, off-centre transom eye, matching the off-centre mast). Added as a
  new section, [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#sheet-entry-through-the-transom-prototype-versus-plans),
  with cross-link summaries on `people/david-c-smith.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, and
  `people/kjell-karlsson.md`. (3) **David Beard**'s "Chine Runners" note — a
  second report from the same Pirates Cove sea trials as batch 203's mainsheet
  fix — described rebuilding "Whisper"'s chine runners to within 1-2 mm after
  sailing a year with them badly out of shape; **Alastair** (who had shaped
  his own freehand with an angle grinder) asked what exactly had been wrong,
  and David's detailed reply relayed Matt Layden's own guidance that the
  runners' angle matters less than their area. Added in full to
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md#chine-runners-rebuilt-after-the-shakedown-cruise)
  with cross-link summaries on `people/david-beard.md` and
  `people/alastair-law.md`. No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content
  extended existing pages; eight files touched (`people/glen-maxwell/matt-layden-eyewitness.md`,
  `people/chris-morejohn.md`, `design/rig.md`, `people/david-c-smith.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/kjell-karlsson.md`, `boats/whisper.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`), plus a one-line citation added to
  `people/jack-gardiner.md`.

* **Update (batch page_2021–page_2030, msgnum 2044-2052,2054)**: 14 April – 3
  May 2004 (msgnum 2053 a genuine archive gap, no page). No single dominant
  thread — several independent exchanges. (1) **Smith** declined a Portland-area
  visit request from **Phil** (WA State, `topsail@...`), comparing himself to
  "an author who keeps the manuscript under wraps," then answered Phil's
  standing alder/hemlock boatbuilding-wood question in depth — Matt Layden's
  own hem-fir scantling rationale, Alaska yellow cedar and old-growth Douglas
  fir for joinery, and Edensaw Hardwoods as a supplier — closing out the open
  item flagged in batch 202; a second correspondent, **Larry** (`LJGeib`,
  distinct from both the below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3` and from
  **Larry Whited**), independently confirmed the same verdict. Extended
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) and resolved
  [construction/hull-planking.md](/construction/hull-planking.md#alder-and-hemlock-for-structural-and-joiner-work).
  (2) **Dave Bolduc** posted Matt Layden's own leach-tabling drawing (made
  years earlier for Little Cruiser's sail), extending the "paradox sails"
  sail-building thread — added to
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#building-a-lugsail-at-home-measuring-yard-deflection-before-cutting)
  with a cross-link summary on `people/bolduc/dave.md`; the accompanying
  file-upload notification folded into the same citation. (3) **Kjell
  Karlsson** ("Enlargening again") reported a berth-widening technique unique
  to his BRIS-material build — omitting sheet-foam insulation and the lower
  bin shelves widens the berth to the bulkheads' own outer dimension, with a
  removable aft shelf for single-vs-pair-sailing — added to
  [boats/bris-paradox.md](/boats/bris-paradox.md#widening-the-berth-by-omitting-the-lower-shelves)
  with cross-link summaries on `people/kjell-karlsson.md`; **Glen Maxwell**'s
  reply restated the standing "resize before upload, not after" Yahoo Groups
  constraint, noted as a second occurrence on
  [people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md](/people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md#diagnosing-yahoos-photo-resolution-limit).
  (4) **David Beard** announced "Whisper" at the Josephine, AL Wooden Boat
  Festival (1-2 May); **Larry Whited** replied hoping to see him at Cedar Key
  instead — a scheduling near-miss. The following week David reported back
  from sea trials at Pirates Cove, AL following Matt Layden's recommended
  mods, with a built (not just proposed) fix for the January "Main Sheet"
  mainsheet-fouling problem: a schedule-40 brass rod fairlead bar across the
  transom, doubling as a yuloh mount and hand-hold. Added in full to
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md#a-wooden-boat-festival-appearance-and-sea-trials-at-pirates-cove)
  and as a technique extension to
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#mainsheet-fouling-on-the-rudder-and-rear-deck-with-an-outboard-fitted),
  with a 1-2 sentence cross-link summary on `people/david-beard.md`. No new
  files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing pages; nine
  files touched (`people/david-c-smith.md`, `design/hull-scantlings.md`,
  `design/rig.md`, `people/bolduc/dave.md`, `boats/bris-paradox.md`,
  `people/kjell-karlsson.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md`, `boats/whisper.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`).

* **Update (batch page_2011–page_2020, msgnum 2034-2043)**: 11–14 April 2004.
  Continuations of two threads already underway plus new one-off newcomer
  traffic. (1) **"Ridiculous name..."**: **Alf Sawatzky** and **"Shorty"**
  (of the PDRacer plywood-dinghy community) both replied to Ken Preston's
  "Pair a Ducks" joke — Shorty topping it with a plan to join two PDRacers
  with a "grout unit"/"brick joiner" for a literal "pair of ducks," and
  mentioning an existing "PuddleDox" (PDRacer-as-Paradox) concept and a
  punning "West Wight Puddle." Added to
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#list-culture).
  (2) **"Got my plans today"** continued: Alf Sawatzky confirmed the El Toro
  is a pram-style dinghy and traded weather banter with Smith; **Alastair**
  cautioned Alf that sailing a Paradox feels much more like sailing a yacht
  than a dinghy, based on his own experience launching Little Jim with
  little prior sailing background — added as a new section on
  [operations/first-boat-suitability.md](/operations/first-boat-suitability.md#sailing-a-paradox-feels-more-like-sailing-a-yacht-than-a-dinghy),
  with a cross-link summary on `people/alastair-law.md`. A new prospective
  builder, **"Ron"** (Hillsboro, Oregon, plans already purchased), asked Smith
  whether he had a Paradox on the water near Portland or was still building;
  **Smith** answered with his own design rationale — the lower Columbia River's
  winter-quiet, shoal-draft cruising grounds, a planned Puget Sound/inland
  BC route, a preference for a multihull if he ever trailers to the Sea of
  Cortez, and his general philosophy of owning several boats each suited to
  a specific requirement rather than one boat for everything — added to
  `people/david-c-smith.md`. Ron and Alf remain below the person-page threshold (no
  build started yet). (3) Two posts from a new one-off correspondent,
  **"Phil"** (WA State): a request to visit Smith's Swash, and a genuine but
  unresolved technical question — whether alder or hemlock suit
  boatbuilding for structural/joiner work — noted on
  [construction/hull-planking.md](/construction/hull-planking.md#lumber-and-plywood-sourcing).
  No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing
  pages; five files touched (`people/david-c-smith.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `operations/first-boat-suitability.md`, `references/mailing-list.md`,
  `design/hull-scantlings.md`).

* **Update (batch page_2001–page_2010, msgnum 2024-2033)**: 31 March – 11 April
  2004. No single dominant thread — three independent exchanges plus one
  standalone post. (1) A **"Mast Pin?"** thread: **Craig** asked why
  construction photos don't show the mast-lowering pin described in Don
  Elliott's articles; **Alastair** explained Paradox's actual arrangement
  (mast foot on a hull-bottom block, held by the deck partners, lifted ~600mm
  clear to lower); **Dave Bolduc** settled the question — Paradox has no such
  pin, but **Little Cruiser** does, her foredeck-stepped mast folding
  backward onto the cabin top via a pin at its foot and two hinged struts.
  Added as a new section on
  [design/mast-partners.md](/components/rig/mast.md#no-mast-lowering-pin-on-paradox-unlike-little-cruiser),
  with cross-link summaries on `people/bolduc/dave.md` and
  `people/alastair-law.md`. (2) A **"Got my plans today"** thread: new prospective
  builder **Alf Sawatzky** (Winnipeg-area carpenter/cabinetmaker, no sailing
  background) introduced himself on receiving his plans; **Smith** gave the
  archive's fullest newcomer-encouragement advice — keep a build log, break
  the project into small tasks, search the archives before asking, and learn
  to sail on a cheap dinghy first (recommending Phil Bolger's "Elegant Punt,"
  the "Cape Cod Frosty," and the "El Toro") — followed by a personal
  weather-banter subthread (Winnipeg vs. NW Oregon climate, an El Toro
  class-association link). Added as a new section on
  [operations/first-boat-suitability.md](/operations/first-boat-suitability.md#advice-for-a-newcomer-at-the-very-start-build-logs-small-tasks-and-a-dinghy-first),
  with a cross-link summary on `people/david-c-smith.md`; Alf remains below the
  person-page threshold (no build started yet), consistent with the file's
  existing "hello members!" newcomer citations. (3) A standalone post: **Ken
  Preston**, a Potter owner still years from building, suggested a future
  Paradox be named with a "two ducks"/"Pair a Ducks" visual pun — added as a
  short "List culture" note on
  [references/mailing-list.md](/references/mailing-list.md#list-culture).
  No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing
  pages per the narrate-once-cross-link convention. David Beard vs. David
  LeBlanc and Smith vs. Derek Clark attribution traps checked throughout: no
  confusion (no David LeBlanc or Derek Clark traffic this batch; `kd7ixk@...`
  /`betelgeuserdude` = Smith and `mbolduc@...`/`boldav38` = Dave Bolduc both
  reconfirmed). Msgnum run 2024-2033 confirmed distinct and fully
  consecutive, continuing directly from batch 200's 2023; all ten byte sizes
  matched the dispatch instructions exactly, no stale-read issue; verified no
  new citation-number collisions in any of the six touched files
  (`design/mast-partners.md`, `people/bolduc/dave.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `operations/first-boat-suitability.md`, `people/david-c-smith.md`,
  `references/mailing-list.md`).

* **~2000 HOLISTIC TREE REVIEW** (post-batch-200, cursor 2000): 5-agent read-only
  audit + 4 single-writer fix passes. Removed 8 stray `</content>` EOF artifacts
  and 2 zero-byte orphan files (`design/steering.md`, `design/tack-strop.md`);
  fixed citation-number collisions in 4 files and corrected the `rig.md` /
  `kjell-karlsson.md` [85]-[87] page/msgnum error (verified against source .eml).
  Register scrub: ~100 "the archive's first…"→"…on record", removed
  "below-threshold correspondent" framing, reduced raw handles in prose, renamed
  16 conversational/question headings to declarative, collapsed `jeff-doryman.md`'s
  repeated disambiguation caveat. Reconciled **142 broken cross-file anchors and
  67 broken `log.md` file links to zero**. Trunk accuracy: propagated the
  batch-196 "first Australian Paradox" correction to the boats/people indexes and
  `andrew-graham.md`, fixed the `paramour-design.md` 14-ft-rule contradiction, and
  expanded the root-index Matt Layden blurb to all eight designs. Deferred
  follow-up cleanup recorded in `.okf-progress/backlog.md`. Next review ≈ post-3000.

* **Update (batch page_1991–page_2000, msgnum 2014-2023)**: 26-29 March 2004.
  Three independent short threads, none dominant. (1) **Glen Maxwell**
  closed out batch 199's "wonderful little boat" compliment with pointers
  to the group's files section (ground plate, vent trunk, water tanks) and
  confirmation that his new, taller sail plan was finished and
  photographed, folded into
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md).
  (2) A new **"Force 10 stove"** thread: **David Beard** asked about a
  Force 10 Seacook gimballed propane galley stove for "Whisper"; **Smith**
  had no direct experience but suggested comparing it against the
  **Forespar Mini-Galley 2000**, and David settled on the Forespar — added
  as a new "Choosing a galley cooking stove" section on
  [operations/cabin-heating.md](/operations/cabin-heating.md#choosing-a-galley-cooking-stove-the-force-10-seacook-and-the-forespar-alternative),
  distinct from that file's existing heating-technique content, with
  cross-links from `people/david-beard.md` and `people/david-c-smith.md`. (3) A new
  **"paradox sails"** thread: **Jack Gardiner**, sewing his own sail on a
  Sailrite LS-1, asked Glen Maxwell for yard/boom sleeve dimensions; Glen
  gave the archive's fullest sail-building technique — measure the yard's
  actual deflection under a 50 lb weight before cutting the sail, per his
  own costly first-sail lesson — extending the existing Dabbler-re-cut
  section of
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#building-a-lugsail-at-home-measuring-yard-deflection-before-cutting),
  with cross-links from `people/jack-gardiner.md` and
  `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md`. A fourth, separate **"Trolling"**
  thread — David Beard's saltwater-fishing question, Jack Gardiner's cheap
  broken-back "Atom Bomber" lure recommendation for Northeast Atlantic
  species, and Smith's detailed Pacific "meathook" trolling rig (tuna cord,
  surgical-tubing shock absorber, feathered jig) — became a new "Fishing
  while cruising" section on
  [operations/provisioning-and-water.md](/operations/provisioning-and-water.md#fishing-while-cruising),
  a natural fit alongside that file's existing note on caught fish
  supplementing the Bolducs' stores, with cross-links from
  `people/david-beard.md`, `people/jack-gardiner.md`, and `people/david-c-smith.md`.
  No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing
  pages per the narrate-once-cross-link convention. David Beard vs. David
  LeBlanc and Smith vs. Derek Clark attribution traps checked throughout: no
  confusion. Msgnum run 2014-2023 confirmed distinct and consecutive,
  continuing from batch 199's 2013.

* **Update (batch page_1981–page_1990, msgnum 2004-2013)**: 25-26 March 2004.
  The carried-over **"Ballast on X-Y tracks and Windsurfer sails"** thread
  reached its conclusion: **Alastair** advised building to the plans and
  sailing first, since without a standard rig or ballast arrangement to
  compare against there is no way to know whether a speculative change is
  really an improvement — interior woodwork besides the bulkheads and tank
  top comes out in about 10 minutes, so a ballast-track system could always
  be retrofitted later. A short sub-exchange floated a quick-release
  ballast-track locking mechanism (likened to a kayaker's Eskimo roll) to
  speed self-righting during a capsize; **Alastair** judged it "hardly
  necessary in a boat that is already self-righting from 160 degrees," and
  **Craig** (the same below-threshold correspondent from the previous batch)
  agreed to build a stock boat first, keeping his speculative ideas as later
  options — extended [weight, ballast, and displacement — sliding ballast on
  X-Y tracks
  (speculative)](/design/weight-and-ballast.md#sliding-ballast-on-x-y-tracks-speculative).
  Separately, the **"More pics"** thread's photo-hosting subplot produced a
  genuinely new historical fact: **Paul** (`kayaker37`) proposed founding a
  second Yahoo group, `paradoxbuilders2`, dedicated to file uploads, and
  **Glen Maxwell** replied that the group could wait, but disclosed for the
  first time that he was **negotiating with a friend to build a dedicated
  Paradox website** to store the group's material — the archive's first
  mention of such a site — extended [Glen C. Maxwell — list founder and
  moderator — a second group considered, and the first mention of a
  dedicated
  website](/people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md#a-second-group-considered-and-the-first-mention-of-a-dedicated-website),
  with a cross-link added from [mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md)
  tying it to the already-recorded bare `paradoxbuilders2` fact; **David
  Beard** replied with a simple thank-you. A minor addition: **Craig**
  drew a snowboard/ski-edge wear analogy for chine-runner design, speculating
  that an efficient runner profile might taper toward the fore-and-aft tips
  — extended [how chine runners work — a snowboard/ski-edge wear analogy
  (speculative)](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md#a-snowboardski-edge-wear-analogy-speculative),
  unanswered in the archive. Craig closed the batch admiring the finished
  boats on the list (naming "ZOE"), asking unanswered questions about Matt's
  new sail, the sink tap, and copper-to-hull attachment. Cross-linked from
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) and
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md). No new files; no person
  page for Craig (below threshold — no boat). Standing open items (Jeff
  UK/roverticket, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, Larry/newspipetr3, Derek Clark,
  Fredrik, Jake, Lugger #2, kayaker37/Paul, paull01/Paul-Seattle,
  Steve/Kansas City, Howard Welch, Jason Stancil, John Hall, Todd, Alf
  Sawatzky, Shane Hersey, Bengt Genell, Bill/wam_53) checked: only
  `kayaker37`/Paul and `crag8`/Craig resurfaced (both already-established
  below-threshold identities, confirmed via `X-Yahoo-Profile` headers); none
  of the others reappeared. David Beard vs. David LeBlanc and Smith vs. Derek
  Clark attribution traps checked against `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:` headers
  throughout: no confusion (no David LeBlanc, Smith, or Derek Clark traffic
  this batch; `dlb@...`/`buster38801` u=55757302 = David Beard reconfirmed).
  Msgnum run 2004-2013 confirmed distinct and fully consecutive, continuing
  directly from batch 198's 2003.

* **Update (batch page_1971–page_1980, msgnum 1994-2003)**: 24-25 March 2004.
  A continuous **"More pics"** thread (Kjell Karlsson's ParaPlastic photos,
  carried from the previous batch) ran through most of the batch: **Glen
  Maxwell** thanked Kjell; **Alastair** asked how the chine runners would be
  attached strongly enough for hard grounding, answered by **Kjell**, who
  described switching a planned stainless-steel bottom sheet to **bronze**
  instead so it could double as a lightning-protection ground plate as well
  as ballast and the runner's base — extended [chine runner construction
  technique — bronze versus steel, if a builder insists on a
  shoe](/chine-runners/construction.md#bronze-versus-steel-if-a-builder-insists-on-a-shoe),
  closing with Alastair's toe-safety aside about launching from a beach. The
  same thread surfaced a **photo-hosting problem**: **Paul** (`kayaker37`)
  reported large inline photos displaying at a fraction of their full
  resolution; **Glen Maxwell** traced it to a Yahoo Groups 400-pixel inline
  display cap, resized and reposted the photos to the files section, and set
  a going-forward files-section size convention — extended [Glen C. Maxwell
  — list founder and moderator — diagnosing Yahoo's photo-resolution
  limit](/people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md#diagnosing-yahoos-photo-resolution-limit);
  **Kjell** separately explained he'd deleted his own full-size photos after
  running out of group upload space, leaving open whether the 30 MB storage
  limit is per-group or per-member. A separate, unrelated **"Ballast on X-Y
  tracks and Windsurfer sails"** thread: a new, below-threshold correspondent,
  **Craig**, speculated (without yet owning a Paradox) about wheeled ballast
  running on intersecting box-channel tracks for underway rebalancing, and
  about adapting a windsurfer's canted "Universal" mast joint to a
  Paradox-type rig — extended [weight, ballast, and displacement — sliding
  ballast on X-Y tracks
  (speculative)](/design/weight-and-ballast.md#sliding-ballast-on-x-y-tracks-speculative),
  unanswered in the archive. Cross-linked from
  [people/kjell-karlsson.md](/people/kjell-karlsson.md) and
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md). No new files; no person page for
  Craig (below threshold — no boat, unanswered idea). Standing open items
  (Jeff UK/roverticket, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, Larry/newspipetr3, Derek
  Clark, Fredrik, Jake, Lugger #2, kayaker37/Paul, paull01/Paul-Seattle,
  Steve/Kansas City, Howard Welch, Jason Stancil, John Hall, Todd, Alf
  Sawatzky, Shane Hersey, Bengt Genell, Bill/wam_53) checked: only
  `kayaker37`/Paul resurfaced (his own standing, already-established
  below-threshold identity, distinct from `paull01`/Paul-Seattle and from
  Paul V.), folded inline per convention; none of the others reappeared.
  David Beard vs. David LeBlanc and Smith vs. Derek Clark attribution traps
  checked against `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:` headers throughout: no
  confusion (no David LeBlanc, Smith, or Derek Clark traffic this batch).
  Msgnum run 1994-2003 confirmed distinct and fully consecutive, continuing
  directly from batch 197's 1993.

* **Update (batch page_1961–page_1970, msgnum 1984-1993)**: 13-24 March 2004.
  Five short, independent exchanges, no single centerpiece thread. The
  **"Hatch"** thread carried from the previous batch got a further reply:
  **Smith** gave a sandpaper-refitting technique for a too-tight or wracking
  hatch, seconding Glen Maxwell's carnauba-wax fix — extended
  [hatch hardware and security](/construction/hatch-hardware.md). A new
  **"Beams and Bulkheads"** thread: **Steve (KC)** asked whether a finished
  deck beam could serve as its own bulkhead-curvature template rather than
  working from the plans, answered yes by **Glen Maxwell** and **Alastair**
  — extended [deck and cabin beam lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md).
  A new **"Lines Comparison"** thread: **Paul** (Seattle, WA) compared
  Paradox, Little Cruiser, and Bolger's Jessie Cooper lines, asking whether
  chine runners would scale to a 25 ft hull; **Nels** answered with a
  detailed Bolger design-lineage survey (Chuck Merrell's Jessie Cooper
  redesign and its ballast shortfall, then Bolger's own move to the Martha
  Jane, AS19, AS29, and AS38) — extended
  [stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md). A new
  **"Electric outboard/battery ballast"** thread: **Bill** proposed gel-cell
  batteries doing double duty as ballast and a power source for an electric
  outboard; **Alastair** agreed on the electric-outboard logic but declined
  an autohelm, and **David Beard** gave a firsthand field data point (a 35
  lb thrust electric motor on "Whisper") — extended
  [auxiliary outboard power](/operations/auxiliary-outboard-power.md). And
  **Kjell Karlsson** posted a further "More pics" ParaPlastic photo update,
  identifying fellow Swedish sailor Lasse (of the BRIS-hulled,
  Atlantic-crossing *Libra*) — extended
  [the "BRIS Paradox"](/boats/bris-paradox.md). Cross-linked from
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md), [people/steve-kc.md](/people/steve-kc.md),
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md),
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md), and
  [people/kjell-karlsson.md](/people/kjell-karlsson.md) per the
  narrate-once-cross-link convention; no new files. Two new below-threshold
  correspondents (Paul of Seattle, and Bill); all standing identity Open
  items checked, none resurfaced. David Beard vs. David LeBlanc and Smith vs.
  Derek Clark attribution traps checked against `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile`
  headers throughout, no confusion.
* **Update (batch page_1951–page_1960, msgnum 1974-1983)**: 1-13 March 2004.
  Six short, independent exchanges, no single centerpiece thread. **Dave
  Bolduc** closed out the "boat plans" FAQ thread carried from the previous
  batch, confirming he had sent Shane Hersey the plans info twice and
  restating his e-mail address with its deliberate anti-spam-bot space.
  A new **"Sheet layout"** thread: **Bengt Genell** (Sweden, a new
  below-threshold plans customer) could not identify the C-formed pieces
  drawn on the 9mm sheet with bulkhead 1; **Alastair** explained they are
  cabin-end pieces laminated to the deck to carry the window bottoms, and
  described a real ply-shortfall workaround (losing an interlocking point
  to a bin-side conflict, then gluing in an offcut) and a reordered layout
  for the equivalent 12mm-sheet pieces. **Todd** resurfaced asking the
  **strongest winds and seas** a Paradox has sailed in; **Glen Maxwell**
  answered that Matt Layden has sailed in winds over 30 kt, restated the
  already-documented Exuma Sound 20-30 kt photo, and pointed to Alastair's
  own rougher-conditions cruising as the fuller record. **Andrew Graham**
  posted a **correction**: an earlier, 2000-built secondhand Paradox (owner
  "Chris," Brisbane, for sale at $3,000 AUD, sailed only once, unballasted
  and sailed with sandbags) predates his own "Zero," softening the
  archive's earlier "first Australian Paradox to sail" claim. A new
  **"Hatch"** thread, distinct from the file's existing locking-hasp
  hazard: **David Beard**'s wood-on-wood sliding-friction complaint, **Glen
  Maxwell**'s carnauba-wax fix, and **Alastair**'s corroborating but only
  partial success (still opens in jerks; suspects aluminium extrusions are
  the real fix). Finally, **Jason Stancil** (a non-Paradox Bolger-boat
  builder, resurfacing below-threshold with an unrelated new question) asked
  about masthead-lightning grounding, having seen a photo of a copper sheet
  under a Paradox's glass — a second, external data point for the
  de-ionization system already documented. Extended
  [building plans](/references/building-plans.md#why-paradox-resists-easy-enlargement)
  (citation [47]), [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md#cabin-end-c-formed-pieces-cut-from-the-same-9mm-sheet-as-bulkhead-1)
  (citations [31]-[32]), [heavy-weather tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md#the-strongest-winds-a-paradox-is-known-to-have-sailed-in)
  (citations [26]-[27]), [Aussie Paradox](/boats/aussie-paradox.md#a-correction-an-earlier-secondhand-australian-paradox)
  (citation [26]), [hatch hardware and security](/construction/hatch-hardware.md#wood-on-wood-friction-and-a-wax-fix)
  (citations [3]-[5]), and [lightning protection](/design/lightning-protection.md#a-grounding-plate-seen-under-the-glass)
  (citation [3]). No new files, no renames, no prunings — all content
  extended existing pages, consistent with narrate-once-cross-link.
* **Update (batch page_1941–page_1950, msgnum 1964-1973)**: 22-26 February
  2004. **Centerpiece:** a single continuous "boat plans" thread — the
  archive's FAQ recurring a further two times. **Alf Sawatzky**
  (Southern Manitoba, carried over below-threshold from batch 194) asked
  where to buy plans; **Jack Gardiner** gave Dave Bolduc's e-mail address
  directly, and **"Jeff UK"** pointed to microcruising.com and its
  sub-$50 price. Days later, **Shane Hersey** (Canberra, Australia, a new
  below-threshold correspondent) reported two unanswered e-mails to Dave
  Bolduc and asked for another way to make contact — drawing three parallel
  answers: **David Beard** offered to relay the request himself;
  **"Jeff UK"** suggested posting to the `microcruising` Yahoo Group
  directly, admitting he too could not get the Bolducs' e-mail to work; and
  **William Serjeant** pointed to Dave's contact address as republished on
  his own paradox-uk.co.uk info page. A second, smaller thread ran inside
  the same "boat plans" subject line: newcomer **Todd**, having just
  received his plans, asked whether the chine-log/sheer-clamp bevels are cut
  before or after installing, answered by **Jack Gardiner** (bevel first,
  then install — he had installed his own after the hull sides were joined)
  and **Alastair** (cut to the sheet-5 profiles, spring the sides on, trim
  bottom/deck to fit; bulkhead bevels off sheet 2 still need adjustment at
  trial assembly, so cut cleats oversize; work entirely in millimetres with
  a metric tape). Extended [building plans](/references/building-plans.md#why-paradox-resists-easy-enlargement)
  (citations [40]-[46]), [hull assembly sequence](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md#bevel-order-for-the-chine-log-and-sheer-clamp-cut-before-or-after-installing)
  (citations [42]-[45]), [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md) (citations
  [81]-[83]), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation [183]), [David
  Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation [199]), [Jeff (UK,
  "doryman_uk")](/people/jeff-doryman.md) (citations [47]-[48], with the
  disambiguation note reaffirmed — still no shared boat name, location, or
  self-identification links "Jeff UK"/`roverticket` to the established
  `doryman_uk`), and [Small Sailboats / Paradox-UK
  websites](/references/paradox-uk-website.md) (citation [24]). No new
  files, no renames, no prunings — all content extended existing pages,
  consistent with narrate-once-cross-link.
* **Update (batch page_1931–page_1940, msgnum 1954-1963)**: 16-23 February
  2004. **Centerpiece:** the "Opinions sought from the offshore experienced"
  thread — **servo_bot** catalogued a set of conventional blue-water safety
  features (sprayhood, leecloth/seatbelt restraints, a small windvane, a
  spare emergency rudder, a foam-filled mast, toerails/handholds, and
  smaller or reinforced cabin windows) and asked whether any would
  meaningfully improve Paradox's offshore readiness; **Alastair** and
  **Smith** answered item by item (a sprayhood adds nothing sailed from the
  enclosed pilothouse; the yuloh already serves as an emergency rudder; the
  mast is already wood/air-filled; toerails and hatch slides already serve
  as handholds; window strength should be judged by what's actually
  "enough," not assumed inadequate — Alastair's own reef-knot-in-a-lexan-
  offcut test backing the point), while **William Serjeant** placed Paradox
  as a coastal/estuary cruiser capable of an ocean crossing "in the right
  hands" but not designed for it. Smith's own windvane follow-up (forward-of-
  mast mounting sits in dirty air abaft the beam) and Alastair's closing
  citation of Matt Layden's Watertribe-forum self-steering quote rounded out
  the thread. A short bridging thread carried over from batch 193's
  unanswered "Has anyone rolled a parodox ?" question: **William Longyard**
  predicted genuine long-distance Paradox voyages within two or three years,
  judging the design "as capable, or more so, than many small boats that
  have crossed oceans." Both threads folded into new sections on
  [blue-water suitability](/design/blue-water-suitability.md#a-catalogue-of-conventional-offshore-features-weighed-and-mostly-declined)
  (citations [27]-[36]), cross-linked from
  [heavy-weather tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md#securing-the-crew-during-a-knockdown)
  and [self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md#weighing-a-tillerpilot-against-a-genuine-wind-vane).
  Elsewhere in the batch, **Jack Gardiner** reported rolling his hull twice
  during construction (bottom application, then flipping back) and gave his
  own window-construction reasoning (laminated epoxy-impregnated plywood,
  rubber-channel-framed windows for easy replacement) — both extending
  [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md#construction-begins); William
  Longyard's rolling/voyage prediction also got a one-line cross-linked
  entry on [William H. Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md). A final
  below-threshold newcomer, **Alf Sawatzky** (Southern Manitoba, Canada),
  introduced himself after discovering microcruising.com and asked where to
  buy plans — no boat or further posts recorded. No new files, no renames,
  no prunings.
* **Update (batch page_1921–page_1930, msgnum 1944-1953)**: 13-16 February
  2004. **Centerpiece:** the reception and correction of **Smith**'s Build
  Manual "Laminating Beams" installment — **Alastair** worried its accuracy
  emphasis might needlessly intimidate novice builders, having gotten exactly
  the boat he wanted from his own deliberately workboat-finish build; Smith
  defended precision as a goal while conceding the design's self-fairing
  plywood tolerates real variance, and invited anyone to draft or amend a
  section; **Steve (Kansas City)**, prebending his own 3mm Douglas Fir beam
  strips, then caught a genuine error in the installment — Smith had reversed
  the springback claim — which Smith corrected, adding his own lamination
  figures (5mm Honduras mahogany, 30-40 clamps per beam, recommending 7-8mm
  in hindsight) and a full-length clamp-pad tip; **Alastair** capped the
  thread with his own figures (7mm strips, steamed first, only 3 clamps) —
  new sections "Precision versus a workboat finish" and "Lamination
  thickness and springback in practice" on [deck and cabin beam
  lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md#the-build-manuals-deck-beam-template-and-laminating-jigs-installment),
  extending [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md), and [Steve
  (Kansas City)](/people/steve-kc.md). A separate "Everglades Challenge"
  thread: Alastair noted Matt Layden was entering the March 2004 Everglades
  Challenge in a sailing kayak rather than Paradox; David Beard predicted
  Matt would be hard to beat "no matter what boat he chooses"; **Paul**
  (`kayaker37`, below-threshold) located Matt's own watertribe.org article on
  a West Marine Stearns inflatable-kayak second-place finish, which Alastair
  clarified was the (separate, already-recorded) Michigan Challenge, not the
  Everglades Challenge, drawing the moral that boat-design debates mean
  little without accounting for crew skill — extends [Matt Layden's
  biography — other craft and
  events](/matt-layden/biography.md#other-craft-and-events), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md),
  and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md). Also a new, unanswered
  correspondent question: **Todd** (`ntsrfer`, the below-threshold newcomer
  from the previous batch) asked whether a Paradox could survive multiple
  rolls or an end-over-end pitchpole in 15-20 ft breaking seas, and whether
  anyone had sailed one to Hawaii or Long Beach-to-Baja-and-back — new
  open-question note on [heavy-weather
  tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md#securing-the-crew-during-a-knockdown).
  No new files; all content extended existing pages. Standing-open-items
  check: none of Jeff UK/roverticket, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold
  Larry/newspipetr3, Derek Clark/dclark52001, Fredrik, Jake, "Lugger #2",
  Steve/Kansas City, Howard Welch, or Jason Stancil resurfaced as new
  identities beyond Steve KC's expected substantive appearance;
  kayaker37/Paul resurfaced once (below-threshold, folded inline per
  convention, no person page); Todd (below threshold, newly recurring)
  likewise folded inline. David Beard vs. David LeBlanc and Smith vs. Derek
  Clark attribution traps checked against `From:`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:` headers
  throughout: no confusion (no David LeBlanc or Derek Clark traffic this
  batch; David Beard's `dlb`/`buster38801` identity, u=55757302, and Smith's
  `kd7ixk`/`betelgeuserdude` identity, u=144918809, both confirmed via
  headers). Msgnum run 1944-1953 confirmed distinct and fully consecutive,
  continuing directly from batch 192's 1943; all ten byte sizes matched the
  dispatch instructions exactly, no stale-read issue; citation-collision
  check clean across all seven touched files (`construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-c-smith.md`, `people/steve-kc.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`, `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md`).

* **Update (batch page_1911–page_1920, msgnum 1934-1943)**: 6-13 February
  2004. (1) The tail of the "yuloh and pivot questions" thread carried from
  the previous batch — **Glen Maxwell**'s and **Alastair**'s answers to
  David Beard's wood-type question (clear fir or plain pine for Glen; Douglas
  Fir, matching the rest of his boat, for Alastair) — new section on [yuloh
  and stowed-rig retention](/components/yuloh.md#wood-for-the-yuloh),
  extending [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) and [Glen Maxwell (build and
  Zoë)](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md). (2) A "Newbie" thread — new
  correspondent **Todd** asked for the Paradox's bench-to-hatch and
  floor-to-hatch cabin dimensions; **David Beard** welcomed him and offered
  to measure "Whisper"; **Glen Maxwell** answered first with exact figures
  from "Zoë" (67 cm/26.8 in bench-to-hatch, 91 cm/36.4 in sole-to-hatch);
  Todd thanked the list, reported he had requested plans, and asked whether
  "Dan" — a Southern California prospective builder seen earlier in the
  archive — ever started his build, left unanswered — new section on [crew
  size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md#glen-maxwells-measured-cabin-dimensions),
  extending [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md), [Glen Maxwell (build and
  Zoë)](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md), and cross-linked from
  [Southern California sailing and the Catalina Island crossing
  question](/operations/southern-california-and-catalina-crossing.md#a-builders-own-start-plans-a-scale-model-and-the-mast-question).
  (3) **Centerpiece:** **Smith**'s Build Manual "Deck Beam Template" and
  "Laminating Jigs" installment — the promised next section after
  "Preparations" — a full method for building the deck, cabin, and hatch
  beam templates off sheets 7 and 10, building the laminating jig to the
  beam's bottom edge, choosing a lamination thickness by trial, and the full
  glue-up/waxing/clamping/24-hour-cure procedure, closed with a "note on
  accuracy" added at **Alastair**'s suggestion that a couple of millimetres
  either way won't affect the finished boat; **Glen Maxwell** called it
  "nicely explained" — new section on [deck and cabin beam
  lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md#the-build-manuals-deck-beam-template-and-laminating-jigs-installment),
  extending [build effort and workspace](/construction/build-effort.md#the-build-manuals-preparations-installment-build-site-and-a-full-tool-inventory)
  (cross-link only), [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), and
  [Glen Maxwell (build and Zoë)](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md). No
  new files; all content extended existing pages. Standing-open-items check:
  none of Jeff UK/roverticket, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold
  Larry/newspipetr3, Derek Clark/dclark52001, Fredrik, Jake, "Lugger #2",
  kayaker37/Paul, Steve/Kansas City, Howard Welch, Jason Stancil, or John Hall
  resurfaced; one new below-threshold newcomer, Todd (`ntsrfer`/`ktsrfer`),
  kept inline per the people threshold. No Smith-vs-Derek-Clark confusion (no
  Derek Clark traffic this batch, though Smith himself is the centerpiece
  contributor — his `kd7ixk`/`betelgeuserdude` identity confirmed via
  headers); no David LeBlanc traffic, so the David Beard-vs-David LeBlanc
  trap was not implicated (David Beard's own `dlb`/`buster38801` identity,
  u=55757302, confirmed via headers). msgnum run 1934-1943 confirmed
  distinct and consecutive, continuing directly from batch 191's 1933.

* **Update (batch page_1901–page_1910, msgnum 1924-1933)**: 1-6 February
  2004. (1) The true close of the "Looking for some info" construction
  thread carried over from the previous batch — **Glen Maxwell**'s reply
  covering his own bin-side/water-tank-top fixes (angle-aluminum bracing
  where a bin side pulled away from the tank top; a dowel-handle tool for
  tightening leak-prone tank lids; plastic trays for canned goods; a towel
  on the afterdeck for wet feet) and his own ballast layout on "Zoë" (22 kg
  forward of bulkhead #2 to starboard, offsetting the battery's weight to
  port, with none aft of bulkhead #4); **Jack Gardiner**'s closing
  thanks, settling on West System epoxy for his own tanks — new section on
  [water tank construction and
  lining](/construction/water-tank-lining.md#a-tank-lid-tightening-tool-and-where-water-problems-actually-come-from),
  extending [interior bin panels and hinged
  floor](/components/interior.md#forward-and-aft-bin-sides-how-far-they-extend-to-the-hull),
  [weight, ballast, and
  displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md#a-laminated-sheet-lead-bottom-as-an-alternative-to-loose-pigs-speculative),
  and [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md#construction-begins).
  (2) A further "Little Jim" photo-gallery exchange — William Serjeant's
  in-person visit to see Alastair's boat, and a further photo set posted to
  his own site — extending [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md#bill-serjeants-visit-february-2004).
  (3) **Centerpiece:** a new "yuloh and pivot questions" thread — **Steve
  (Kansas City)** asked how to read the plans' 30 mm longitudinal-concavity
  figure and how to build the stern-mounted pivot bolt and its socket;
  **Alastair** described laminating his own yuloh from two planks over a
  30 mm block and a stainless-stud/spherical-bearing-ball pivot seated in a
  recessed tufnol socket, plus Dave Bolduc's bungee-cord fix for the yuloh
  jumping off the ball; **Glen Maxwell** described his own brazed-bronze
  pivot bolt built to the plans, with only his own learning curve (covered
  separately in his sea-trials article) as a real difficulty; **David
  Beard** closed the thread asking what wood Alastair used, left unanswered
  in this batch — new section on [yuloh and stowed-rig
  retention](/components/yuloh.md#building-the-yuloh-itself-blade-curvature-and-the-pivot-boltsocket),
  extending [Steve (Kansas City)](/people/steve-kc.md),
  [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), [Glen Maxwell (build and
  Zoë)](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md), and
  [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md). No new files; all content extended
  existing pages. Standing-open-items check: none of Jeff UK/roverticket,
  botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold Larry/newspipetr3, Derek
  Clark/dclark52001, Fredrik, Jake, "Lugger #2", kayaker37/Paul, Steve/Kansas
  City (here promoted from below-threshold watch-item to a substantive
  yuloh-construction contributor, still filed at his existing person page),
  Howard Welch, Jason Stancil, or John Hall resurfaced beyond Steve KC's own
  continuing presence. No Smith or Derek Clark traffic this batch, so the Smith-
  vs-Derek-Clark trap was not implicated; no David LeBlanc traffic, so the
  David Beard-vs-David LeBlanc trap was not implicated (David Beard's own
  `dlb`/`buster38801` identity confirmed via headers as usual). msgnum run
  1924-1933 confirmed distinct and consecutive, continuing directly from
  batch 190's 1923.

* **Update (batch page_1891–page_1900, msgnum 1914-1923)**: 27 January - 1
  February 2004. (1) The true final close of the multi-batch "Sven's book,
  concluded" translation-project thread — **William Longyard**'s offer to
  approach his own publisher with the already-translated sample chapter,
  reasoning that a volunteer-translated manuscript would make far better
  economic sense to present than an unproven proposal, and **Kjell
  Karlsson**'s agreement that this was the sensible way forward — extending
  [the BRIS
  Paradox](/boats/bris-paradox.md#a-proposed-group-project-to-translate-sven-yrvinds-full-book-into-english),
  [William Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md), and
  [Kjell Karlsson](/people/kjell-karlsson.md). (2) **Centerpiece:** a new
  "Sheet Lead Ballast" thread — a new below-threshold Tasmanian builder,
  **John Hall**, floated laminating sheet lead into the bottom panel rather
  than cleating loose pigs; Kjell Karlsson countered with an outer
  bronze/stainless sheet instead; **Alastair** raised the practical
  objection that the plans' ballast range exists for trip-to-trip
  adjustment, not build-weight margin, and that his own boat took four
  outings to trim correctly — then gave his own real, weighed ballast
  figures (120 kg lead, 640 kg boat overall) answering Kjell's
  weekend-versus-long-tour follow-up — new section on [weight, ballast, and
  displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md#a-laminated-sheet-lead-bottom-as-an-alternative-to-loose-pigs-speculative),
  extending [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) and
  [Kjell Karlsson](/people/kjell-karlsson.md). (3) Jack Gardiner's "Looking
  for some info" construction-detail thread — vent trunk top dimensions and
  a before-decking mast-fitting tip, food-grade epoxy advice for water tanks
  intended for drinking, and forward-vs-aft bin-side extents — new section
  on [mast partners and vent
  trunk](/components/rig/mast.md#vent-trunk-top-dimensions-and-why-exact-measurements-dont-matter-much),
  extending [water tank construction and
  lining](/construction/water-tank-lining.md), [interior bin panels and
  hinged floor](/components/interior.md#forward-and-aft-bin-sides-how-far-they-extend-to-the-hull),
  and [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md). No new files; all content
  extended existing pages.

* **Update (batch page_1881–page_1890, msgnum 1904-1913)**: 25-27 January
  2004. (1) The "Double Rudders" self-steering thread's continuation and true
  close — William Serjeant's Bowden-cable thanks and tillerpilot-consensus
  close; a pointed dissent from **Jeffery Please ("Jeff UK")**, revealed as a
  director of **VectaVane Ltd.**, a UK wind-vane self-steering manufacturer,
  arguing (despite his own commercial stake) that fitting any wind vane to
  Paradox is "a waste of time"; Serjeant's point-by-point rebuttal, citing
  Sven Yrvind's own rudder-integrated self-steering system as a possible
  model; Please's paddle-less, forward-of-mast wind-vane idea from the James
  Wharram catamaran-building website; and Serjeant's own closing case for why
  self-steering matters — freeing time for watchkeeping, navigation, and
  cooking, and relieving the tedium of long tiller-holding stretches — new
  sections on
  [self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md#a-vectavane-directors-skeptical-outside-view),
  extending [Jeff (UK, "doryman_uk")](/people/jeff-doryman.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md),
  and [Small Sailboats / Paradox-UK websites](/references/paradox-uk-website.md).
  (2) A **heaving-to** sub-thread — Alastair's question on how to heave to in
  a Paradox; Glen Maxwell's sea-anchor technique (a starboard-set rode with a
  snatch-blocked aft-led line, settling the bow a few degrees off the wind on
  the starboard tack for right-of-way, plus a Capt. Voss book recommendation);
  and William Serjeant's own untested lugsail-heave-to method, with striking
  all sail and raising the rudder as the simplest certain fallback — new
  section on [heavy-weather
  tactics](/operations/heavy-weather-tactics.md#a-sea-anchor-technique-for-heaving-to-lying-a-few-degrees-off-the-wind),
  cross-linked from self-steering. (3) **Centerpiece:** the true final close
  of the multi-batch "Sven's book, concluded" translation-project thread —
  Kjell Karlsson's own last word, relaying that Sven Yrvind does not approve
  an e-book but is greatly interested in finding an English-speaking
  publisher, for whom the already-translated "Chapter9" could serve as a
  sample, and expects a professional translator (not Kjell) to handle the
  rest — extending [the BRIS
  Paradox](/boats/bris-paradox.md#a-proposed-group-project-to-translate-sven-yrvinds-full-book-into-english)
  and [Kjell Karlsson](/people/kjell-karlsson.md). No new files; all content
  extended existing pages.

* **Update (batch page_1871–page_1880, msgnum 1894-1903)**: 24-25 January
  2004. (1) The "Double Rudders" thread's practical-self-steering coda —
  Smith's tillerpilot/wind-vane assessment (longitudinal tillerpilot mount,
  mainsheet-fouling and beach-ability obstacles, a sketched
  vane-plus-trim-tab linkage) and Alastair's own view (inside-hull tiller
  pilot, a forward-of-mast vane idea, Matt Layden's Watertribe
  sleep-while-sailing anecdote as evidence elaborate self-steering is
  unnecessary), closed the next day by William Serjeant thanking Alastair
  and hoping to visit and see "Little Jim" — new section on
  [self-steering](/operations/self-steering.md#weighing-a-tillerpilot-against-a-genuine-wind-vane),
  extending [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), and
  [Small Sailboats / Paradox-UK websites](/references/paradox-uk-website.md).
  (2) A final coda to the "Sea Trial article" saga — below-threshold Derek
  Clark's belated thank-you for the pictures — extending
  [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md#press-coverage-two-2003-magazine-articles). (3) Jack
  Gardiner resolves his own cabin-height thinking-aloud with a practical
  answer: a secure, weathertight **exterior hatch** over the forward
  stateroom, for outside access to seldom-needed stowage rather than
  interior room — extending
  [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md#construction-begins) and
  [interior bin panels and hinged floor](/components/interior.md#forward-compartment-access).
  (4) **Centerpiece:** "Sven's book, concluded" — Sven Yrvind declines to
  put his complete book online (one already-translated Paradox chapter is
  fine), while at work on his next boat and next book (to be written
  directly in English) bound for the Azores; he separately judges the
  translated Paradox chapter under-credited the **big rudder's role in
  lateral resistance** — cross-linked into [rudder and chine
  experiments](/components/rudder.md#the-big-rudder-as-a-leeway-reducer); Kjell
  Karlsson reports his own "ParaPlastic" now has sides and bottom on;
  William Longyard clarifies no one had proposed a cyberspace release, only
  a proper English print edition; Nels owns the cyberspace/PayPal e-book
  idea as his own suggestion; Longyard's closing verdict favors a proper
  North American/UK publisher over a direct release, with Yrvind to
  receive full royalties either way — extending [the BRIS
  Paradox](/boats/bris-paradox.md#a-proposed-group-project-to-translate-sven-yrvinds-full-book-into-english),
  [Kjell Karlsson](/people/kjell-karlsson.md),
  [William H. Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md), and
  [Nels](/people/nels.md). One page (page_1873, an off-topic promotional
  post for an unrelated "boat_camping" Yahoo Group, no Paradox content) is
  recorded here for completeness but not folded into the bundle body. No
  new files; all content extended existing pages.

* **Update (batch page_1861–page_1870, msgnum 1884-1893)**: 24 January 2004.
  (1) The tail of Jack Gardiner's "building update"/cabin-height tangent —
  his own good-humored self-correction and Alastair's answer to his earlier
  forward-stateroom storage question — extended
  [Jack Gardiner](/people/jack-gardiner.md),
  [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), and
  [interior bin panels and hinged floor](/components/interior.md#forward-compartment-access).
  (2) A new **"chinerunners"** thread — Jack asks whether the hull bottom
  should be sealed first and the runners fitted afterward as a sacrificial,
  replaceable rub rail; Alastair answers that the standard integral-build
  order is right, since a Paradox chine runner is "built like a tank" and
