  Kjell Karlsson added a sandwich-panel cautionary anecdote from his own
  "BRIS Paradox" build: a 5 mm Divinycell baffle went from easily workable
  to catastrophically stiff — collapsing "like an explosion in my shop" —
  once both faces were glassed. All folded into a new section of
  [boats/bris-paradox.md](/boats/bris-paradox.md#why-yrvind-specified-a-flexible-not-rigid-sandwich)
  (citations 21–28), cross-linked from
  [people/william-longyard.md](/people/william-longyard.md) (citations
  16–18, plus a closing note on the book's reception), 
  [people/kjell-karlsson.md](/people/kjell-karlsson.md) (citation 5), and
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 163, Glen's "couldn't put it down" praise of Longyard's book).
  Separately, Smith's "Robust construction" post gave the archive's most
  forceful professional-boatbuilder case that Paradox's design-as-drawn
  needs no strengthening — backed by plywood cost figures and his own
  trouble-free sheer-clamp/chine-log installation — and David Beard's reply
  agreeing with Smith revealed that "Whisper"'s sides were originally built
  with **1/4 in ply** rather than the plans' 1/2 in, closing the loop with
  the earlier undersize-side-panel discovery; he added a second 1/4 in
  layer of his own during a later refit. Folded into
  [construction/hull-planking.md](/construction/hull-planking.md#the-case-for-a-second-layer-of-cloth)
  (citations 14–15, and citation 13 updated from "unanswered" to answered),
  cross-linked from [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 41) and
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (citations 159–161, and
  the Undersize-side-panels paragraph extended). No new files created, no
  promotions or prunings. All three standing backlog items (Jeff UK,
  botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold Larry/`newspipetr3`) were
  checked and none resurfaced.

* **Batch 116 (pages 1151–1160, msgnum 1168–1177, 24 August – 7 September
  2003) — two 2003 magazine articles about "Zoë" and "Whisper," a
  sleeping-arrangements exchange, a new hatch-security warning, Ken
  Riggins's first construction progress, and a still-open glass-layer
  question.** Read all ten source e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header
  was distinct (1168–1177), confirming no stale-read issue, and all ten
  source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly. Msgnum
  mapping was a **clean, fully consecutive run (1168–1177) with no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 115's 1167 — the thirtieth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last thirty-one. Dave Bolduc flagged Glen
  Maxwell's *Messing About In Boats* article on "Zoë"'s 2001 shakedown
  cruise, and ten days later Ron Hoddinott's *Small Craft Advisor* "Cedar
  Key Small Boat Meet" report covering both "Zoë" and David Beard's
  "Whisper" (and sighting Matt Layden, "the reclusive designer") — folded
  into a new section of [boats/zoe.md](/boats/zoe.md#press-coverage-two-2003-magazine-articles)
  and a new section of
  [operations/group-cruise-reports.md](/operations/group-cruise-reports.md#press-coverage-the-2003-small-craft-advisor-cedar-key-article),
  cross-linked from [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md)
  (citations 70, 72), [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 161), and a new footnote on Matt's low public profile in
  [matt-layden/biography.md](/matt-layden/biography.md) (citation 43).
  Replying to Andrew Graham's two-up sleeping-test photos on "Aussie
  Paradox," Dave Bolduc described his and Mindy's head-to-foot/side-by-side
  sleeping methods aboard Little Cruiser, and Alastair described his own
  camping-derived sleep-mat-and-foam-block system — folded into a new
  section of [operations/crew-size-and-fit.md](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md#sleeping-two-aboard-a-paradox-family-cabin),
  cross-linked from [boats/aussie-paradox.md](/boats/aussie-paradox.md),
  [people/andrew-graham.md](/people/andrew-graham.md),
  [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md), [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md),
  and [boats/little-jim.md](/boats/little-jim.md). David Beard's "Word of
  Caution!" warned that a locking hatch hasp's hinge must go on the aft
  window, never the hatch itself, or a sailor can lock himself in; Alastair
  answered with a simpler padlock-through-the-hatch-rail alternative —
  **promoted to new [construction/hatch-hardware.md](/construction/hatch-hardware.md)**,
  cross-linked from [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md),
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md), and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md);
  added to [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md). Ken Riggins
  (introduced batch 115) posted his first real construction progress —
  a first deck beam removed from the jig, "dead on" — drawing Glen
  Maxwell's welcome; folded as a continuation into
  [construction/build-effort.md](/construction/build-effort.md#discovering-the-design-through-the-bolducs-site)
  (citations 22–23). David Beard's "Glass" question — whether a second
  layer of 6 oz cloth over the sides and top is worth the added epoxy and
  labor — was folded into a new section of
  [construction/hull-planking.md](/construction/hull-planking.md#the-case-for-a-second-layer-of-cloth)
  as a standing open question, no reply recorded. Checked all three
  standing backlog items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the
  below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced in this batch.
  No prunings. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the fourteen files that received new citations
  ([boats/zoe.md](/boats/zoe.md) 25–27,
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  161–162, [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md) 70–72,
  [operations/crew-size-and-fit.md](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) 27–28,
  [boats/aussie-paradox.md](/boats/aussie-paradox.md) 10–11,
  [people/andrew-graham.md](/people/andrew-graham.md) 7–8,
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) 115–116,
  [boats/little-jim.md](/boats/little-jim.md) 74,
  [construction/hatch-hardware.md](/construction/hatch-hardware.md) 1–2 (new file),
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) 157–158,
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md) 70–71,
  [construction/hull-planking.md](/construction/hull-planking.md) 13,
  [construction/build-effort.md](/construction/build-effort.md) 22–23,
  [operations/group-cruise-reports.md](/operations/group-cruise-reports.md) 30,
  [matt-layden/biography.md](/matt-layden/biography.md) 43).

* **Batch 115 (pages 1141–1150, msgnum 1158–1167, 19–23 August 2003) —
  mainsheet/pintle fouling fix, a rainwater-collection thread, a newcomer's
  thanks, and Andrew Graham's fullest "Aussie Paradox" construction report
  to date.** Read all ten source e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was
  distinct (1158–1167), confirming no stale-read issue, and all ten source
  files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly. Msgnum mapping
  was a **clean, fully consecutive run (1158–1167) with no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 114's 1157 — the twenty-ninth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last thirty. **Smith** and **Alastair** closed out
  the prior batch's rudder-stock-height/mainsheet-fouling problem: Smith
  suggested a webbing tie from rudder head to deck (not shock cord) or a
  deck-led sheet; Alastair clarified the sheet actually rides over the top
  of the pintle rather than dropping into the notch, described his own
  pintle-cap fix, and raised a still-open question about whether a
  deck-led sheet could keep the correct boom-end load with roller
  reefing — folded into
  [design/rudder.md](/components/rudder.md#rudder-stock-height-and-mainsheet-fouling-at-the-transom-notch)
  (citations 23–24), cross-linked from
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 39) and
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 113). A new **"Water
  Collection"** thread — David Beard asking whether the plans'
  rain-catchment plumbing is worth installing if the tanks themselves
  won't be used, just jugs/bladders — drew Glen Maxwell's confirmation
  that the same setup works fine run to a hose on the output side, and
  Alastair's fuller field report (works as drawn, pump/siphon out via a
  bung, but not worth the extra deck penetration unless rain catchment
  specifically is wanted) — folded into a new section of
  [construction/water-tank-lining.md](/construction/water-tank-lining.md#rainwater-collection-plumbing-for-filling-jugs-or-bladders)
  (citations 13–15), cross-linked from
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 156),
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 158), and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation
  114). A new one-off newcomer, **Ken Riggins**, thanked the Bolducs and
  Don Elliott for prompt plans/articles delivery and the list for its
  build photos — folded as a short addendum into
  [construction/build-effort.md](/construction/build-effort.md#discovering-the-design-through-the-bolducs-site)
  (citations 20–21), cross-linked from
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 159); kept inline per the people threshold (single "thanks"
  message, no boat yet). This batch's centerpiece: **Andrew Graham**
  delivered the archive's fullest single "Aussie Paradox" construction
  report — chine-log/sheer-clamp bevel technique without a table saw, a
  doubled-up sides/bottom lamination to stiffen the Australian ply,
  biaxial-glass/aluminium-powder bottom glassing figures, vent-trunk and
  interior-panel/seat/table detail, Abelflex closed-cell foam insulation,
  and rudder blade/stock construction including a self-corrected
  20mm pintle-position measuring error — folded into a new section of
  [boats/aussie-paradox.md](/boats/aussie-paradox.md#a-fuller-progress-report-sides-bottom-deck-framing-and-interior-detail)
  (citations 7–9), cross-linked from
  [people/andrew-graham.md](/people/andrew-graham.md) (citation 6),
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 160), and [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 40, Smith's
  reply hoping to see Andrew's launch photos soon). No new files created,
  no promotions or prunings. Checked all three standing backlog
  identity/below-threshold items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel,
  "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced.

* **Batch 114 (pages 1131–1140, msgnum 1148–1157, 18–19 August 2003) —
  "Shim deckbeam #1?": a recurring cross-builder deck-beam-height
  discrepancy, a rudder-stock/mainsheet fouling problem, and two thread
  codas.** Read all ten source e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was
  distinct (1148–1157), confirming no stale-read issue, and all ten source
  files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly. Msgnum mapping
  was a **clean, fully consecutive run (1148–1157) with no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 113's 1147 — the twenty-eighth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last twenty-nine. **Smith**, dry-fitting his hull,
  found deck beam #1 **below fair at the centerline**, matching a shim
  already visible on **Hugh's New Zealand Paradox**; **Andrew Graham**
  independently hit the identical shortfall on **Aussie Paradox**, fixing
  it with a 5mm centerline shim and a shaved frame 3, and sidestepping the
  equivalent problem on his intermediate beams by running them
  longitudinally rather than athwartships; **Glen Maxwell**, unaffected on
  "Zoë," described his angled-trough sheer-clamp mounting technique
  ("putty and paint will make her what she ain't"); and **Alastair**
  suspects he met the same issue but never isolated it amid his own
  extensive gap-filling — folded into a new section of
  [construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md#shimming-deck-beam-1-and-its-neighbor-to-fair-the-centerline)
  (citations 13–19), with cross-linked summaries added to
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 38),
  [people/andrew-graham.md](/people/andrew-graham.md) (citation 5),
  [boats/aussie-paradox.md](/boats/aussie-paradox.md) (citation 6, new
  section), [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 111), and
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 157). The same thread carried Andrew Graham's report of
  laminating chine runners to the sides before the bottom (folded into the
  same section, no outcome recorded) and Alastair's separate rudder-stock
  height problem — the mainsheet catching in the notch between rudder and
  transom against the top pintle — folded into a new section of
  [design/rudder.md](/components/rudder.md#rudder-stock-height-and-mainsheet-fouling-at-the-transom-notch)
  (citation 22), cross-linked from
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 112). Smith also posted
  a standalone follow-up to his own unanswered "braided carbon sleeves"
  sourcing question (batch 112), naming a candidate supplier, **A&P
  Technology** (`braider.com`) — folded into
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#a-birdmouth-carbon-fiber-composite-spar-as-an-alternative-to-the-plans-rig)'s
  existing birdmouth-carbon-spar section (citation 54), cross-linked from
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 37). **Don Elliott**'s one-line
  "Thanks Mike" closed out the "Building Paradox booklet a special notice"
  printing-defect thread (batches 112–113) — folded as a closing line into
  [references/building-the-paradox-articles.md](/references/building-the-paradox-articles.md#a-printing-defect-in-the-booklet-form-missing-chapter-12-or-13)
  (citation 14), cross-linked from
  [people/don-elliott.md](/people/don-elliott.md) (citation 16). No new
  files created, no promotions or prunings. Checked all three standing
  backlog identity/below-threshold items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie
  Fossel, "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced.

* **Batch 113 (pages 1121–1130, msgnum 1138–1147, 16–18 August 2003) —
  "Water damage": epoxy vs. paint on a hull with prior rot; a booklet
  printing-defect notice; and the "Chine logs" thread's tail (installation
  order, GPS-shielding test plan).** Read all ten source e-mails; every
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct (1138–1147), confirming no stale-read
  issue, and all ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions
  exactly. Msgnum mapping was a **clean, fully consecutive run (1138–1147)
  with no gaps**, continuing directly from batch 112's 1137 — the
  twenty-seventh gap-free ten-page batch of the last twenty-eight.
  **David Beard**, redoing "[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)"'s interior a
  second time, worried that epoxy-coating over a spot that once suffered
  water damage might trap residual moisture; **Larry Whited** recommended an
  antifreeze pre-treatment (David had already used a boric-acid/antifreeze
  mix) followed by epoxy, drawing on his own boyhood epoxy-saturation repair
  of a 14 ft sailboat, possibly taught to him by Platt Monfort of Airolite
  fame; **Nels** questioned the whole approach in favor of plain paint;
  **Alastair** made the practical case for epoxy (no surface prep, must
  withstand heavy gear, stays visible for monitoring renewed rot); and David
  closed the thread reporting that **Matt Layden** himself recommends epoxy
  inside and out, finished with paint — folded into a new section of
  [construction/epoxy-coating-technique.md](/construction/epoxy-coating-technique.md#sealing-a-hull-with-prior-waterrot-damage-epoxy-vs-paint)
  (citations 9–15), with cross-linked summaries added to
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md) (citations 68–69),
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (citations 153–155),
  [people/nels.md](/people/nels.md) (citation 22), and
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 110). Separately,
  **Don Elliott** sent a notice to recent "Building the Paradox" booklet
  buyers that a print run had some copies missing Chapter 12 or 13
  (identifiable by its righting-moment drawing), offering replacement pages
  by e-mail or mail; a new one-off correspondent, **Mike Sieloff**, confirmed
  his own copy was complete — folded into a new section of
  [references/building-the-paradox-articles.md](/references/building-the-paradox-articles.md#a-printing-defect-in-the-booklet-form-missing-chapter-12-or-13)
  (citations 12–13), cross-linked from
  [people/don-elliott.md](/people/don-elliott.md) (citation 15); Mike Sieloff
  kept inline per the people threshold (single message, no boat). Finally,
  **David C. Smith** replied to Alastair's prior "Chine logs" message (batch 112),
  confirming the plan to fit sheer clamps before chine logs and expecting
  little trouble from them, wondering whether Matt Layden's own prototype
  used green or air-dried lumber for its chine logs, and detailing a test
  plan for GPS reception through foil-faced foam insulation (a mocked-up
  cabintop test, a car-GPS analogy, a vent-trunk external-antenna fallback,
  reliance on a small Danish radar reflector like "Zoë"'s rather than radar,
  and the foil's expected electrolytic-action and bonding benefits) — folded
  into a new section of
  [construction/chine-runner-construction.md](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md#foil-faced-foam-and-radiogps-shielding)
  (citation 14), cross-linked from
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 36) and
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (this Smith-authored citation was
  placed in `chine-runner-construction.md` and `dc.md` only, not duplicated
  into `alastair-law.md`, which received only its own water-damage citation
  above). No new files created, no promotions or prunings; checked all three
  standing Open items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the
  below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced in this batch.
  Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the
  eight files that received new citations
  (`construction/epoxy-coating-technique.md` [9]-[15],
  `boats/whisper.md` [68]-[69], `people/david-beard.md` [153]-[155],
  `people/nels.md` [22], `people/alastair-law.md` [110],
  `construction/chine-runner-construction.md` [14], `people/david-c-smith.md` [36],
  `references/building-the-paradox-articles.md` [12]-[13],
  `people/don-elliott.md` [15]).

* **Batch 112 (pages 1111–1120, msgnum 1128–1137, 14–16 August 2003) — the
  "Chine logs" thread: milling solid chine logs, a birdmouth carbon-fiber
  spar proposal, casting lead ballast in a chine-runner mold, and hardware
  bonding.** Read all ten source e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was
  distinct (1128–1137), confirming no stale-read issue, and all ten source
  files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly. Msgnum mapping
  was a **clean, fully consecutive run (1128–1137) with no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 111's 1127 — the twenty-sixth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last twenty-seven. Content: [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md)
  opened the batch reporting that milling chine logs from **Honduras
  Mahogany** (used throughout his boat) proved much harder to bend than
  white oak, possibly requiring resawing and lamination, and — in a
  separate message the same night — proposed building his spars using the
  **birdmouth method**, with a braided carbon-fiber sleeve wetted out over
  the wood mandrel, matching designer Matt Layden's own original
  composite-rig intent; he had found an orthopedic-supply source for the
  sleeve but hoped for an aerospace-grade one instead — folded into
  [construction/chine-runner-construction.md](/chine-runners/construction.md#milling-solid-chine-logs-from-a-dense-hardwood)
  (citation 8) and
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#a-birdmouth-carbon-fiber-composite-spar-as-an-alternative-to-the-plans-rig)
  (citation 53), with a sourcing cross-link (no new citation) added to
  [references/materials-sourcing-links.md](/references/materials-sourcing-links.md).
  [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md) replied describing
  his own pre-cut-block chine-runner method, then proposed reusing a shaped
  runner section as a **mold to cast lead ballast**, hardware-bonded to the
  chine log, to move weight further out on the lever arm — drawing a
  movable-vs-fixed-ballast debate from a new one-off correspondent, **roger
  chan** (kept below the person-page threshold: a single message, no boat),
  and from established members [Nels](/people/nels.md) and
  [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), both confirming that Paradox's small
  size and inside steering make **shifting crew weight**, not ballast, the
  practical way to trim her; Alastair separately described his own
  batten-and-fill chine-runner-shaping method and warned that **foil-faced
  foam insulation blocks radio signals** (e.g. GPS) in the cabin — all
  folded into
  [construction/chine-runner-construction.md](/chine-runners/construction.md#casting-lead-ballast-into-the-runners)
  (citations 9–13), cross-linked from [people/nels.md](/people/nels.md)
  (citation 21) and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) (citations
  108–109). The same thread's **hardware-bonding** technique — Glen's
  epoxy-set-fastener pull-test method as an alternative to thru-bolting deck
  hardware, and Smith's caution that direct sun on black-colored bonded
  hardware could generate enough heat to weaken the epoxy bond — was folded
  into
  [construction/fastening-and-gluing.md](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md#hardware-bonding-epoxy-set-fasteners-in-place-of-thru-bolting)
  (citations 18–20), cross-linked from [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md)
  (citations 34–35) and
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citations 155–156). Separately, the "New Boatbuilding Method" thread
  (opened batch 111) closed out: Chilean boatbuilder Antonio Bordeu answered
  Glen Maxwell's sourcing question about his frameless urethane-foam
  composite method (a two-part isocyanate/polyol foam sold anywhere
  fiberglass supplies are sold, best bought ready-made from a
  refrigeration-supply company rather than mixed from scratch) — folded into
  [design/aluminum-construction-idea.md](/design/aluminum-construction-idea.md)
  (citation 6), correcting that file's now-stale "no reply is recorded"
  line from batch 111; also corrected
  [design/index.md](/design/index.md)'s one-line summary of that file,
  which had not been updated when batch 111 added the urethane-foam
  section. No new files created, no promotions or prunings; **roger chan**
  kept inline per the people threshold (a single appearance, no boat).
  Checked all three standing backlog Open items ("Jeff UK,"
  "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`):
  none resurfaced in this batch. Verified no new citation-number collisions
  were introduced across the eight files that received new citations
  ([construction/chine-runner-construction.md](/chine-runners/construction.md)
  8–13,
  [construction/fastening-and-gluing.md](/construction/fastening-and-gluing.md)
  18–20, [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md) 53,
  [design/aluminum-construction-idea.md](/design/aluminum-construction-idea.md)
  6, [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) 34–35, [people/nels.md](/people/nels.md)
  21, [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md) 108–109,
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  155–156).

* **Batch 111 (pages 1101–1110, msgnum 1118–1127, 3–14 August 2003) — the
  "New Sail" thread's follow-up replies, a named New Zealand builder, and
  two thin one-off outside pitches.** Read all ten source e-mails; every
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct (1118–1127), confirming no
  stale-read issue, and all ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch
  instructions exactly. **Special check on the identical 1421-byte pair:**
  page_1107 (msgnum 1124, Antonio Bordeu's "New Boatbuilding Method") and
  page_1109 (msgnum 1126, Bjorn Harbo's "Sven Yrvind's new Book") were
  directly confirmed via `cat` to have different msgnums, senders,
  subjects, and bodies — two genuinely distinct posts, not a duplicate; the
  identical byte count is coincidental. Msgnum mapping was a **clean,
  fully consecutive run (1118–1127) with no gaps**, continuing directly
  from batch 110's 1117. Content: three replies to Glen Maxwell's "New
  Sail" post (batch 110) — [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) and
  [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md) both asked the cloth weight and whether Dabbler
  followed Matt Layden's instructions, and Glen answered fully (5 oz
  cloth, sailmaker Stuart's Nutshell-pram-fleet pedigree, confirmation the
  leech tell-tales work well, and Matt Layden's own two-inch-camber "eye
  for measurements" story, with the string-and-batten measurement
  technique) while also offering his superseded original sail for sale —
  folded into
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#the-dabbler-re-cut-mark-ii-sail-a-decisive-improvement)
  (citations 50–52), cross-linked from [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md)
  (citation 32). Dave Bolduc posted a further round of New Zealand Paradox
  photos, and this batch's exchange with Glen Maxwell reveals the
  previously unnamed New Zealand builder's first name, **Hugh** (a
  then-67-year-old builder), who hoped to have the boat sailing by the New
  Zealand summer — updated
  [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) and [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md)
  (citations 68–69) accordingly. Smith's own "materials" post pointed the list
  to `onlinemetals.com` (a Seattle small-quantity metal supplier) alongside
  a build-progress note (deckbeams laminated in May, sides due to be
  sprung around the bulkheads that week) — folded into
  [references/materials-sourcing-links.md](/references/materials-sourcing-links.md)
  (citation 7) and [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citation 33). Two thin,
  unrelated outside pitches: Chilean boatbuilder Antonio Bordeu introduced
  a frameless urethane-foam composite method with no Paradox connection,
  drawing one curious but unresolved question from Glen Maxwell and no
  further reply — folded as a short new section into
  [design/aluminum-construction-idea.md](/design/aluminum-construction-idea.md)
  (citations 4–5), alongside the file's existing aluminum-construction
  speculation as a matching "outside pitch, never adopted" case. Bjorn
  Harbo posted directly to the list for the first time, announcing Sven
  Yrvind's newly received Swedish-language book, which includes a chapter
  on Paradox, chine runners, and the Yrvind/Layden design cooperation —
  folded into
  [boats/bris-paradox.md](/boats/bris-paradox.md#reference-material-and-a-related-yrvind-mailing-list)
  (citation 20), paying off the prior batch's secondhand mention that the
  wider group wanted the book translated. **Skipped:** page_1110 (msgnum
  1127, Smith, subject "Chine logs") has an empty body (`Content-Length: 1`
  in the header, confirmed genuinely blank on direct read) — no content to
  extract. No new files created, no promotions or prunings. Checked all
  three standing backlog Open items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel,
  the below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced in this
  batch. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced across
  the six files that received new citations
  ([design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md) 50–52, [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md)
  32–33, [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md) 68–69,
  [references/materials-sourcing-links.md](/references/materials-sourcing-links.md)
  7, [design/aluminum-construction-idea.md](/design/aluminum-construction-idea.md)
  4–5, [boats/bris-paradox.md](/boats/bris-paradox.md) 20).

* **Batch 110 (pages 1091–1100, msgnum 1107–1112 and 1114–1117, 28 July – 2
  August 2003) — the "HAM savvy?" thread's full technical payoff, and a new
  "Tank Tops" and "New Sail" thread each closed out in full.** Read all ten
  source e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct (1107, 1108,
  1109, 1110, 1111, 1112, 1114, 1115, 1116, 1117) confirming no stale-read
  issue, and all ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions
  exactly. Msgnum mapping had one real, confirmed **archive gap at 1113**
  (page_1096 = 1112, page_1097 = 1114, both distinct content, distinct
  sizes) — otherwise fully consecutive, continuing directly from batch 109's
  1106. Content: the "HAM savvy?" thread (opened batch 109) ran to its full
  conclusion — [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md)'s firsthand
  Outbacker-vs-Buddipole portable-antenna comparison and Waterway Radio and
  Cruising Club net practice; a technical exchange between
  [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md) and [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md) on
  SailMail vs. Winlink/Airmail, Pactor I/II/III TNC cost and speed, the
  legality of ham radio for marine SSB use, and weatherfax reception; and
  Dave Bolduc's closing PocketMail alternative — folded into three new
  sections of
  [operations/radio-and-electronics.md](/operations/radio-and-electronics.md)
  (citations 4–9), with cross-linked citations added to
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) (citations 30–31),
  [people/david-leblanc.md](/people/david-leblanc.md) (citations 47–48), and
  [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (citations 66–67). A new
  "Tank Tops" thread — [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) asking whether
  the plans-structural tank tops need to be both epoxied and screwed down or
  just screwed, since he uses his purely as dry storage and wants them
  removable for inspection, answered by [Glen
  Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (a screw every 4 in is fine) and closed
  by David Beard confirming no detectable flex sailing "Whisper" this way —
  folded into a new section of
  [construction/water-tank-lining.md](/construction/water-tank-lining.md#fastening-the-tank-tops-when-used-purely-as-storage-not-water-tanks)
  (citations 10–12), cross-linked from
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md) (citations 151–152) and
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 153). Glen Maxwell's "New Sail" post closed out the Dabbler
  re-cut plan set up in batch ~85 (Ft. Desoto, Everglades Challenge
  lead-up): his Mark II sail from Dabbler arrived beautifully made, fit
  exactly per Matt Layden's own new-sail dimensions (6 in trimmable off the
  boom end), and proved "no comparison" in performance — much more draft,
  quicker power-up, no more tack-to-tack asymmetry, tighter trim, higher
  pointing — with Glen's enthusiastic recommendation of Dabbler despite the
  price — folded into a new section of
  [design/rig.md](/components/rig/index.md#the-dabbler-re-cut-mark-ii-sail-a-decisive-improvement)
  (citation 49), cross-linked from
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md)
  (citation 154). No new files created; no promotions or prunings. Checked
  all three standing backlog Open items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie
  Fossel, the below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`): none resurfaced in
  this batch. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the eight files that received new citations.

* **Batch 109 (pages 1081–1090, msgnum 1097–1106, 14–23 July 2003) — two
  thread-tails closed out, a plywood-grade question settled, and a new
  radio/electronics topic opened.** Read all ten source e-mails; every
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct and the run was fully consecutive
  (1097–1106) with no gaps, continuing directly from batch 108's 1096 — the
  twenty-fourth gap-free ten-page batch of the last twenty-five. All ten
  source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly and no
  stale-read issue surfaced. Content: the "Bottom Paint ??" thread (batch 108)
  closed with [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md)'s one-line thanks and
  decision to re-apply conventional antifouling to "Whisper" — folded as a
  closing line into
  [construction/bottom-painting.md](/construction/bottom-painting.md#why-wax-enamel-and-other-antifouling-shortcuts-fail)
  (citation 33). The "Transom" crown/bevel thread also closed, with
  [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md) catching and correcting his own fatigue-caused
  rudder-template drafting slip (a plumb transom shown instead of the plans'
  raked one) — folded into
  [construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md#transom-and-bulkhead-4-crown-and-fitting-bevels-at-trial-assembly)
  (citation 16). New builder Duane Hall (`keywestdale`, Cookeville, TN,
  introduced batch 108), plans in hand, asked the archive's recurring
  exterior-vs-marine plywood question:
  [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) gave the fullest species-specific
  answer yet (good-quality Douglas fir exterior ply, not white pine, is
  structurally sound and shares marine grade's glue, but marine is worth the
  cost for the side panels; avoid thin deck veneers regardless of grade), and
  [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) reinforced it from his own "Zoë"
  build — exterior ply's real cost is finishing labor, not structural risk,
  which "makes marine ply a steal" by comparison; Duane chose marine — folded
  into
  [construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md#a-new-builders-exterior-vs-marine-choice-and-why-marine-usually-wins-on-labor)
  (new section, citations 22–26) with cross-linked summaries in
  [people/don-elliott.md](/people/don-elliott.md),
  [people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md](/people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md),
  and [construction/build-effort.md](/construction/build-effort.md); Duane
  remains below the person-page threshold. Separately, Smith opened a new "HAM
  savvy?" thread proposing to use the Paradox mast's existing
  lightning-protection wiring as a ready-made HF radio antenna groundplane
  with an automatic tuner — a genuinely new topic with no prior home in the
  tree, **promoted to new
  [operations/radio-and-electronics.md](/operations/radio-and-electronics.md)**
  (citations 1–3), cross-linked from
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md#history-and-refit) (which
  already documents the same mast wiring's lightning-protection role) and
  [people/david-leblanc.md](/people/david-leblanc.md) (whose reply noted Dave
  Bolduc had moved from an Outbacker to a Buddipole antenna aboard Little
  Cruiser); added to [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md). No new
  boats/people promoted beyond the new topic file, no prunings; all three
  standing Open items checked (Jeff UK, botebilder/Ernie Fossel,
  below-threshold Larry/newspipetr3), none resurfaced; cursor.txt set to
  1090; sonnet.
* **Batch 108 (pages 1071–1080, msgnum 1087–1096, 11–14 July 2003) — a thin
  batch, two thread-tails: the "Transom" crown/bevel question closed out, and
  a new "Bottom Paint ??" thread ran its full course.** Read all ten source
  e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct and the run was fully
  consecutive (1087–1096) with no gaps, continuing directly from batch 107's
  1086 — the twenty-third gap-free ten-page batch of the last twenty-four.
  All ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly and
  no stale-read issue surfaced. Content: David C. Smith (`betelgeuserdude`) asked
  whether the transom's side framing (and bulkhead #4) should carry crown,
  given inconsistent plan sheets; [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) answered
  from his own build (a slight, asymmetric crown, not enough to cause
  trouble) and offered a looser philosophy — don't fuss over exact bevels
  ahead of time, shape them freehand with an angle grinder at trial assembly
  instead — which Smith adopted for his own stem bevel — folded into
  [construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md#transom-and-bulkhead-4-crown-and-fitting-bevels-at-trial-assembly)
  (new section, citations 13–15) with cross-linked summaries in
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md) and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md).
  Separately, [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) asked for antifouling
  alternatives for "Whisper" (Interlux Brightsides below the waterline, thin
  films, or Sven Yrvind's reported Bris-hull waxing practice), drawing Smith's
  extensive technical case against wax/enamel/LPU shortcuts and his
  epoxy-barrier/vinyl/Pettit-Trinidad recommendation for serious cruising
  (Interlux Micron, tinted green, for his own boat), a tint follow-up with
  [William Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md), David LeBlanc's (`whisper@`/
  `meerkatoz` — confirmed distinct from David Beard's `dlb@`/`buster38801` per
  the standing disambiguation note) own four-day SeaPearl-fouling warning
  against skipping bottom paint entirely, and Larry Whited's Copperpoxy/VC
  Offshore recommendation from his SeaPearl 28 — folded into
  [construction/bottom-painting.md](/construction/bottom-painting.md#why-wax-enamel-and-other-antifouling-shortcuts-fail)
  (new section, citations 26–32) with cross-linked summaries in
  [people/david-c-smith.md](/people/david-c-smith.md), [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md)
  (citation 149), [people/david-leblanc.md](/people/david-leblanc.md)
  (citation 45), and [people/william-longyard.md](/people/william-longyard.md)
  (citation 15). No new files, no promotions/prunings; all three standing
  Open items checked (Jeff UK, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold
  Larry/newspipetr3), none resurfaced; cursor.txt set to 1080; sonnet.

* **Batch 107 (pages 1061–1070, msgnum 1077–1086, 9–11 July 2003) — a thin
  batch: a newcomer-welcome thread and a book-purchase-logistics thread, plus
  one genuinely empty message.** Read all ten source e-mails; every
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct and the run was fully consecutive
  (1077–1086) with no gaps, continuing directly from batch 106's 1076 — the
  twenty-second gap-free ten-page batch of the last twenty-three. All ten
  source files' byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly and no
  stale-read issue surfaced. Content: prospective builder "Duane"
  (`keywestdale`, Cookeville, TN) continued the "LOOKS LIKE I FOUND MY FUTURE
  BOAT" thread opened in batch 106 — traded locations with
  [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (Tupelo, MS, sailing one Paradox and
  building a second), was gently ribbed by "Larry" (`LJGeib`) and

  completed his Yahoo Groups profile, and was welcomed aboard by
  [Glen Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) — folded into

  (citation 23); Duane stays below the person-page threshold (no boat/build
  yet), consistent with his introduction in batch 106. Separately,
  [William Longyard](/people/william-longyard.md)'s "A Speck on the Sea"
  book announcement (batch 106) drew a follow-up: Glen Maxwell asked whether
  to cancel his standing Amazon order for a signed copy, Longyard offered a
  copy-exchange for postage cost once Amazon shipments began, and a UK list
  member (`kevin1b`, previously a one-off correspondent) asked about
  cash-by-post payment and overseas shipping — no answer to Kevin's question
  is recorded — folded into
  [people/william-longyard.md](/people/william-longyard.md) (citations
  12–14; also fixed a pre-existing duplicated `# Citations` heading in that
  file). David C. Smith (`betelgeuserdude`) posted a message titled "Transom" with a
  completely empty body (`Content-Length: 1`) — logged as skipped, no content
  to extract. No new files, no promotions/prunings; all three standing Open
  items checked (Jeff UK, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, below-threshold
  Larry/newspipetr3), none resurfaced; cursor.txt set to 1070; sonnet.
* **Register-shift cleanup pass (all 130 concept pages).** Rewrote the whole
  tree from a conversation-chronicle register ("X asked, Y answered on date Z")
  into universal, encyclopedic knowledge about the boat, its people, and its
  history — backed by the forum threads through the **fully preserved**
  `# Citations` blocks. Person pages were reshaped from message-by-message logs
  into characterizations (e.g. [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) 1303→267
  lines, [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) 1201→270); topic/design/construction/
  operations pages got declarative thematic headings with blow-by-blow
  turn-taking collapsed into stated facts; boat pages kept their full narrative,
  de-conversation-ized. Archive-ingestion artifacts ("in this batch",
  duplicate-post forensics, "one-off correspondent") were removed from article
  prose; citation identity and evidence were left intact (light-scrub of
  ingestion phrasing only). 445 cross-file anchors broken by heading changes
  were degraded to clean file-links (40 still-valid anchors kept); every
  `index.md` description was refreshed to the new one-sentence frontmatter.
  Editorial spec: `.okf-progress/CLEANUP-SPEC.md`; pre-cleanup backup:
  `.okf-progress/backups/bundle-2026-07-03/`.
* **Batch 106 (pages 1051–1060, msgnum 1067–1076, 3–10 July 2003) — a
  seamanship-vs-seaworthiness essay, a materials round-up, and a new
  prospective builder found via the Bolducs' site.** Read all ten source
  e-mails; every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct and the run was
  fully consecutive (1067–1076) with no gaps, continuing directly from
  batch 105's 1066 — the twenty-first gap-free ten-page batch of the
  last twenty-two. All ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch
  instructions exactly and no stale-read issue surfaced. Content: new
  one-off correspondent Blake Lauder asked how far offshore a Paradox
  could safely venture, drawing [David C. Smith](/people/david-c-smith.md)'s fullest statement
  yet on seamanship-over-seaworthiness — a seamanlike attitude, gained
  through experience, matters more than a vessel's in-hindsight
  "seaworthy" label; he judged the Paradox "a masterful design" refined
  through the designer's own years of experimentation, and saw no reason
  it could not make transoceanic passages in an experienced sailor's
  hands — endorsed without qualification by Glen Maxwell ("Exceptionally
  well put Smith!") and David Beard ("Just wait till you spend time in
  one") — folded into `design/blue-water-suitability.md` (new section,
  citations `[12]`-`[17]`) with a cross-linked summary in `people/david-c-smith.md`.
  Smith separately opened a "materials" thread comparing epoxy brands
  (West System vs. System Three, the latter slightly more flexible but
  earning his preference for the rest of his build) and non-skid deck
  coatings (sand-in-paint vs. Glen Maxwell's walnut-shell-in-epoxy vs.
  his own favored Hypalon-rubber-in-latex product, "Spantex"/former
  "Flex-o-Tex," eight years' maintenance-free service on a prior boat) —
  folded into `construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md` (new section)
  and `construction/non-skid-deck.md` (new section), cross-linked from
  `people/david-c-smith.md`. William Longyard announced his new book, *A Speck on
  the Sea* (McGraw-Hill), profiling over 70 small-boat ocean voyagers
  since Roman times — folded into `people/william-longyard.md` (new
  section). A new prospective builder, "keywestdale" (Duane, of
  Tennessee), introduced himself after finding
  [microcruising.com](/references/microcruising-website.md) by chance
  and choosing the Paradox over a Glen-L design and a Long Micro on the
  strength of the Bolducs' and Don Elliott's prompt replies and the
  design's loyal, active builders' group — folded into
  `construction/build-effort.md` (new section, "Discovering the design
  through the Bolducs' site") with a cross-linked reply from David Beard
  in `people/david-beard.md`; kept inline per the people threshold (no
  boat/build yet). No new files, no promotions or prunings. All three
  standing Open items (Jeff UK, botebilder/Ernie Fossel, the
  below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`) checked; none resurfaced.
  Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the
  six files that received new citations (`design/blue-water-suitability.md`
  `[12]`-`[17]`, `construction/epoxy-brands-and-chemistry.md` `[7]`,
  `construction/non-skid-deck.md` `[5]`, `people/david-c-smith.md` `[19]`-`[21]`,
  `people/william-longyard.md` `[11]`, `construction/build-effort.md`
  `[11]`-`[12]`, `people/david-beard.md` `[147]`-`[148]`).
* **Batch 105 (pages 1041–1050, msgnum 1057–1066, 23–29 June 2003) — the
  "Plastic Paradox" thread wound all the way down, and a fresh, nineteenth
  recurrence of the plans-sourcing FAQ.** Read all ten source e-mails;
  every `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header was distinct and the run was fully
  consecutive (1057–1066) with no gaps, continuing directly from batch
  104's 1056 — the twentieth gap-free ten-page batch of the last
  twenty-one. All ten source files' byte sizes matched the batch
  instructions exactly and no stale-read issue surfaced. Content: Kjell
  Karlsson's 23 June "Plastic Paradox" answer (batch 104) drew a final
  round of replies closing out the "BRIS Paradox" stiffness/flexibility
  tangent — "proaconstrictor" conceded the design's own merits without
  either side fully resolving the stiffness question; Kjell posted a DIAB
  Sandwich Handbook reference link (broken, then fixed two days later
  after David Beard reported it wouldn't load); Nels pointed Kjell to a
  second, Yrvind-focused mailing list (`microcruising`) and its Oslo
  contact Bjorn Harbo, relaying secondhand that Yrvind had once tried a
  plywood/cloth-laminate Bris hull informed by Paradox's own features
  before reverting to the divinycell sandwich; and the thread closed with
  good-natured banter between Kjell and "proaconstrictor" on motors,
  national pride, and Lutheranism — folded into `boats/bris-paradox.md`
  (one new section, full narrative, citations `[12]`-`[19]`) with brief
  cross-linked summaries in `people/kjell-karlsson.md`, `people/nels.md`
  (new citation `[20]`), and `people/david-beard.md` (new citation
  `[146]`) per the "narrate once, cross-link" convention. Separately, a
  new one-off newcomer, Blake Lauder (`blakelauder`), asked the
  archive's now-nineteenth recurrence of the plans-sourcing FAQ ("Obviously
  I am a nubie..."), answered the next day by Larry Whited with the
  familiar Dave Bolduc/$35/microcruising.com pointer — the FAQ's most
  concise recurrence yet, a single confident reply with no hedging —
  folded into `references/building-plans.md` (new section, citations
  `[38]`-`[39]`). No new files created, no promotions or prunings; Blake
  Lauder and "proaconstrictor" both kept inline per the people threshold
  (established/one-off precedent, no ambiguity). All three standing Open
  items ("Jeff UK"/`roverticket`, "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the
  below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`) checked directly against all ten
  source e-mails: none resurfaced. Verified no new citation-number
  collisions were introduced across the five files that received new
  citations (`boats/bris-paradox.md` `[12]`-`[19]`, `people/
  kjell-karlsson.md` `[4]`, `people/nels.md` `[20]`, `people/
  david-beard.md` `[146]`, `references/building-plans.md` `[38]`-`[39]`).
  `cursor.txt` set to 1050.

* **CLEANUP — David Beard / David LeBlanc attribution correction (single-writer
  retrofit pass, resolving the Open backlog item from batch 104).** Audited
  every citation in `people/david-leblanc.md` (47 citations) and the 3
  specific instances the backlog item had already identified, plus spot-
  checked `people/david-beard.md`'s citations for the reverse error, by
  reading each underlying `source-emails/page_NNN.eml`'s `From:`/
  `X-Yahoo-Profile:` headers directly rather than trusting existing labels.
  Confirmed the ground truth: `dlb@...`/`X-Yahoo-Profile: buster38801` is
  **David Beard**'s own address/handle; `whisper@...`/`X-Yahoo-Profile:
  meerkatoz` is **David LeBlanc**'s. **Re-attributed 4 citations from David
  LeBlanc to David Beard** (all Beard→LeBlanc-direction errors; the reverse
  spot-check of `david-beard.md` found no LeBlanc material mislabeled as
  Beard): (1) `boats/bris-paradox.md` `[4]` (page_1023/msgnum 1039, "Welcome
  Kjell, Keep us posted") — relabeled David Beard, plus its prose paragraph
  which had wrongly described `dlb` as one of David LeBlanc's own addresses;
  (2) `people/david-leblanc.md`'s old `[46]` (same page_1023 message) —
  removed from that page and its "Welcoming Kjell Karlsson" section split:
  the "Welcome Kjell" half moved to a new section on `people/david-beard.md`
  (new citation `[143]`), the epoxy-elasticity point the following day
  (genuinely `whisper@...`/`meerkatoz`) kept on David LeBlanc's page under a
  retitled section; (3)-(4) `people/david-leblanc.md`'s old `[42]`-`[43]`
  (page_959/page_960, msgnum 975-976, the "flood waters and shoal draft"
  photo upload and "file atach./Whisper album" follow-up, both genuinely
  `dlb@...`/`buster38801`) — this was a **new instance beyond the 3 known
  ones**, found during this pass's full citation-by-citation audit; moved to
  a new section on `people/david-beard.md` (new citations `[144]`-`[145]`),
  with a matching short cross-linked section added to `boats/whisper.md`
  (the natural boat-page home, since the upload was added to "Whisper"'s own
  photo album). `people/kjell-karlsson.md` was checked per the backlog
  item's citation `[2]` but was **already correct** (its prose already named
  David Beard for the welcome and David LeBlanc for the epoxy point) — no
  edit needed there. Renumbered `people/david-leblanc.md`'s remaining
  citations to close the gaps (old `[44]`-`[47]` → new `[42]`-`[44]`);
  verified no duplicate or missing citation numbers afterward in any touched
  file (`david-leblanc.md` `[0]`-`[44]`, `david-beard.md` `[1]`-`[145]`,
  `bris-paradox.md` `[1]`-`[11]`, `kjell-karlsson.md` `[1]`-`[3]`,
  `whisper.md` unchanged numbering). Added a mutual disambiguation note to
  both `people/david-leblanc.md`'s and `people/david-beard.md`'s
  introductions explaining the double name/address coincidence (David
  Beard's handle `dlb`/`buster38801` superficially resembles "David
  LeBlanc"'s initials, while David LeBlanc's own address happens to contain
  the word "whisper," the name of Beard's boat) and stating explicitly that
  every remaining citation on David LeBlanc's page is confirmed
  `whisper@...`/`meerkatoz`. Full-tree grep confirmed no other file in the
  bundle mislabels a `dlb`/`buster38801` message as "David LeBlanc" — the
  error was fully confined to the two person pages plus `bris-paradox.md`'s
  single citation, all now corrected; **no evidence found of the error
  extending further**, so no broader whole-tree sweep is flagged as still
  needed. See the backlog for the item's resolution.

* **Batch 104 (pages 1031–1040, msgnum 1047–1056, 21–23 June 2003) — the
  "BRIS Paradox" laminate-schedule/stiffness thread resolved by Kjell
  Karlsson himself, the "LED array navigation lights?" thread's final
  real-world Coast Guard-enforcement/collision-liability asides, a new
  "Voids" lamination-technique thread on "Whisper," and a secondhand
  report of the archive's first New Zealand Paradox build.** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files; confirmed each of the ten byte sizes matched
  the batch instructions exactly (1452, 9072, 2061, 4857, 1406, 5929,
  6890, 1566, 2855, 3650 bytes for pages 1031–1040 respectively) and
  each page's own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header and message body were
  genuinely distinct — no stale-read issue surfaced this batch. Pages
  1031–1040 mapped to msgnum **1047, 1048, 1049, 1050, 1051, 1052,
  1053, 1054, 1055, 1056** — a clean, fully consecutive run with **no
  gaps**, continuing directly from batch 103's 1046 — the nineteenth
  gap-free ten-page batch of the last twenty. Dates ran 21–23 June
  2003. Content opened with "proaconstrictor" pressing a further
  stiffness/closed-cell-foam question into the "Briss Paradox"
  sub-thread (unanswered in this batch), then, two days later, Kjell
  Karlsson answering Glen Maxwell's laminate-schedule question himself
  under a new subject, "Plastic Paradox" — restating Sven Yrvind's
  "globally stiff and locally flexible" design goal with fuller
  lamination figures (fiberglass/polyester elongation percentages,
  layer counts, after-cure temperature) — drawing a brief, enthusiastic
  reply from David Beard — folded into `boats/bris-paradox.md` (two new
  sections, full narrative), with brief cross-linked summaries added to
  `people/kjell-karlsson.md`, `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md`,
  and `people/david-beard.md` per the "narrate once, cross-link"
  convention. Glen Maxwell also gave Andrew Graham's "Aussie Paradox dry
  run" a brief closing reply, folded into `boats/aussie-paradox.md` and
  `people/andrew-graham.md`. The "LED array navigation lights?" thread
  closed out with two final replies — "bigeddytom" (a new one-off,
  "Tom on Sandpiper") relaying a Florida kerosene-anchor-light/Coast
  Guard anecdote, and Larry Whited's closing collision-liability/
  shallow-draft-anchoring observation — folded into
  `operations/running-lights.md`. A new "Voids" thread — David Beard
  asking what problems a hidden lamination void might cause on
  "Whisper," reassured by a new one-off, "sctree" ("Rick") — was folded
  into `construction/fastening-and-gluing.md` (new section, full
  technical detail) with brief cross-linked summaries in
  `boats/whisper.md` and `people/david-beard.md`. Dave Bolduc reported,
  secondhand, an unnamed, near-complete Paradox build in New
  Zealand — built to the plans except for round, birdmouth-method
  spars — the archive's first record of a New Zealand build; folded
  into `people/dave-bolduc.md` (new section) with a cross-reference
  added to `boats/index.md`'s "other known builds" grouping. No new
  files created, no promotions or prunings; all three standing Open
  items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the below-threshold
  "Larry"/`newspipetr3`) checked — none resurfaced. While researching
  the "Voids" thread's `dlb`/`buster38801` byline, confirmed (by
  cross-checking `From`/`X-Yahoo-Profile` headers directly) that this
  handle belongs to **David Beard**, not David LeBlanc — surfacing a
  pre-existing misattribution in the bundle (three prior citations
  across `boats/bris-paradox.md`, `people/kjell-karlsson.md`, and
  `people/david-leblanc.md` label a `dlb`/`buster38801` message as
  "David LeBlanc"); corrected only the new citations added this batch
  to the right person, and recorded the retrofit as a new backlog Open
  item rather than fixing the pre-existing instances mid-batch. Verified
  no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the ten
  files that received new citations (`boats/bris-paradox.md`
  `[9]`-`[11]`, `people/kjell-karlsson.md` `[3]`,
  `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md` `[151]`,
  `boats/aussie-paradox.md` `[5]`, `operations/running-lights.md`
  `[13]`-`[14]`, `construction/fastening-and-gluing.md` `[15]`-`[17]`,
  `boats/whisper.md` `[66]`-`[67]`, `people/david-beard.md`
  `[140]`-`[142]`, `people/dave-bolduc.md` `[65]`).

* **Batch 103 (pages 1021–1030, msgnum 1037–1046, 19–21 June 2003) — the
  tail of Kjell Karlsson's "BRIS Paradox" welcome/laminate-schedule
  thread, Andrew Graham's first "Aussie Paradox" hull-assembly
  milestone, and the close of the LED-vs-kerosene navigation-lights
  thread.** Read all ten source `.eml` files; confirmed each of the ten
  byte sizes matched the batch instructions exactly (10954, 24628,
  10672, 2993, 13391, 3137, 6041, 7612, 3235, 1458 bytes for pages
  1021–1030 respectively) and each page's own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header
  and message body were genuinely distinct — no stale-read issue
  surfaced this batch. Pages 1021–1030 mapped to msgnum **1037, 1038,
  1039, 1040, 1041, 1042, 1043, 1044, 1045, 1046** — a clean, fully
  consecutive run with **no gaps**, continuing directly from batch
  102's 1036 — the eighteenth gap-free ten-page batch of the last
  nineteen. Dates ran 19–21 June 2003. Content opened with a round of
  welcomes to Kjell Karlsson's "BRIS Paradox" introduction (Bill
  Serjeant, Glen Maxwell, and David LeBlanc), followed by Kjell's own
  Divinycell density/lamination-weight follow-up, then a separate
  "Briss Paradox" sub-thread in which Glen Maxwell pressed for the
  hull's laminate schedule, drawing William Longyard's recollection of
  a personal conversation with Sven Yrvind (who wanted a deliberately
  flexible, not stiff, hull) and a partial rebuttal from David LeBlanc
  (epoxy spans a range of elastic moduli) — folded into
  `boats/bris-paradox.md` (two new sections), `people/kjell-karlsson.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md`, `people/david-leblanc.md`, and
  `people/william-longyard.md`. The cross-posted "LED array navigation
  lights?" thread (opened batch 102) closed out with two shorter
  replies — Fred Klitzke's Coleman-fluorescent-lantern alternative and
  a new one-off, "proaconstrictor," on relaxed Coast Guard enforcement
  and cheap integrated solar/LED "moonlights" — folded into
  `operations/running-lights.md`; neither drew a dedicated page (Fred
  Klitzke remains an established inline mention elsewhere in the
  bundle, "proaconstrictor" stays inline per the people threshold).
  Andrew Graham ("Aussie Paradox," Brisbane) posted a "dry run" progress
  update — the boat's first hull-assembly milestone, a test fit of the
  sides against the frames, plus refined deck-beam and transom-baffle
  changes from his introductory post — folded into
  `boats/aussie-paradox.md` (new section) and `people/andrew-graham.md`
  (brief cross-linked summary per the "narrate once, cross-link"
  convention). No new files created, no promotions or prunings; all
  three standing Open items ("Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel, the
  below-threshold "Larry"/`newspipetr3`) checked — none resurfaced.
  Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the
  seven files that received new citations (`boats/bris-paradox.md`
  `[2]`-`[8]`, `people/kjell-karlsson.md` `[2]`,
  `boats/aussie-paradox.md` `[4]`, `people/andrew-graham.md` `[4]`,
  `operations/running-lights.md` `[11]`-`[12]`,
  `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md` `[149]`-`[150]`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md` `[46]`-`[47]`, `people/william-longyard.md`
  `[10]`).

* **Batch 102 (pages 1011–1020, msgnum 1027–1036, 17–19 June 2003) — a
  light maintenance stretch (a rudder-checklist correction, a light-air
  handling suggestion, an LED-vs-kerosene navigation-lights debate)
  closing with a substantial new introduction: Sweden's first
  identified Paradox builder, and the archive's only composite-material
  build.** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each
  genuinely distinct (an initial read of pages 1017–1020 hit stale
  cached content identical to pages 1013–1016; re-read via direct file
  access confirmed all ten pages carry distinct headers and bodies).
  Re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  1011–1020 mapped to **1027, 1028, 1029, 1030, 1031, 1032, 1033, 1034,
  1035, 1036** — a clean, fully consecutive run with **no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 101's 1026 — the seventeenth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last eighteen. Dates ran 17–19 June 2003.
  Content opened with Don Elliott's one-line correction to his own
  "Odd Paradox control problem" tacking checklist (30 degrees of rudder
  easing either side, not 15), followed by a new one-off correspondent,
  Jim Goeckermann, offering a Bolger-Weekender-derived light-air
  crew-weight-shifting suggestion — Alastair tried it but found moving
  about inside Paradox has little effect on her heeling — folded into
  `design/rudder.md` (two new sections) and `people/don-elliott.md`.
  Alastair also answered Richard Green's design-verdict question from
  batch 101 ("Yes, it works," pointing to his website's trip reports),
  drawing a further thank-you from Richard — folded into
  `boats/little-jim.md` and `people/alastair-law.md`; Richard Green stays
  an inline, cross-linked mention per the people threshold (four
  messages, still no boat or build of his own). A cross-posted "LED
  array navigation lights?" thread — a new one-off correspondent, Susan
  Davis, asking about a solar/battery-powered LED nav-light setup —
  drew David LeBlanc's cross-posting complaint and kerosene-lamp
  counter-argument, Larry Whited's pointer to Dave Bolduc's
  microcruising.com supplier review, and Smith's detailed LED rebuttal
  (including a direct challenge to the Pardeys' published advice and a
  Paradox-specific COLREGs point already established in the archive) —
  folded into `operations/running-lights.md` (new section), `people/
  david-leblanc.md`, and `people/david-c-smith.md`; Susan Davis stays an inline,
  cross-linked mention per the people threshold, as she already was
  elsewhere in the bundle. The batch closed with **Kjell Karlsson**
  ("kjellkkk"), introducing himself and a decade-plus, in-progress
  Paradox variant built in Sven Yrvind's "BRIS" foam-sandwich composite
  method rather than plywood — a personal acquaintance of Yrvind since
  the mid-1970s, he learned of Matt Layden and Paradox through Yrvind in
  1998, narrowly missed meeting Matt in person during a 1999 Florida
  trip, and built a beamier, tank-less, stainless-steel-bottomed,
  carbon-rigged two-person variant with Yrvind's and (at second hand)
  Matt's blessing — the archive's first identified Swedish builder and
  its only recorded non-plywood Paradox: **promoted to new
  `people/kjell-karlsson.md` and `boats/bris-paradox.md`**, cross-linked
  from `design/blue-water-suitability.md` (the existing Yrvind/"Bris"
  design-comparison thread this build makes concrete), `design/
  variants/index.md`, and a new footnote in `matt-layden/biography.md`
  on the near-miss Matt Layden meeting. Updated `boats/index.md` and
  `people/index.md`. No prunings. While reviewing the "Odd Paradox
  control problem" thread's carryover, caught and fixed Don Elliott's
  own one-line correction (15→30 degrees) directly in `design/rudder.md`
  and `people/don-elliott.md` rather than leaving the two figures in
  tension. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the nine files that received new citations (`design/rudder.md`
  `[19]`-`[21]`, `people/don-elliott.md` `[10d]`, `boats/little-jim.md`
  `[72]`-`[73]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[106]`, `operations/
  running-lights.md` `[7]`-`[10]`, `people/david-leblanc.md` `[45]`,
  `people/david-c-smith.md` `[18]`, `matt-layden/biography.md` `[42]`,
  `boats/bris-paradox.md` `[1]` [new file], `people/kjell-karlsson.md`
  `[1]` [new file]) — the pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell/*`
  collision history is unchanged (Glen did not post this batch).
  Checked both standing unconfirmed-identity Open items ("Jeff
  UK"/`roverticket` and "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): neither resurfaced
  in this batch, both remain open. The below-threshold "Larry"
  (`newspipetr3`) also did not resurface.

* **Batch 101 (pages 1001–1010, msgnum 1017–1026, 13–17 June 2003) — the
  first normal batch after the post-1000 holistic review; a light,
  mostly maintenance/follow-up stretch closing out several standing
  loose ends plus a new "Larry" wanting to buy a second Paradox.** Read
  all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  1001–1010 mapped to **1017, 1018, 1019, 1020, 1021, 1022, 1023, 1024,
  1025, 1026** — a clean, fully consecutive run with **no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 100's 1016 — the sixteenth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last seventeen. Dates ran 13–17 June 2003.
  Content opened with Alastair closing out two standing questions from
  the "More photos" thread: David Beard's transom-port question
  (no leak risk — the port sits outside the baffle and drains
  overboard) and a new one-off newcomer's (Jim Goeckermann) leaking-
  inspection-plates/build-hours question (decent O-ring covers rather
  than teflon tape; his own 18-month build, guessed at a year for a
  repeat or a couple of months for an experienced full-time builder) —
  folded into `boats/little-jim.md` (two new sections),
  `construction/transom-deck-plate.md` (two new sections), and
  `construction/build-effort.md` (new "Time" data point). A "Paradox
  for sale??" thread introduced a new, UK-connected correspondent,
  **"Larry"** (`newspipetr3`/`rumtub`) — explicitly distinct from the
  already-established American Larry Whited (`lwhited`/`LJGeib`) — who
  had just met Alastair's "little Jim" in person and wanted to buy a
  second, already-built Paradox in addition to building his own; drew
  only joking wordplay replies ("paradoxen" from David LeBlanc,
  "Herdaoxen" from Nels), no substantive help — folded into
  `operations/secondhand-market.md` (new section), `people/david-
  leblanc.md`, and `people/nels.md`. The same "Larry" reappeared two
  days later, met in person again when Alastair took his son out for a
  2-up day sail on Poole Harbour — folded into `boats/little-jim.md`
  (new section) and `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md` (new two-up data
  point), and drew the first substantive design-verdict question from
  a new one-off admirer, Richard Green (previously seen only thanking
  Alastair for his website in batch 100). Alastair also opened a new,
  still-unexplained "Odd Paradox control problem" thread — a sudden,
  total, temporary loss of rudder effect mid-tack in a disturbed sea —
  which drew a light-air-tacking-trouble/rudder-up account from David
  Beard and a detailed seven-point tacking-technique checklist from
  **Don Elliott**, resurfacing again after his January 2003
  reappearance; folded into `boats/little-jim.md` (new section),
  `design/rudder.md` (new section), `people/david-beard.md`, and
  `people/don-elliott.md` (new section). No new files created, no
  promotions or prunings — "Larry" (`newspipetr3`), Richard Green, and
  Jim Goeckermann all kept as inline, cross-linked mentions per the
  people threshold, consistent with `people/index.md`'s existing
  practice of listing only recurring/promoted names. Checked both
  standing unconfirmed-identity Open items ("Jeff UK"/`roverticket` and
  "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): neither resurfaced in this batch, both
  remain open. **While verifying citation numbering, caught and fixed
  a self-introduced collision in `people/nels.md`** — this batch's new
  citation was initially numbered `[18]`, unnoticed-duplicating a
  pre-existing `[18]` (page_878, msgnum 893, the "beer and pizza
  America's Cup" quip) located at the very end of the file, past the
  point read during the batch's initial pass; renumbered the new
  citation to `[19]` before finalizing. Verified no other new
  citation-number collisions were introduced across the eleven files
  that received new citations (`boats/little-jim.md` `[65]`-`[71]`,
  `design/rudder.md` `[16]`-`[18]`, `construction/transom-deck-
  plate.md` `[15]`-`[16]`, `construction/build-effort.md` `[10]`,
  `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md` `[26]`, `operations/secondhand-
  market.md` `[20]`-`[22]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[105]`, `people/
  david-beard.md` `[139]`, `people/david-leblanc.md` `[44]`,
  `people/nels.md` `[19]`, `people/don-elliott.md` `[10c]`) — the
  pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md` collision areas remain
  unchanged (Glen did not post at all in this batch) and still await
  the deferred post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **RESTRUCTURE (post-1000 holistic tree review, Pass R2) — lower-risk
  cleanups across the tree.** Executed as a dedicated single-writer
  restructure pass following Pass R1 (glen-maxwell.md split, below).
  **(A)** `matt-layden/index.md`: removed its `# Citations` section and
  "Big Cruiser" open-question section — it had been the only index file
  in the bundle carrying citations/open-questions rather than pure
  nav/summary — and added the missing "Balboa 20" interlude to its
  design-lineage one-liner so it matches `biography.md`'s fuller lineage.
  Folded "Big Cruiser" (an unconfirmed February 2003 one-off mention of a
  Layden design name, never described or resurfaced across ~1600 messages
  of runway) to a single cited sentence under a new "Asides and
  footnotes" heading in `matt-layden/biography.md` (citation `[41]`); no
  stub file created, since it was never promoted. **(B)** `bundle/paradox.md`:
  added the **165° self-righting** figure (now corroborated by two
  independent sources) to the ballast/stability bullet, and added a
  furling clause to the rig bullet covering the **rolling-boom furling
  system** — worked from the cabin, with the topping-lift fix as the
  field-proven solution and Matt Layden's own foot-pressure technique —
  the archive's single most-discussed mechanical feature, previously
  absent from the trunk. **(C)** Merged the tiny, single-source,
  single-citation Chris Curtis pair: `boats/curtis-paradox.md` kept as the
  canonical boat-primary home (absorbed the "Sandpoint, Idaho" location
  detail from the person page), `people/chris-curtis.md` reduced to a
  one-line redirect stub (not deleted, for inbound links); updated
  `people/index.md` and `boats/index.md` descriptions. **(D)** Consolidated
  three thin `references/` link-list files into their natural homes:
  `general-boatbuilding-links.md` and `west-system-resources.md` folded
  into `materials-sourcing-links.md` (which now covers epoxy/fiberglass
  sourcing, general boatbuilding community links, and West System
  pointers as three sections); `smallboats-group.md` folded into
  `mailing-list.md`'s new "Sibling and successor groups" section
  alongside the existing Micro Cruising group reference. All three source
  files reduced to one-line stubs; `references/index.md` updated.
  **(E)** Folded `operations/newcomer-faq-comfort-and-safety.md` (six
  unanswered newcomer questions) into `operations/first-boat-suitability.md`
  as an "open newcomer questions" subsection, and
  `construction/bulkhead-3-cleats-question.md` into
  `construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md` as a subsection; both source
  files reduced to stubs; `operations/index.md` and `construction/index.md`
  updated. **(F)** Added a one-line "two independent Australian builds"
  see-also grouping to `boats/index.md`, linking `walden.md` (Chris
  Becker) and `aussie-paradox.md` (Andrew Graham) as a discoverability
  aid only — the two builds remain otherwise unconnected. **(G)** Backlog
  updates in `.okf-progress/backlog.md`: added a new standing
  "narrate once, cross-link" rule (boat pages carry the full
  build/cruise narrative; person pages get a short summary + link) to
  prevent the confirmed little-jim/alastair, whisper/david-beard,
  zoe/glen-maxwell, and walden/chris-becker duplication from compounding;
  added a new Open item, "post-1000 review — deferred follow-up cleanup,"
  listing lower-priority, do-when-convenient items this review
  deliberately deferred (retrofitting the four duplicated pairs to the
  new convention; an optional `little-jim.md` split; an optional
  `stretched-paradox.md` split; two minor category calls; one
  duplicated-retelling trim); moved "Big Cruiser" and the "Holistic
  review checkpoint (~post 1000)" Open items to Resolved, dated
  2026-07-03; left the two unconfirmed-identity Open items ("Jeff
  UK"/`roverticket` and "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel) unchanged, the review
  having confirmed the existing keep-separate discipline is correct.
  **Verification:** re-read every edited/created file after writing to
  confirm no sourced fact was dropped in any merge — each merge target's
  new prose was checked line-by-line against the original source file's
  content and citations before the source was reduced to a stub; citation
  numbering was kept internally consistent within every touched file (new
  citations appended after the highest existing number in that file, no
  renumbering of untouched citations). **Next holistic review: ~post
  2000**, per the new Resolved backlog entry.

* **RESTRUCTURE (post-1000 holistic tree review, Pass R1) —
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` renumbered and split into a role-based
  folder.** Executed as a dedicated single-writer restructure pass, not
  a content batch. Glen C. Maxwell's file had grown to ~1954 lines (≈2×
  the next-largest file) across ~30 append batches, carrying two known
  duplicate-citation-number collisions (`[71]` page_592 vs page_420,
  `[72]` page_599 vs page_425, `[84]` page_476 vs page_470 — flagged
  since batch 63 — plus a separate `[172]` page_942 vs page_940
  duplicate flagged by batch 99) and covering three distinguishable
  roles. **Split into a hub + three role-based sub-files:**
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` now a short index/summary page (bio + "In
  detail" navigation, no citations of its own, exactly like every other
  index/hub file in the bundle) at 40 lines, preserving the ~90 files'
  worth of existing inbound links to that path; `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md`
  (1608 lines, 148 citations) — his own build/"Zoë" launch/sailing/trip
  reports and the construction/painting/rot/epoxy/rigging Q&A given as a
  fellow builder; `people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md`
  (239 lines, 22 citations) — founding the group, the recurring
  "where to buy plans" FAQ, welcome messages, the six-occurrence
  tripod-link spam saga, and recruiting volunteer co-moderators (Dave
  Bolduc, Paul V., David LeBlanc); `people/glen-maxwell/matt-layden-eyewitness.md`
  (192 lines, 14 citations) — his firsthand, in-person Matt Layden
  material: the Ft. Desoto sail-alongside before the 2003 Everglades
  Challenge, the haul-out-roller sketch, a tacking-speed asymmetry
  discovered while sailing with Matt, race-relay posts, the Cedar Key
  Messabout meeting (Matt's foot-pressure furling technique revealed),
  and welcoming Matt Layden's own first-ever list post — kept clean and
  prominent as primary-goal content. **Citation collisions fixed by
  construction, not patched:** each of the three sub-files' citations
  were renumbered from scratch, `[1]`..`[n]` in first-appearance order,
  discarding the old global numbering entirely — so the old
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]`/`[172]` collisions simply do not exist in the new
  files. Verified: 148+22+14 = **184 total citations across the three
  sub-files, matching the original file's 184 bibliography entries
  exactly** (179 numbered + `[42b]` + the four duplicate-number extras)
  — confirming no content was lost or duplicated; every sub-file's
  citation numbers are sequential with no duplicates (spot-checked one
  transcription duplicate introduced mid-rewrite — page_369/msgnum 367
  briefly appeared as both `[62]` and `[125]` in `build-and-zoe.md` —
  caught and corrected before finishing). This file never used inline
  `[nn]` prose markers (confirmed against the same convention in
  `people/alastair-law.md`), so no inline-marker rewriting was needed beyond
  two "see citation `[nn]` above" back-references (old `[86]`, `[68]`),
  which were converted to descriptive prose links. **Inbound links (~90
  referencing files):** plain links to `/people/glen-maxwell.md` (no
  anchor) were left as-is — they correctly resolve to the hub. Anchor
  links to sections that moved into a sub-file were repointed: five
  "spammed pictures thread" anchors (`references/mailing-list.md`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md`, `construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md`,
  `people/paul-v.md` ×2) now point to
  `people/glen-maxwell/list-founder-and-moderator.md#...`; nine
  "Building encouragement" / seat-cushion / CE-CLR anchors
  (`boats/zoe.md` ×2, `design/chine-runners-and-windward-performance.md`,
  `operations/cabin-heating.md`, `construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md`,
  `design/furling-system.md`, `operations/sailing-characteristics.md`
  ×2, `people/paul-v.md`, `operations/trailering.md`) now point to
  `people/glen-maxwell/build-and-zoe.md#...`. Three anchor references
  inside this log file's own historical entries (batches 90, 96, and
  others referencing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]`/rope-size/layout-reversed
  anchors) were deliberately left untouched, per the review's own
  instructions not to edit historical log prose. **Matt Layden
  cross-links added:** `matt-layden/biography.md` — a pointer to the
  consolidated eyewitness record added right after the "2003 Everglades
  Challenge: an eyewitness account" heading, and a second pointer at the
  Cedar Key Messabout meeting section; `matt-layden/index.md` — a "See
  also" sentence appended to the Matt Layden biography bullet pointing to
  the new eyewitness sub-page as "the archive's single richest
  primary-source record of the designer himself." **Updated
  `people/index.md`'s** Glen Maxwell entry to describe the new
  hub+three-sub-file structure instead of restating the full inline
  chronology. **Verification:** grepped the entire bundle for
  `glen-maxwell` in all forms; confirmed no live-content anchor points
  at a nonexistent heading (checked each of the five moved-anchor
  targets against the sub-files' actual `##` headings); confirmed the
  hub file and all three sub-files exist at their expected paths; ran a
  citation-count reconciliation (184 = 184) as the content-completeness
  check in lieu of a line-by-line diff, given the scale of the
  rewrite. Updated `.okf-progress/backlog.md`: marked the "Duplicate
  citation numbers in `people/glen-maxwell.md`" Open item RESOLVED.
  Did not touch `.okf-progress/cursor.txt` or `.okf-progress/batches.log`
  (orchestrator-owned, not part of this review pass).

* **Batch 100 (pages 991–1000, msgnum 1007–1016, 11–13 June 2003) — a
  thin, mostly-maintenance batch: an interior photo gallery, a
  water-tank-leak fix, and two new one-off newcomers; the last content
  batch before the post-1000 holistic tree review.** Read all ten
  source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  991–1000 mapped to **1007, 1008, 1009, 1010, 1011, 1012, 1013, 1014,
  1015, 1016** — a clean, fully consecutive run with **no gaps**,
  continuing directly from batch 99's 1006 — the fifteenth gap-free
  ten-page batch of the last sixteen. Dates ran 11–13 June 2003.
  Content opened with the tail of a `microcruising`-list-crossposted
  "Anchor Rodes" thread (cc'd to `paradoxbuilders`): Glen Maxwell's own
  stern-set/walk-forward anchoring routine, braided-nylon-rode
  preference, and a rode-bag-lost-overboard-at-a-pier anecdote, and
  David C. Smith's own out-loud reasoning on stowing rode aboard a Paradox's
  cockpit-less layout (stern rollers rejected as too heavy, a
  webbing-reel-in-a-rig-hoop idea left unsettled) — folded into
  `operations/ground-tackle.md` (new section), `people/glen-maxwell.md`,
  and `people/david-c-smith.md`. Alastair then posted the interior photos of
  "little Jim" that David Beard had asked for back in May, drawing
  Glen Maxwell's fuller water-tank-leak dowel-handle fix (extending his
  original January 2002 tip), Alastair's own Vaseline-sealed
  workaround, a new one-off admirer (**Richard Green**, `DrBulldogge`),
  and David Beard's own follow-up transom-port question (left
  unanswered) — folded into `boats/little-jim.md` (new section),
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, and
