  `construction/transom-deck-plate.md` (cross-linked). The batch closed
  with a new one-off correspondent, **Jim Goeckermann**
  (`jgoeckermann`), asking about sealing leaking inspection plates and
  build-hours for a "workboat"-finish hull; David C. Smith answered the sealing
  question with a glycerin-lubrication and Channellock-pliers-leverage
  O-ring trick from his own professional watermaker-membrane
  experience (the build-hours question went unanswered) — folded into
  `construction/transom-deck-plate.md` (new section) and `people/david-c-smith.md`.
  No new files created; Greg Blanchette, Richard Green, and Jim
  Goeckermann all kept as inline, cross-linked one-off mentions per the
  people threshold. Checked all three standing identity/naming Open
  items ("Big Cruiser," "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): none
  resurfaced. **Confirmed the post-1000 holistic-review trigger,
  already met as of batch 99, remains met** — this batch's own msgnum
  range (1007-1016) is entirely past 1000, and the batch itself was
  read as the last content batch before that review, per the
  orchestrator's plan. Verified no new citation-number collisions were
  introduced across the five files that received new citations
  (`operations/ground-tackle.md` `[15]`-`[16]`, `boats/little-jim.md`
  `[60]`-`[64]`, `construction/transom-deck-plate.md` `[13]`-`[14]`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[178]`-`[179]`, `people/david-c-smith.md`
  `[14]`-`[17]`, `people/david-beard.md` `[137]`-`[138]`) — the
  pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md` collision areas (`[71]`/`[72]`/
  `[84]` and the separate `[172]` duplicate) are unchanged and remain
  queued for the post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **Batch 99 (pages 981–990, msgnum 997–1006, 1–10 June 2003) — the list
  crossed its own 1,000th posted message, a "Foam Cushions" cabin-bedding
  thread, and a substantial new Australian builder.** Read all ten source
  `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every
  msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 981–990 mapped to
  **997, 998, 999, 1000, 1001, 1002, 1003, 1004, 1005, 1006** — a clean,
  fully consecutive run with **no gaps**, continuing directly from batch
  98's 996 — the fourteenth gap-free ten-page batch of the last fifteen.
  Dates ran 1–10 June 2003. Content opened with the tail of batch 98's
  Portland-cruise reply thread (Glen Maxwell's one-line "Great story
  Al"), then Glen opened a new **"Foam Cushions"** thread giving the
  archive's fullest cabin-bedding technique yet — a foam-by-mail source
  tested at the Cedar Key Messabout, cut to cover the entire cabin sole —
  which drew a Therm-a-Rest/Sunbrella-covered-inner-tube alternative from
  David C. Smith; in between his two replies in that thread, **Smith's own post
  happened to be the `paradoxbuilders` list's 1,000th**, which he noted
  with a one-line "Hey my post is number 1000!" aside. Days later, Smith
  reported his own plywood purchase for his in-progress build (Joubert
  Okoume BS-1088, Honduras Mahogany lumber, West System epoxy) — folded
  into `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` as a new,
  concrete data point alongside the existing general debate. The batch
  closed with a substantial new introduction: **Andrew Graham**
  (`amgraham2001`), Brisbane, Australia, posting as **"Aussie Paradox"**
  — the archive's second identified Australian builder after Chris
  Becker's "Walden" — a year into holding the plans, having first built
  a covered patio and shed, with frames already cut and set up and
  several construction deviations explicitly flagged as his own
  unapproved changes (a single-sheet frame/transom layout suited to
  Australian 2400x1200mm plywood, a widened transom, omitted mid bins);
  welcomed by Glen Maxwell and Alastair, the latter answering two of his
  questions (bin removability, water-tank necessity). Judged Andrew's
  introduction substantial enough (real name, location, detailed
  construction choices, a photo gallery, welcomed by two established
  members) to clear the boats/people threshold on the same basis as
  Chris Becker and Smith — **promoted to new `people/andrew-graham.md` and
  `boats/aussie-paradox.md`**, cross-linked from `boats/index.md` and
  `people/index.md`. Folded the rest into `boats/little-jim.md` (two new
  sections: the Foam Cushions thread and the 1,000th-post aside),
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` (two new sections), `people/david-c-smith.md` (two new
  sections), and `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`
  (one new section). No prunings. While adding new citations to
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, **discovered a pre-existing citation
  collision** (a duplicate `[172]`, from two different March/April-2003
  batches' additions, already present before this batch and unrelated to
  the standing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision item) — left both instances
  in place with an inline note rather than renumbering mid-batch, and
  flagged it in `.okf-progress/backlog.md` for the post-1000 renumbering
  pass; assigned this batch's own three new Glen Maxwell citations
  `[175]`-`[177]`, safely above the file's highest pre-existing number,
  to avoid compounding the problem. Checked all three standing
  identity/naming Open items ("Big Cruiser," "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/
  Ernie Fossel): none resurfaced in this batch. Confirmed the
  holistic-review item's trigger (post 1000) is essentially **now
  met** — this batch's own msgnum range crossed **1000** exactly, and we
  are at page 990, one batch away from the 991–1000 archive-page
  milestone — the orchestrator should run the post-1000 holistic review
  starting with (or immediately after) batch 100. Verified no new
  citation-number collisions were introduced across the six files that
  received new citations (`boats/little-jim.md` `[54]`-`[59]`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[175]`-`[177]`, `people/david-c-smith.md`
  `[10]`-`[13]`, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`
  `[21]`, `people/andrew-graham.md` `[1]`-`[3]` [new file],
  `boats/aussie-paradox.md` `[1]`-`[3]` [new file]).

* **Batch 98 (pages 971–980, msgnum 987–996, 28 May – 1 June 2003) — the
  long-outstanding Portland cruise write-up finally arrived.** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  971–980 mapped to **987, 988, 989, 990, 991, 992, 993, 994, 995,
  996** — a clean, fully consecutive run with **no gaps**, continuing
  directly from batch 97's 986. Dates ran 28 May – 1 June 2003, entirely
  one continuous reply thread ("Re: Further adventures of Little Jim
  (long)"). This batch delivered exactly the report batch 97 had flagged
  as outstanding: on 28 May, Alastair posted **"the landmark voyage of
  Little Jim,"** the archive's fullest single cruising narrative to
  date — the full 27-nm-each-way Poole Harbour to Portland Harbour
  round trip, including a genuine tidal-race transit past **St Albans
  Head** (green water aboard, no danger, no leak through the hatch), a
  rain-shower fogging test that doubled as a live demonstration of
  Paradox's shoal-draft collision-avoidance trick, a chance shore
  meeting with "a friend of **Jeff UK**" in Portland, and overtaking a
  Drascombe Lugger under sail — closing "We did it!... What a great
  boat, thanks Matt." The write-up drew the archive's longest reply
  thread yet, running through 1 June: David Beard's anti-fog-cleaner
  tip and a second cabin fan/exhaust-port idea; a concrete
  outboard-weight/trim comparison (a 65 lb motor unbalanceable vs. a
  27 lb 2 hp Honda that "works great"); David C. Smith's stability/chain-ferry
  questions, answered with the clearest confidence statement yet in
  "little Jim"'s offshore seaworthiness; a new one-off correspondent,
  **Neil McVicar** (Glasgow), asking about the helming position (no
  cockpit — braced in a cabin corner); and, prompted by that, a
  cabin-cushion sub-thread in which **Dave Bolduc gave the archive's
  first direct, firsthand confirmation of what Matt Layden actually
  uses for cabin comfort aboard the original Paradox** — closed-cell
  foam pads or a small vinyl-covered cushion. Folded into
  `boats/little-jim.md` (two major new sections plus the reply thread),
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`,
  `people/david-c-smith.md`, `boats/whisper.md`, `people/jeff-doryman.md`
  (cross-linked note on the friend-of-"Jeff UK" contact — no linking
  detail, identity question still open), `boats/index.md`. No new files
  created; Neil McVicar kept as an inline, cross-linked mention (one-off,
  no boat) per the people threshold. Checked all three standing
  identity/naming Open items ("Big Cruiser," "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/
  Ernie Fossel): "Jeff UK" himself did not reappear (only a friend of his,
  with no linking detail), so the Open item is updated but not resolved;
  "Big Cruiser" and "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel did not resurface.
  Confirmed the holistic-review item's trigger (post 1000) is not yet
  met — we are at page 980, 20 pages / 2 batches away, so the
  orchestrator should finalize the post-1000 review plan now. Verified
  no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the seven
  files that received new citations (`boats/little-jim.md`
  `[44]`-`[53]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[101]`-`[104]`,
  `people/david-beard.md` `[134]`-`[137]`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`
  `[64]`, `people/david-c-smith.md` `[9]`, `boats/whisper.md` `[63]`-`[65]`,
  `people/jeff-doryman.md` `[19]`).

* **Batch 97 (pages 961–970, msgnum 977–986, 23–27 May 2003) — the
  Portland cruise weather-routing exchange and its "splendid time"
  homecoming tease, plus a shared moment crediting Matt Layden's own
  "motors are a luxury" philosophy.** Read all ten source `.eml` files
  and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum from
  its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 961–970 mapped to **977, 978,
  979, 980, 981, 982, 983, 984, 985, 986** — a clean, fully consecutive
  run with **no gaps**, continuing directly from batch 96's 976 (which
  had ended with a genuine gap at 967). Dates ran 23–27 May 2003, with a
  trivial same-day ordering quirk (page_968/msgnum 984 timestamped
  17:25:24 UTC, page_969/msgnum 985 timestamped 17:12:43 UTC, both 27
  May — not a real gap or misfile, just two messages sent minutes apart
  and delivered in msgnum rather than strict wall-clock order). Content
  was the continuation of the "Further adventures of Little Jim"
  Portland-cruise thread (carried over from batch 96) plus a short new
  pair of threads. Bill Serjeant (`barnacleid`) sent Alastair a weather
  warning ahead of his planned Saturday departure (favourable SW3/4,
  but stay clear of the Portland Race), immediately corrected himself
  on the approach direction, and mentioned his own River Crouch trip in
  "Caleb"; Alastair replied he'd actually leave Sunday; Bill reported
  back from an "excellent day on the Crouch" and invited Alastair to
  visit SE England. Four days later, on 27 May, Alastair reported he
  was **back from the full 4-day trip** with "a splendid time," more
  than ever convinced Paradox suits British-climate cruising — but only
  promised a **fuller write-up "tomorrow,"** not included in this batch;
  **batch 98 should watch for that report** (did "little Jim" actually
  reach Portland, and how did she sail in open water?). Folded into
  `boats/little-jim.md` (two new sections) and `people/alastair-law.md`.
  Separately, David Beard reported back from a "Whisper" overnighter
  having deliberately left the outboard home in favor of a makeshift
  kayak-paddle yuloh, crediting **Matt Layden's own paraphrased view
  that motors are a luxury, not a crutch** — the archive's clearest
  statement yet of this philosophy, secondhand via David. The same
  weekend produced two related threads: Dave Bolduc's reply balanced
  Matt's view against his own more engine-friendly stance, called the
  yuloh "a MUST" aboard their boats, and recalled sailing two months
  alongside Matt in the Bahamas; and David's own new "Sail Trim" thread
  (a starboard-tack-only luff flutter) drew Dave Bolduc's "one tack is
  always better" lug-rig observation and, three days later, a
  contrasting report from Alastair — his own best performance and
  easiest tacking came on the *port* tack (sail against the mast),
  opposite to the pattern Dave Bolduc and Glen Maxwell's own experience
  had suggested — plus Alastair's own matching motor-free account after
  his Portland trip. Folded into `operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md`
  (new section), `operations/sailing-characteristics.md` (new section),
  `matt-layden/biography.md` (new subsection), `boats/whisper.md` (new
  section), `design/rig.md` (cross-link), and `people/david-beard.md`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`. No new files created,
  no promotions or prunings — every thread matched an existing,
  well-developed home; Bill Serjeant remains an inline mention (already
  established, cross-linked, no boat/build of his own). Checked all
  three standing identity/naming Open items ("Big Cruiser," "Jeff UK,"
  "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): **none resurfaced in this batch.**
  Confirmed the holistic-review item's trigger (post 1000) is not
  met — we are at page 970, roughly 30 pages / ~3 batches away, and the
  orchestrator should be finalizing the post-1000 review plan now; the
  pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision
  area is unchanged (Glen did not post at all in this batch). Verified
  no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the eight
  files that received new citations (`boats/little-jim.md`
  `[39]`-`[43]`, `operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md`
  `[20]`-`[22]`, `operations/sailing-characteristics.md`
  `[25]`-`[27]`, `matt-layden/biography.md` `[40]`, `boats/whisper.md`
  `[61]`-`[62]`, `people/david-beard.md` `[132]`-`[133]`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md` `[63]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[97]`-`[100]`).

* **Batch 96 (pages 951–960, msgnum 966–976, 9–21 May 2003) — the "New
  Paradox Sail" thread's warm reception tails off, and Alastair delivers
  the archive's fullest cruising account yet.** Read all ten source
  `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every
  msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 951–960 mapped to
  **966, 968, 969, 970, 971, 972, 973, 974, 975, 976** — a genuine gap
  at **msgnum 967** (no page maps to it, the first gap since batch 84's
  msgnum 854), otherwise consecutive, continuing directly from batch
  95's 965. Real date spread: 966–972 clustered on 9 May 2003 (the day
  after and two days after Matt Layden's first list post), then jumps to
  974 (20 May), 973 (19 May, filed slightly out of strict date order
  relative to 974), and 975–976 (21 May) — the archive's furthest point
  yet into **late May 2003**. Content opened with the tail of the "New
  Paradox Sail" thread batch 95 had already made its centerpiece: two
  more welcomes to Matt Layden — a correspondent signing "Jeff UK"
  (Yahoo profile `roverticket`, congratulating Matt and asking for
  Paradox photos, notable for a new wrinkle: his e-mail header gives his
  first name as "Jay," a data point that neither confirms nor resolves
  the standing "Jeff UK" identity question) and Alastair's own brief
  welcome — plus a materials-level follow-up from Dave Bolduc (switching
  Little Cruiser's next sail to the same 4.5 oz Defender Dacron Matt
  used, versus their existing 3.9 oz Sailrite cloth). Folded into
  `matt-layden/biography.md` (new subsection), `construction/sailmaking.md`
  (new subsection), `people/jeff-doryman.md` (new subsection on the "Jay"
  wrinkle), `people/dave-bolduc.md`, and `people/alastair-law.md`. The batch
  centerpiece was **Alastair's "Little Jim's third outing"** (9 May
  2003) — the fullest single cruising narrative in the archive to date:
  a two-day Poole Harbour cruise to Brownsea and Green Islands (reefed
  at a steady 30 degrees of heel, "must do that capsize test!"), a
  near-grounding on Poole's unusual double-high-water tide pattern, an
  8-mile GPS speed trial to Bournemouth Pier averaging 3.7 kn, a foul-wind
  Studland Bay ice-cream detour, and a long tidal beat home — drawing
  brief replies from Dave Bolduc and William H. Longyard. Ten days later
  (19 May) Alastair announced plans for "little Jim"'s first
  long-distance cruise, a 4-day, 27-mile-each-way round trip from Poole
  Harbour to Portland Harbour, with no outcome yet recorded — batch 97
  should watch for the report. Both folded into `boats/little-jim.md`
  (two new sections), `people/alastair-law.md` (two new sections plus a
  top-description update), and `people/william-longyard.md` (new
  section). A final, minor thread: David LeBlanc, posting under his
  established `buster38801` handle, uploaded a bare-captioned photo
  ("flood waters and shoal draft") and added another photo to the
  group's "Whisper" album — folded into `people/david-leblanc.md` (new
  section) with no further detail recorded. One bare-link classifieds
  spam message (`aimarbati`, "easiest way to sell or buy your boat")
  drew no reply and is not otherwise recorded, consistent with how prior
  unreacted one-off noise has been handled. No new files created, no
  promotions or prunings — every thread matched an existing, well-developed
  home. Checked all three standing identity/naming Open items: "Jeff UK"
  reappeared (see above) but supplied no *confirming* link, so remains
  deferred with a new, noted-but-inconclusive data point ("Jay"); "Big
  Cruiser" and "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel did not resurface. Confirmed the
  holistic-review item's trigger (post 1000) is not met — we are at page
  960, roughly 40 pages / ~4 batches away, close enough that the
  orchestrator should be actively finalizing plans for it now; the
  pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision
  area is unchanged (Glen did not post in this batch at all). Verified no
  new citation-number collisions were introduced across the nine files
  that received new citations (`matt-layden/biography.md` `[38]`-`[39]`,
  `construction/sailmaking.md` `[15]`, `people/jeff-doryman.md` `[18]`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md` `[62]`, `boats/little-jim.md` `[35]`-`[38]`,
  `people/alastair-law.md` `[94]`-`[96]`, `people/william-longyard.md` `[9]`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md` `[42]`-`[43]`).

* **Batch 95 (pages 941–950, msgnum 956–965, 5–9 May 2003) — MATT LAYDEN
  POSTS TO THE LIST FOR THE FIRST TIME.** Read all ten source `.eml`
  files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum
  from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 941–950 mapped to a
  **clean, consecutive run 956–965** (page_941→956 ... page_950→965),
  continuing directly from batch 94's 955 with **no gaps** — the
  eleventh gap-free ten-page batch in a row — spanning 5–9 May 2003 (a
  short jump from 5 to 7 to 8–9 May). Content opened with the tail of
  the "rig details" thread carried over from batch 94: Smith's own
  cautious follow-up on Matt's foot-pressure furling trick (still
  leaning toward fitting a topping lift regardless) and a tack-strop
  slack question; then Glen Maxwell, still not having seen Alastair's
  own topping-lift rig firsthand, asked him to describe it in detail —
  Alastair's answer included a genuinely new **continuous
  halyard/furling-line** refinement (a single line doing both jobs,
  leaving only a small loop of rope in the cabin), which Glen called
  "a great idea." Folded into `design/furling-system.md` (three new
  sections), `people/david-c-smith.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`, and
  `people/alastair-law.md`. **BATCH CENTERPIECE:** four days after the 2003
  Cedar Key Messabout, **Matt Layden posted to `paradoxbuilders` himself
  for the first time** — the only message from the designer's own hand
  recorded anywhere in this archive — revealing he had been quietly
  lurking for a month or two before three separate in-person
  conversations at Cedar Key (with Glen, Larry Whited, and Dave Bolduc)
  finally drew him out. He shared a complete sailmaking recipe —
  dimensions, broadseam schedule, rounds, cut tabling, pre-tensioned
  luff tape — for a **new, more weatherly sail** he had cut for the
  original Paradox in February 2003 (shortened foot, raised peak, at
  some cost to furling neatness), which he had evidently raced a month
  later at the Everglades Challenge. David Beard was the first to
  welcome him ("Welcome Mat..."); Dave Bolduc, who had recently sailed
  the boat himself, confirmed the new sail "sets very nicely"; Glen
  Maxwell archived the post to the group's files section. This
  landmark event was folded into `matt-layden/biography.md` (major new
  section), `matt-layden/index.md`, `boats/original-paradox.md` (new
  section — the new sail as a boat-level equipment fact),
  `construction/sailmaking.md` (Matt's full technical recipe, extending
  the existing Bolduc-sourced sailmaking content), `design/rig.md`
  (summary + cross-links), and `people/glen-maxwell.md`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `people/david-c-smith.md`
  (reaction sections). A final, smaller thread closed the batch: Dave
  Bolduc asked after the **West Coast Trailer Sailers**' website, which
  a friend said carried Cedar Key photos of two Paradoxes; Larry Whited
  supplied the link, and Smith confirmed the photos showed "Whisper" and
  "Zoë" together — independent, outside photographic corroboration
  (alongside the archive's own planning thread and the Bolducs'
  microcruising.com cruising log) that the messabout came off as
  planned. Folded into `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` (new
  section) and `people/dave-bolduc.md`. No new files created — every
  thread matched an existing, well-developed home despite the batch's
  outsized significance; judged Matt's own post substantial enough for
  a major new biography.md section plus a full technical page in
  sailmaking.md, but not a standalone leaf, since it slots naturally
  alongside the existing rig/furling/sailmaking architecture. No
  promotions or prunings needed — all participants already have
  pages. Checked all three standing identity/naming Open items ("Big
  Cruiser," "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): none resurfaced in
  this batch. Confirmed the holistic-review item's trigger (post 1000)
  is not met — we are at page 950, ~50 pages / ~5 batches away, now
  close enough that the orchestrator should actively plan for it; the
  pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision
  area is unchanged (this batch's three new citations, `[172]`-`[174]`,
  were appended at the file's end). Verified no new citation-number
  collisions were introduced across the ten files that received new
  citations (`design/furling-system.md` `[39]`-`[42]`, `design/rig.md`
  `[47]`-`[48]`, `construction/sailmaking.md` `[13]`-`[14]`,
  `matt-layden/biography.md` `[34]`-`[37]`, `boats/original-paradox.md`
  `[42]`-`[43]`, `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` `[27]`-`[29]`,
  `people/david-c-smith.md` `[7]`-`[8]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[93]`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[172]`-`[174]`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`
  `[61]`, `people/david-beard.md` `[131]`).

* **Batch 94 (pages 931–940, msgnum 946–955, 30 April–4 May 2003) — THE
  2003 CEDAR KEY MESSABOUT: GLEN MAXWELL MEETS MATT LAYDEN IN PERSON, AND
  A LONG-OPEN FURLING QUESTION IS ANSWERED.** Read all ten source `.eml`
  files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum
  from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 931–940 mapped to a
  **clean, consecutive run 946–955** (page_931→946 ... page_940→955),
  continuing directly from batch 93's 945 with **no gaps** — the tenth
  gap-free ten-page batch in a row — with a real date gap inside the
  sequence: 946–952 all fell on 30 April–1 May 2003, then a 3-day jump to
  953–955 on 4 May 2003, the Sunday right after the Cedar Key Messabout
  weekend (3–4 May). Content closed out two threads batch 93 had left
  open. First, the tail of "Lil Cruiser Plans": **"Jeff UK"** (Yahoo
  profile `roverticket`) gave concrete scaled-up Paradox dimensions
  (15' 10" LOA, corrected the same afternoon to a 1.15x scale factor),
  and the thread's sharpie-hull-shape aside drew a short tangent into
  Reuel Parker's sharpie-design books between Larry ("LJGeib") and a new
  one-off correspondent, **Patrick Hooker** (Alaska) — folded into
  `design/variants/stretched-paradox.md` (two new sections),
  `people/david-leblanc.md`, and a cross-linked note in
  `people/jeff-doryman.md` on the "Jeff UK" identity question (still
  unresolved — no linking detail this batch either, though a new
  circumstantial point was noted). **BATCH CENTERPIECE:** after a four-day
  gap, new correspondent Smith's own Paradox plans arrived, and he asked a
  tack-pipe deck-wear question — answered by both Alastair and Glen
  Maxwell from their own boats' matching swaged-copper-pipe fairlead
  solution. Glen's reply then delivered the long-anticipated payoff: he
  confirmed he had attended the **2003 Cedar Key Messabout (3–4 May)** as
  planned and had **talked to Matt Layden there in person** — the
  archive's second recorded firsthand Matt Layden meeting, after Ft.
  Desoto — learning and relaying Matt's own technique for furling without
  a topping lift: simple foot pressure on the furling line and halyard in
  turn, freeing both hands. This finally answers a question the archive
  had left open since Bert Vercauteren first raised it in December 2001.
  Folded into `design/rig.md` (new section), `design/furling-system.md`
  (new section resolving its own standing open question),
  `matt-layden/biography.md` (new section — a second in-person Matt
  Layden sighting), `matt-layden/index.md`, `operations/group-cruise-reports.md`
  (new section confirming the messabout went ahead and closing the loop
  on Glen's April commitment to attend), and `people/glen-maxwell.md` and
  `people/david-c-smith.md` (new sections plus top-description updates). No new
  files, promotions, or prunings — the Cedar Key content matched four
  existing homes well; Patrick Hooker stays inline per the people
  threshold. Verified no new citation-number collisions across the eight
  files touched (`design/rig.md` `[44]`–`[46]`, `design/furling-system.md`
  `[38]`, `matt-layden/biography.md` `[33]`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`
  `[172]`, `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` `[26]`, `people/david-c-smith.md`
  `[6]`, `design/variants/stretched-paradox.md` `[45]`–`[50]`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md` `[41]`).

* **Batch 93 (pages 921–930, msgnum 936–945, 25–30 April 2003) — Smith's
  FULLER INTRODUCTION AND PROMOTION; "LIL CRUISER PLANS."** Read all ten
  source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 921–930 mapped
  to a **clean, consecutive run 936–945** (page_921→936 ... page_930→945),
  continuing directly from batch 92's 935 with **no gaps** — the ninth
  gap-free ten-page batch in a row — spanning 25–30 April 2003 (a short
  gap from 25 to 28 to 29–30 April). Content was a direct continuation of
  two threads batch 92 had already opened (the "Sails," thread and "(Son
  of) Little Jim (II, the sequel)" cabin-heating/tell-tale sub-thread),
  plus one new thread. **BATCH CENTERPIECE:** new correspondent **David C. Smith**
  (`betelgeuserdude`, introduced batch 92) filled out considerably —
  answering David LeBlanc's question about his prior boat, he revealed he
  had owned and modified a 20 ft Elver canoe yawl (leeboards for a
  centerboard), tied that experience directly to his own designer-fidelity
  credo, gave the archive's fullest single statement yet on the long-running
  foam-flotation debate (judging it a builder's-discretion detail, with a
  half-joking CO2-air-bladder aside), and pressed his own still-unanswered
  gimballed-kerosene-lamp cabin-heating question — Alastair answered part
  of it (no spot above eye height; his own cabin uses LEDs) and David
  LeBlanc suggested a Welsh miner's lamp. Smith also closed out the tell-tale
  question (leech placement, cassette tape) with Glen Maxwell, who
  separately admitted he hadn't thought of stiffening his yard with carbon
  tow as Smith had suggested. Given Smith's recurring, multi-thread engagement
  across two batches (design philosophy, sail trim, cabin heating, his own
  boat history), **promoted him from an inline mention to his own
  `people/david-c-smith.md`**, cross-linked from `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `design/rig.md`, `operations/cabin-heating.md`,
  `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, and
  `people/index.md`. Separately, Dave Bolduc gave a fuller cold-weather-
  cruising account with new physical evidence (bow-wave-shaped ice cuts
  found under old paint) for Matt Layden's established Cape Cod
  wintering-aboard-Little-Cruiser story, plus a foam-in-the-windows
  condensation detail — folded into `operations/cabin-heating.md` and
  `people/dave-bolduc.md`. The batch's other new thread, "Lil Cruiser
  Plans," saw David LeBlanc admit Paradox is too small for him and ask
  about Little Cruiser plans or an enlarged Paradox instead; Dave Bolduc
  reconfirmed no real Little Cruiser plans exist, recommended a 10-20%
  Paradox stretch, and revealed Matt Layden has been asked more than once
  to draw plans for a larger sharpie but hasn't; Larry ("LJGeib")
  independently corroborated the 10% scaling rule from his own two built
  sharpies — folded into `design/variants/stretched-paradox.md` (new
  section), `matt-layden/little-cruiser.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`, and
  `people/david-leblanc.md`. A small housekeeping item: Glen Maxwell's own
  brief reply to Alastair's "Little Jim pictures" post (echoing 440ford's
  and Longyard's congratulations) was added to `boats/little-jim.md`. No
  new files needed beyond `people/david-c-smith.md`; no other promotions or
  prunings. All three standing identity/naming Open items ("Big Cruiser,"
  "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel) were checked and remain correctly
  deferred, none resurfaced. Confirmed the holistic-review item's trigger
  (post 1000) is not met — we are at page 930, roughly 70 pages / ~7
  batches away. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the eight files that received new citations (`design/rig.md`
  `[42]`-`[43]`, `boats/little-jim.md` `[34]`,
  `operations/cabin-heating.md` `[10]`-`[13]`,
  `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md` (new cross-reference
  section, no new numbered citations), `design/variants/stretched-paradox.md`
  `[42]`-`[44]`, `people/dave-bolduc.md` `[59]`-`[60]`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md` `[40]`, `people/david-c-smith.md` `[1]`-`[5]`).

* **Batch 92 (pages 911–920, msgnum 926–935, 23–24 April 2003) — "LITTLE
  JIM"'S FIRST REAL SAIL, AND THE CEDAR KEY THREAD RUNS ITS COURSE.** Read
  all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  911–920 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run 926–935** (page_911→926 ...
  page_920→935), continuing directly from batch 91's 925 with **no
  gaps** — the eighth gap-free ten-page batch in a row — all falling
  within a tight two-day window, 23–24 April 2003. This batch was almost
  entirely a direct continuation of two threads already opened in batch
  91 (the "Newbie, sails, Cedar Key Messabout" reply chain and "little
  Jim"'s launch), with a great deal of quoted-reply-chain repetition and
  relatively little genuinely new content by message count, but two solid
  new threads: **BATCH CENTERPIECE:** Alastair took "little Jim" out for
  her **first proper sail** on 23 April — "Whooee!! what a boat!" — an
  overnight from Poole Harbour to Middlebere Lake and back, over 5 kn
  (unconfirmed) at best, up to 20 degrees of heel, a course held for 2
  miles with the tiller locked, and two failed tacks; new correspondent
  **David C. Smith** (`betelgeuserdude`) replied with sheet-easing/tell-tale advice
  that likely explains the tacking trouble, plus his own unanswered
  flowerpot-safety/gimballed-kerosene-lamp cabin-heating question — folded
  into `boats/little-jim.md` (two new sections), `people/alastair-law.md` (new
  section), and `operations/cabin-heating.md` (new section). Separately,
  the Cedar Key messabout thread ran its course with practical logistics:
  David Beard asked Larry Whited about typical weather, drawing a general
  regional forecast from a new one-off correspondent, **Bill Turnbull**
  (~75 miles south of Cedar Key), then Larry's own fuller local answer
  (morning wind to 20 kt dying by afternoon, no fishing tournament that
  year, Gulfside Motel lodging, sailing his Seapearl trimaran, "hope we
  can have two paradoxes!") — folded into `operations/group-cruise-
  reports.md` (new section) and `people/david-beard.md` (new section).
  Alastair posted the promised "little Jim" launch photographs on 24
  April, drawing brief congratulations from two more one-off
  correspondents, **"440ford"** and **William H. Longyard** (already an
  established inline/threshold person, given one new short section) —
  folded into `boats/little-jim.md` and `people/william-longyard.md`.
  New one-off inline mentions this batch (Bill Turnbull, "440ford") both
  stay below the people threshold (single message, no boat, no follow-up)
  per established precedent — no promotions. No new files created, no
  restructuring needed — every thread matched an existing home well.
  Checked all three standing identity/naming Open items ("Big Cruiser,"
  "Jeff UK," "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel): **none resurfaced in this
  batch**, all remain correctly deferred. Confirmed the holistic-review
  item's trigger (post 1000) is not met — we are at page 920, roughly 80
  pages / ~8 batches away, close enough that the orchestrator should
  continue planning for it; the pre-existing `people/glen-maxwell.md`
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area is unchanged (Glen did not post in
  this batch at all — no new citations added to his file). Verified no
  new citation-number collisions were introduced across the six files
  that received new citations (`boats/little-jim.md` `[29]`-`[33]`,
  `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` `[22]`-`[25]`,
  `operations/cabin-heating.md` `[9]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[89]`-`[92]`,
  `people/david-beard.md` `[129]`-`[130]`, `people/william-longyard.md`
  `[8]`).

## 2026-07-02

* **Batch 91 (pages 901–910, msgnum 916–925, 20–22 April 2003) — TRAILER,
  BALLAST FOLLOW-UP, AND A NEW BUILDER ASKS ABOUT MATT'S SAIL.** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  901–910 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run 916–925** (page_901→916 ...
  page_910→925), continuing directly from batch 90's 915 with **no
  gaps** — the seventh gap-free ten-page batch in a row — all falling
  within a tight three-day window, 20–22 April 2003. Two short threads
  carried over directly from "Little Jim"'s launch (batch 90): **David
  Beard** asked Alastair about the launch's custom trailer, drawing a
  brief "simple design of parallel rollers" answer with pictures
  promised; and **Alastair** himself refined Larry Whited's ballast
  diagnosis with a precise follow-up calculation — "little Jim" was only
  **~20 kg (44 lb) light** of her design weight at launch — and suggested
  the launch day's **force-6 wind**, not ballast, was the more likely
  cause of her uncertain stability. Folded into `boats/little-jim.md`
  (new section), `design/weight-and-ballast.md` (new subsection),
  `people/alastair-law.md`, and `people/david-beard.md`. **BATCH
  CENTERPIECE:** a new correspondent, **David C. Smith** (`betelgeuserdude`, a
  self-described professional boatbuilder of 20 years who read the
  entire archive before posting), introduced himself under "Newbie...,"
  announced he would build his own Paradox "sistership," and endorsed
  the list's designer-fidelity ethic in his own words. Asking Glen
  Maxwell to elaborate on his sail's "need for improvement" drew the
  archive's **fullest retelling yet** of the March 2003 Ft. Desoto sail
  exchange with Matt Layden: Glen's own sail was cut **too flat**, while
  Matt's needed **more draft, not less**; the return beat saw Matt "put a
  butt kicking on me to windward"; Matt separately diagnosed a **bowing
  yard** needing a small crescent cut; and Glen confirmed **Matt makes
  his own sails**, fiddling by cutting and re-cutting — advice Glen
  planned to take up himself. Smith offered his own sailmaker's take on the
  yard-bow problem from a bad experience with his own sprit. The same
  message also confirmed **five Paradoxes now afloat** (counting "Little
  Jim") and opened planning for the **2003 Cedar Key messabout**
  (3–4 May) — Larry Whited confirmed the date and pointed to an archival
  photo site, Glen committed to attend and offered "Zoë" for test
  sailing, and David Beard asked after then withdrew a dates question on
  reading Larry's reply. Folded into `design/rig.md` (new section),
  `matt-layden/biography.md` (new section), `boats/original-paradox.md`
  (cross-reference added), `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` (new
  section), `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, and
  `people/alastair-law.md`. Checked both standing identity-caution Open items
  and the "Big Cruiser" item: none resurfaced. **Smith kept as an inline,
  cross-linked mention** (two messages, one thread, announced intent to
  build but no boat/progress yet) per the people threshold — not yet
  promoted, per precedent (Nels, botebilder/Ernie Fossel). No new files
  created, no promotions or prunings — every thread matched an existing
  home well despite fresh ground (a first new-builder introduction in
  some time, and the fullest-yet account of Matt's own sailmaking
  practice). Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the nine files that received new citations
  (`boats/little-jim.md` `[26]`-`[28]`, `design/weight-and-ballast.md`
  `[44]`, `design/rig.md` `[39]`-`[41]`, `matt-layden/biography.md`
  `[31]`-`[32]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[87]`-`[88]`,
  `people/david-beard.md` `[126]`-`[128]`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`
  `[170]`-`[171]`, `operations/group-cruise-reports.md` `[17]`-`[21]`) —
  the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` remain unchanged and untouched, still awaiting
  the post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **Batch 90 (pages 891–900, msgnum 906–915, 18–20 April 2003) — LITTLE
  JIM LAUNCHES AT EASTER.** Read all ten source `.eml` files and
  confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum from its own
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 891–900 mapped to a **clean, consecutive
  run 906–915** (page_891→906 ... page_900→915), continuing directly from
  batch 89's 905 with **no gaps** — the sixth gap-free ten-page batch in a
  row — all falling within a tight three-day window, 18–20 April 2003.
  **BATCH CENTERPIECE:** the carried-over "Little Jim" launch thread
  finally resolved — **Alastair launched "little Jim"** from Ridge Wharf
  boatyard, Poole Harbour, at 11:30 on **19 April 2003**, right around his
  own hoped-for Easter 2003 target (Easter fell on 20 April that year):
  **"It floats! It's watertight! It keeps bumping into things!!"** Cold,
  force-6 weather kept a full trial short, but Alastair reported his
  custom trailer worked well, took his first yuloh trial (bow blew off in
  a beam wind, no damage), and found the boat's stability at rest
  uncertain — prompting **Larry Whited** to diagnose a known problem
  (insufficient ballast to sink an empty Paradox to its lines), which
  **Glen Maxwell** and **David Beard** both corroborated from their own
  boats ("Zoë" sailed fine with no water aboard, though Matt Layden judged
  her "a little low"; "Whisper" could nearly dunk her mast leaning out at
  the dock). Folded into `boats/little-jim.md` (new "Launched" section),
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `design/weight-and-ballast.md` (new "unloaded
  launch" section), `boats/zoe.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`, and
  `people/david-beard.md`. The launch was set up by a short preceding
  thread in which **Dave Bolduc** posted a closer picture of the original
  Paradox's new bronze-rod-tipped rudder/yuloh and a cleat-fouling detail,
  prompting Alastair to misread a changed sheeting arrangement — corrected
  by **Glen Maxwell** (unchanged; the photo showed the prototype, the
  plans use a two-to-one purchase; the mainsheet does snag on the rudder
  head, yuloh mount, and cleats, but Matt's own fix is "very simple") —
  folded into `boats/original-paradox.md` (new section), `people/dave-bolduc.md`,
  and `people/glen-maxwell.md`. A final, separate thread: two days after
  the launch, **Dave Bolduc** revealed that he and Mindy, on their way
  home from that year's Bahamas trip, had **visited Glen Maxwell and his
  wife** — the archive's only recorded in-person meeting between two of
  its most prominent correspondents, with Glen finally seeing Little
  Cruiser firsthand — folded into `people/dave-bolduc.md`. Checked both
  standing identity-caution Open items and the "Big Cruiser" item: none
  resurfaced or gained new detail this batch; all three remain open,
  unchanged. No new files created, no promotions or prunings — every
  thread matched an existing home well. Verified no new citation-number
  collisions were introduced across the eight files that received new
  citations (`boats/little-jim.md` `[20]`-`[25]`,
  `boats/original-paradox.md` `[39]`-`[41]`, `boats/zoe.md` `[24]`,
  `design/weight-and-ballast.md` `[41]`-`[43]`, `people/alastair-law.md`
  `[85]`-`[86]`, `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[168]`-`[169]`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md` `[57]`-`[58]`, `people/david-beard.md`
  `[125]`) — the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` remain unchanged and untouched, still awaiting
  the post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **Batch 89 (pages 881–890, msgnum 896–905, 16 March – 18 April 2003) —
  AFTER THE WIN: A VISIT TO MATT AND A NEW OAR.** Read all ten source
  `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every
  msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 881–890 mapped to a
  **clean, consecutive run 896–905** (page_881→896 ... page_890→905),
  continuing directly from batch 88's 895 with **no gaps** — the fifth
  gap-free ten-page batch in a row — though real dates inside the batch
  jumped twice: 896–898 (16–20 March), then 899–901 (29–30 March), then
  902–905 (14–18 April), the archive's furthest point yet into **April
  2003**. The Everglades Challenge aftermath thread that had run
  continuously since batch 82 fell quiet this batch, replaced by the
  archive's **first post-race, in-person Matt Layden sighting**: a month
  after the win, **Dave Bolduc** revealed he and Mindy had visited Matt
  "this Spring," and that he himself got to **scull the original Paradox**,
  coached by Matt to put his whole body into the stroke. Paradox now
  carried a **new sculling oar** — straighter, with a wider, longer
  blade, more powerful but demanding much more wrist action — strong
  enough a change that **Matt's own wrist had hurt during the first few
  days of the Everglades Challenge** using it; bronze rod now reinforced
  the tips of both the new oar and the rudder. **Alastair** separately
  recalled Matt's own figure of averaging **2 knots** yuloh-sculling the
  race's calm section. Folded into `boats/original-paradox.md` (new
  section), `matt-layden/biography.md`, `matt-layden/index.md`,
  `operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md` (two new sections),
  `people/dave-bolduc.md`, and `people/alastair-law.md`. The prompting thread
  was David Beard's "Solar Panel" question — installing a proper 12-volt
  system on "Whisper" and asking about solar panels and yuloh propulsion
  speed — answered by Alastair (roof-tile solar panels) and **Paul V.**
  (a specific 5-watt product link); folded into `boats/whisper.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`, `people/paul-v.md`. Two smaller threads: David
  Beard's off-the-shelf chaise-lounger cabin-cushion fix (`boats/whisper.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`); and a "back on the (saw) horse" thread in
  which a correspondent posting as **"botebilder"** — his e-mail address
  revealing the name **Ernie Fossel** — reported his in-progress Paradox
  and boat shed destroyed by a windstorm the previous New Year's Eve,
  answered by **Alastair**'s only early-build lesson learned (don't fuss
  over cleat sizing; get the bulkhead shapes right) — folded into
  `construction/build-effort.md` (new section) and `people/alastair-law.md`.
  Checked both standing identity-caution Open items: the **"Big Cruiser"**
  name did not resurface, and **"Jeff UK"** did not reappear with any
  linking detail — both remain open. Added one new Open item: whether
  "botebilder"/Ernie Fossel is the same person as the already-established
  "Ernie" (`whittlinfool`, Sebastopol, CA) — cross-linked but not merged,
  pending a stronger link. No new files needed — every thread matched an
  existing home well despite covering fresh ground (a post-race Matt
  Layden sighting, a total-loss rebuild story, and the bundle's first
  solar/electrical-power question).

* **Batch 88 (pages 871–880, msgnum 886–895, 12–14 March 2003) — THE
  EVERGLADES CHALLENGE AFTERMATH, CONTINUED.** Read all ten source `.eml`
  files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum from
  its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 871–880 mapped to a **clean,
  consecutive run 886–895** (page_871→886 ... page_880→895), continuing
  directly from batch 87's 885 with **no gaps** — the fourth fully
  gap-free ten-page batch in a row. All ten messages fell within
  **12–14 March 2003**, directly continuing batch 87's "another race
  comment:-)" thread and its "MATT WINS!" reply-chain. **Larry Whited**
  speculated a two-person, two-rowing-station Sea Pearl crew could be
  competitive, judged the race's rules had drifted from its original form,
  and repeated his own hope to build a "+10%" enlarged Paradox "similar in
  length to Little Cruiser"; **Ron Eike** guessed at a future 15 ft
  minimum-length rule and relayed a stray WaterTribe-forum Sea Pearl
  sighting at Cape Sable; **Paul V.** wondered why no Hobie-cat catamarans
  had entered, drawing **Glen Maxwell**'s answer that a Hobie-18-hulled
  tacking proa ("Weed Warrior," the eventual runner-up) had raced instead;
  a recurring one-off, **"patowatomie,"** added that "Crazy Russian" had
  entered a catamaran of his own; and **Nels** closed the thread with a
  lighthearted "beer and pizza America's Cup" microcruiser-racing joke.
  Finally, **"Jeff UK"** (a distinct Yahoo handle/profile from the
  established "Jeff (UK, doryman_uk)," not confirmed to be the same
  person) sent an admiring, delayed congratulation from across the
  Atlantic — "What a man. What a boat. Well done Matt. Well done
  PARADOX." — the archive's only reaction to the win from outside North
  America. All folded into `boats/original-paradox.md` (two new sections,
  "More aftermath" and "A late congratulation from across the Atlantic"),
  `matt-layden/biography.md`, `people/nels.md`, `people/ron-eike.md`,
  `people/paul-v.md`, and `people/glen-maxwell.md` — no new files needed,
  every thread matched an existing home well; the race arc first opened
  in batch 82 is now, with this batch, fully wound down to ordinary list
  chatter. Checked the **"Big Cruiser"** backlog item: "patowatomie"
  reappeared in this batch but only in the unrelated race-boats
  discussion, with no mention of "Big Cruiser" itself — the item's
  trigger was **not met**, and it remains open in `matt-layden/index.md`
  and the backlog. The **holistic-review item** also remains correctly
  deferred (page 880, ~12 batches from the post-1000 checkpoint), and this
  batch never touched the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area
  in `people/glen-maxwell.md` (its one new citation, `[167]`, was appended
  at the file's end). No promotions or prunings: "Jeff UK," "patowatomie"
  (still no boat, no follow-up), Larry Whited, and Ron Eike all stay
  correctly below or at their established inline/page status. Verified no
  new citation-number collisions were introduced across the six files
  touched (`boats/original-paradox.md` `[26]`-`[34]`,
  `matt-layden/biography.md` no new citations (narrative-only addition),
  `people/nels.md` `[18]`, `people/ron-eike.md` `[10]`, `people/paul-v.md`
  `[47]`, `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[167]`).

* **Batch 87 (pages 861–870, msgnum 876–885, 11–12 March 2003) — THE
  EVERGLADES CHALLENGE FINISH.** Read all ten source `.eml` files and
  confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived every msgnum from its own
  `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 861–870 mapped to a **clean, consecutive
  run 876–885** (page_861→876 ... page_870→885), continuing directly from
  batch 86's 875 with **no gaps** — the third fully gap-free ten-page batch
  in a row. All ten messages fell within **11–12 March 2003**, the tightest
  and most eventful window yet: the **2003 WaterTribe Everglades Challenge
  reached its conclusion**, resolving the carryover thread flagged since
  batch 82. Glen Maxwell relayed Matt's Checkpoint 4 (273 miles) comment
  ("Spectacular sailing... dolphins on the bow"); Nels then posted six of
  the batch's ten messages as the archive's most active real-time
  chronicler — a "race report" on the chasing pack (Team Weed Warrior 2nd,
  9h20m back), an analysis of why Matt was winning so decisively (the
  roller-launch design, sleeping underway vs. paddlers who must land to
  rest), a genuinely new incident (Matt **stopped mid-race to rescue an
  overturned kayaker and tow him to shore**), and finally, under the
  subject **"MATT WINS!,"** the archive's own contemporaneous posting of
  the full official WaterTribe results: **finished at Key Largo, 304
  miles, 11 March 2003 at 17:51, first overall** — with nobody else having
  yet reached Checkpoint 4 — matching the win already known secondhand
  from microcruising.com. Nels also relayed a forum post recounting a
  collect call from Matt himself ("tired but in good spirits"). The
  aftermath drew a short but substantive exchange on *why* a heavy
  displacement monohull beat lighter, paddled/multihull competition: Ron
  Eike wondered why the Seapearl trimaran hadn't been more competitive;
  Larry Whited (a Seapearl owner himself) explained the heavy Paradox
  "powers through the slop with inertia" where a light multihull is
  "thrown off the waves"; Nels credited Matt's own skill as much as the
  boat and closed with "Perhaps he may have created a class of his own!
  The microcruiser class." All race content folded into
  `boats/original-paradox.md` (a substantial new "Race to the finish" /
  "MATT WINS!" / "Aftermath" set of sections with a full results table —
  judged to still belong in the existing file rather than split to its own
  leaf, since it completes rather than outgrows the existing Everglades
  Challenge narrative already there), `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `people/nels.md` (now the archive's fullest single source on the race's
  final two days), `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/alastair-law.md` (a
  one-line "must be close now" guess, correct within hours), and
  `people/ron-eike.md`, plus index touch-ups (`boats/index.md`,
  `matt-layden/index.md`, `people/index.md`). No new files needed. No
  promotions: Larry Whited stays inline per the established precedent
  (recurring, substantive on this topic, but never with his own boat/build
  content). Resolved the backlog's HIGH PRIORITY carryover item (the
  overdue Everglades Challenge result) — see `.okf-progress/backlog.md`.
  The "Big Cruiser" Open item's trigger was not met (no mention in this
  batch). Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the six files that received new citations
  (`boats/original-paradox.md` `[16]`-`[25]`, `matt-layden/biography.md`
  `[23]`-`[27]`, `people/nels.md` `[12]`-`[17]`, `people/ron-eike.md`
  `[9]`, `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[166]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[82]`).

* **Batch 86 (pages 851–860, msgnum 866–875, 9–11 March 2003).**
  Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  851–860 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run 866–875**
  (page_851→866, 852→867, 853→868, 854→869, 855→870, 856→871, 857→872,
  858→873, 859→874, 860→875), continuing directly from batch 85's 865
  with **no gaps** — the second fully gap-free ten-page batch in a row.
  All ten messages fell within a tight **9–11 March 2003** window — the
  archive's most temporally compressed batch yet — and form a single
  continuous cluster: the **2003 WaterTribe Everglades Challenge in
  progress**, plus a related sail/reefing thread sparked directly by
  Glen Maxwell's Ft. Desoto account from batch 85.
  **Race progress** (the batch's centerpiece): Paul V. relayed the
  WaterTribe results site three times — 9 March, Matt about 10 hours
  behind the paddling kayakers after Checkpoint 2 but reporting "a good
  night's sleep while sailing offshore" (bearing out his stated race
  tactic); 10 March, "Matt's in front!" — first competitor to reach
  Checkpoint 3, headed offshore. Replies followed from **Nels**
  ("Go Matt Go!") and **Larry Whited** ("a new paradigm... for the
  Watertribe and adventure racing"). No finish/result is recorded yet —
  the race was still in progress, roughly a fifth of the way through its
  ~300 miles, as of the last message read; the already-known
  first-in-class-and-overall win (from microcruising.com) remains to be
  confirmed by a contemporaneous report in a later batch.
  **The "Sails" thread**: David Beard offered to send his own "Whisper"
  sail to Dabbler alongside Glen's for a re-cut (noting Dabbler's
  ~3-month backlog), then asked whether Glen had noticed how Matt reefed
  his sail — drawing Glen's reconstruction from memory (Matt's sail
  rolling counter-clockwise) and a genuinely new discovery: sailing
  reefed alongside Matt, Glen found "Zoë" a full knot slower on
  starboard tack than port, and, weighing Matt's criticism of his sail
  shape against the Dabbler wait, floated **sewing a replacement sail
  himself** ("if we can build the boat we can build the sail").
  Separately, Alastair asked for full technical detail on Matt's solo
  haul-out rollers, drawing a hand-sketched, detailed answer from Glen
  (two sets of 6 in x 2 in boat rollers on 2x4 trestles, stowed in the
  starboard bilge). All folded into existing files
  (`boats/original-paradox.md`, `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `design/rig.md`, `design/furling-system.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `people/paul-v.md`,
  `people/nels.md`, `boats/whisper.md`) plus index touch-ups
  (`boats/index.md`, `design/index.md`, `people/index.md`,
  `matt-layden/index.md`) — no new files needed, every thread matched an
  existing home well. No promotions or prunings: Larry Whited stays
  inline per the established precedent. The "Big Cruiser" backlog item's
  trigger was not met (no mention anywhere in this batch); both standing
  Open items remain correctly deferred. Verified no new citation-number
  collisions were introduced across the nine content files touched (the
  pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`
  are unchanged, still awaiting the post-1000 renumbering pass).

* **Batch 85 (pages 841–850, msgnum 856–865, 5 February – 9 March 2003).**
  Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  841–850 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run 856–865**
  (page_841→856, 842→857, 843→858, 844→859, 845→860, 846→861, 847→862,
  848→863, 849→864, 850→865), continuing directly from batch 84's 855
  with **no gaps** — the first fully gap-free ten-page batch in several
  rounds. Real date jumps within the batch: 856–858 fell on 5–6 February
  2003 (continuing directly from batch 84), then 859–860 (13–14
  February), 861 (20 February), 862 (2 March), and 863–865 (9 March) —
  the archive has now moved into **March 2003**, squarely inside the
  window the archive has anticipated since batch 82 for Matt Layden's
  **2003 WaterTribe Everglades Challenge**.
  The batch's centerpiece is the archive's only **firsthand, in-person
  eyewitness account of Matt Layden under sail**: on 9 March 2003,
  **Glen Maxwell** reported sailing alongside Matt in the original
  Paradox at Ft. Desoto on the two days before the race start, describing
  Matt's solo haul-out rig (two rollers plus a four-part-tied-to-a-six-part
  block and tackle), Matt's stated race tactic (sleeping while underway),
  and — most notably — that Matt's windward sailing ability was **"far
  superior"** to his own, despite Glen having sailed his own Paradox for
  well over a year; Glen also relayed the race's early progress via the
  WaterTribe tracking site (Matt at Checkpoint 2, Placida FL, helping a
  fellow competitor). Folded into
  [boats/original-paradox.md](/boats/original-paradox.md)
  and cross-linked into
  [matt-layden/biography.md](/matt-layden/biography.md)
  and [people/glen-maxwell.md](/people/glen-maxwell.md). The same day,
  **Alastair** announced "[Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md)" had reached
  a major landmark — all the actual making complete, only paint and
  fitting-out left — with the archive's fullest single-build cost
  accounting (£2,751.16 boat + £1,502.12 custom trailer) and a hoped-for
  Easter 2003 launch, asking in the same message after Matt's race
  progress (which prompted Glen's report above); Glen replied with brief
  congratulations. Earlier in the batch, a "Bottom paint" thread resolved
  a previously-unanswered question in
  [construction/bottom-painting.md](/construction/bottom-painting.md):
  David Beard's year-old bottom paint should simply be sanded off, plus
  detailed 3M-respirator/PPE advice Glen and David gave a new,
  health-anxious correspondent, **Fred Klitzke** (his third batch of
  appearances, still kept inline — no boat or build of his own). David
  Beard also floated (unanswered) converting "[Whisper](/boats/whisper.md)"
  into an engine-only **"Power Sharpie"** for local rivers rather than
  selling her — the archive's only proposal to repower rather than resell
  a completed Paradox — and logged GPS motoring speeds (2 kt idle, 5 kt at
  1/4 throttle) with his 4 hp outboard, folded into
  [operations/hull-speed.md](/operations/hull-speed.md).
  All content folded into existing files
  (`construction/bottom-painting.md`, `boats/whisper.md`,
  `operations/hull-speed.md`, `boats/little-jim.md`,
  `boats/original-paradox.md`, `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `matt-layden/index.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`) plus index touch-ups — no new files needed,
  every thread matched an existing home well. No promotions: Fred Klitzke
  stays inline per the people threshold despite a third batch of
  appearances (still no boat or build of his own). The standing "Big
  Cruiser" Open item was **not** triggered — the name did not resurface in
  this batch. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the ten files touched, and reconfirmed the pre-existing
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md` are
  unchanged, still awaiting the post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **Batch 84 (pages 831–840, msgnum 845–853 + 855, 10 January – 5 February
  2003).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely
  distinct; re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header.
  Pages 831–840 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run 845–853**
  (page_831→845, 832→846, 833→847, 834→848, 835→849, 836→850, 837→851,
  838→852, 839→853), continuing directly from batch 83's 844, followed by
  a **genuine gap at msgnum 854** (no `page_NNN.eml` maps to it) before
  page_840→855 — batch 85 should keep deriving every msgnum from its own
  header rather than assuming continuity. Real date jumps within the
  batch: 845–848 fell on 10–12 January 2003 (continuing directly from
  batch 83), then a jump to 849 (19 January), 850–851 (22 January), 852–853
  (3 February), and 855 (5 February) — the archive has now moved into
  **February 2003**. Several small threads, all folded into existing
  files, no new concept files needed. **Don Elliott** resurfaced a second
  time (10 January), the day after his batch-83 reappearance, with the
  archive's fullest designer-fidelity argument against enlarging Paradox —
  width fixed by 4 ft ply sheets, lengthening alone changes displacement
  distribution, true scaling needs a full redesign with model testing —
  folded into
  [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md)
  and [people/don-elliott.md](/people/don-elliott.md). In the same message
  thread, **Larry Whited** added a second, independent contemporaneous
  mention of Matt Layden's Watertribe Everglades Challenge entry (first
  reported by Dave Bolduc in batch 82), cross-linked into
  [boats/original-paradox.md](/boats/original-paradox.md).
  **Alastair** then resolved Fred Klitzke's previously-unanswered
  motor-space/bottom-thickness follow-up questions from batch 83's plans
  FAQ (11–12 January), folded into
  [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md)
  and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md); Fred, replying, revealed
  Dave Bolduc had gone unresponsive to his own contact attempt. A new
  one-off correspondent asked (unanswered, cross-posted to `microcruising`)
  whether UHMW polyethylene sheet could sheath a Paradox's bottom, folded
  into
  [Rudder and chine experiments](/components/rudder.md).
  A new prospective builder's basic rig question (does the sail rub the
  mast, is the mast centered) was answered plainly by Alastair — yes on
  starboard tack, and no, the mast is offset to port — folded into
  [Rig and sail](/components/rig/index.md).
  A new one-off correspondent asked about a Matt Layden design named **"Big
  Cruiser,"** unknown anywhere else in the archive or in the ingested
  microcruising.com material; Harald Lillevang pointed to sketches in the
  microcruising group's files section without confirming the name — recorded
  as an open, unresolved question rather than an established design, in
  [matt-layden/index.md](/matt-layden/index.md).
  Finally, David Beard asked (unanswered) how to safely strip a year-old
  coat of bottom paint from "Whisper" before adding more glass, folded into
  [Bottom painting over epoxy](/construction/bottom-painting.md),
  [boats/whisper.md](/boats/whisper.md), and
  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md). No promotions: all
  one-off posters this batch (Fred Klitzke, Larry Whited, "Rake993"/
  "quent_r", "wolgram123"/Richard Wolgram, "patowatomie") stay inline per
  the people threshold — none introduced a boat, a sustained thread, or a
  follow-up commitment. Confirmed both standing backlog Open items remain
  correctly deferred: the holistic-review trigger (post 1000) is not yet
  met (page 840, ~16 batches out), and this batch never touched
  `people/glen-maxwell.md` at all (Glen does not appear as a poster in
  pages 831–840), so the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area
  is unchanged. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the eleven files touched
  ([references/building-plans.md](/references/building-plans.md),
  [people/don-elliott.md](/people/don-elliott.md),
  [boats/original-paradox.md](/boats/original-paradox.md),

  [matt-layden/index.md](/matt-layden/index.md),

  [people/david-beard.md](/people/david-beard.md)), plus light index

  `boats/index.md`, `references/index.md`, and `people/index.md`.

* **Batch 83 (pages 821–830, msgnum 835–844, 9–10 January 2003).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 821–830 mapped to
  a **clean, consecutive run (835–844)** — page_821→835, 822→836, 823→837,
  824→838, 825→839, 826→840, 827→841, 828→842, 829→843, 830→844 — with no
  missing msgnums and no gaps, continuing directly from batch 82's 834,
  entirely within one day (9–10 January 2003, nine of ten on 10 January).
  Two threads, both continuations of ones batch 82 had already opened, no
  genuinely new concepts needed. **(1) "Scaling Paradox and Other
  Questions" continued** — Larry Whited pointed the newcomer to Jeff (UK)'s
  own "Paramour" scaling work; Mat ("mat_man22") gave a more technical
  answer (±10% length-only rule of thumb; Lars Larsson's *Principles of
  Yacht Design* formula for larger scale-ups; a caution against too much
  beam for chine runners; a dory-vs-ballasted-sharpie seaworthiness
  aside, with Jay Benford's 26 ft bilge-keeled dory as an example) and
  separately linked Benford's new "HAPPY" 14 ft design; and **Alastair**
  closed the thread with the archive's established 10% scaling answer plus
  a notable corroboration — he recalled a graph "with the plans or with
  Dons articles" showing the design self-rights from 165° of heel, the
  archive's first record of this figure (previously known only from a
  later microcruising.com interview) appearing on a builder's own plans
  paperwork. Folded into
  [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md),
  with the self-righting corroboration cross-linked into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md),
  [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md), and
  [people/mat-man22.md](/people/mat-man22.md). **(2) The "buy Paradox
  plans" FAQ's fifteenth recurrence** — a new, tools-hesitant correspondent,
  Fred Klitzke, drew the archive's fullest reassurance yet for this
  recurring question: Glen Maxwell gave the familiar Dave-Bolduc/$35
  pointer (with a new caveat — the Bolducs would soon be away sailing for
  months) and Alastair gave a detailed modest-tools/bodger-not-woodworker
  pep talk plus a pointer to his own "Little Jim" photo gallery, which
  Fred found impressive but "very complex," asking unanswered follow-up
  questions about motor space and the bottom's thickness. A short,
  separate tangent (Fred's Cape Dory Typhoon mention, Paul V.'s Tanzer 28/
  Annie-Hill-book aside replying to Mat's Benford-dory reference) was
  folded into the scaling thread instead, since it replied there. Folded
  into
  [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md)
  and [people/alastair-law.md](/people/alastair-law.md). No new concepts/files
  needed — both threads matched existing homes well. No promotions: Fred
  Klitzke stays inline (three messages, one thread, no boat, no follow-up
  commitment) per the people threshold, the same treatment as every other
  one-off newcomer in this FAQ's long history. Confirmed both standing
  backlog Open items remain correctly deferred: the holistic-review
  trigger (post 1000) is not met (still ~170 pages / ~17 batches away),
  and this batch never touched `people/glen-maxwell.md` at all (Glen does
  not appear as a poster in pages 821–830), so the pre-existing
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area is unchanged. Verified no new
  citation-number collisions were introduced across the five files with
  new citations (`design/variants/stretched-paradox.md` `[37]`-`[41]`,
  `design/weight-and-ballast.md` `[40]`, `references/building-plans.md`
  `[29]`-`[33]`, `people/alastair-law.md` `[76]`-`[77]`,
  `people/mat-man22.md` `[8]`-`[9]`).

* **Batch 82 (pages 811–820, msgnum 825–834, 8–10 January 2003).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header and cross-checked
  UTC-normalized `Date:`/`Received:` order against msgnum order — all
  agreed exactly. Pages 811–820 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run
  (825–834)** — page_811→825, 812→826, 813→827, 814→828, 815→829,
  816→830, 817→831, 818→832, 819→833, 820→834 — with no missing msgnums
  and no gaps, continuing directly from batch 81's 824, entirely within a
  tight three-day window (8–10 January 2003). Two threads. **(1) The
  "How heavy is a fully loaded and ballasted Paradox?" thread's tail**
  (8–9 Jan) — a new one-off correspondent, Mary Jack, tried to referee
  the weight-vs-displacement physics debate with a liveweight/deadweight
  terminology framing; Larry ("LJGeib") followed up with citations to two
  outside physics sources on Archimedes' principle; the debate ended
  without either side conceding. Separately, Dave Bolduc gave his own
  1,400 lb Paradox displacement figure plus a fifth weight data point
  (Little Cruiser's own 1,000–1,100 lb partial / ~1,600 lb loaded
  trailering weights), and — the batch's most notable Matt Layden content —
  reported, in the archive's own **contemporaneous record**, that Matt had
  entered the original Paradox in the **2003 WaterTribe Everglades
  Challenge** (Fort Desoto–Key Largo, ~260 mi), a race the archive
  elsewhere (via microcruising.com) already records Matt won first in
  class and overall; Ron Eike asked how Matt would satisfy the race's
  above-high-tide-line launch rule, and Paul V. (as "sohaven2002")
  wondered about shallow-water shortcuts. Folded into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md)
  (main record, extended), [original Paradox](/boats/original-paradox.md)
  (Everglades Challenge corroboration), [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md),
  [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md), [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md),
  and [Ron Eike](/people/ron-eike.md). **(2) Two smaller threads**: a
  14th recurrence of the "where to buy plans" FAQ from a first-ever
  Italian correspondent, answered by **Don Elliott** — his first recorded
  post since resigning from the list in October 2001 over the
  foam-flotation dispute, restating his own "Building the Paradox"
  booklet offer at unchanged terms — folded into
  [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md),
  [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md), and
  [Building the Paradox articles](/references/building-the-paradox-articles.md);
  and a newcomer's "Scaling Paradox and Other Questions" (18–20 ft
  enlargement plus a revived bilge-keel/self-righting question), answered
  by Paul V. with pointers to four larger sharpie designs and a belief
  that chine runners would work at larger scale, though unseen in
  practice — folded into
  [Stretched Paradox](/design/variants/stretched-paradox.md)
  and [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md). No new concepts/files needed — all
  threads matched existing homes well. No promotions: Mary Jack,
  "trabuccofubacco," and "servo_bot" all stay inline (one message each,
  no boat, no follow-up) per the people threshold. Verified no new
  citation-number collisions were introduced across the ten files touched
  (caught and fixed one near-miss in `people/dave-bolduc.md`, where two
  new citations were initially misnumbered `[42]`/`[43]`, colliding with
  that file's own pre-existing `[42]`/`[43]`; renumbered to `[52]`/`[53]`
  before finalizing).

* **Batch 81 (pages 801–810, msgnum 815–824, 6–8 January 2003).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header and cross-checked
  UTC-normalized `Date:` order against msgnum order and `In-Reply-To`
  chains — all agreed exactly. Pages 801–810 mapped to a **clean,
  consecutive run (815–824)** — page_801→815, 802→816, 803→817, 804→818,
  805→819, 806→820, 807→821, 808→822, 809→823, 810→824 — with no missing
  msgnums and no gaps, entirely within a tight three-day window (6–8
  January 2003, continuing directly from batch 80's 6 January start). A
  light batch, effectively two threads. **(1) A one-line coda to "Build
  Book"** (6 Jan) — Nels replied to Paul V.'s bulkhead-cutting update with
  an unrelated pointer to a much-larger boat's hull-building weblog
  (`jboats.weblogger.com`), not followed up — folded into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md),
  [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md), and [Nels](/people/nels.md). **(2) "How
  heavy is a fully loaded and ballasted Paradox?"** (7–8 Jan) — the
  batch's main content: Paul V. asked the list a fully loaded Paradox's
  weight; David LeBlanc answered 1,400#; Alastair gave two figures —
  1,400 lb **design displacement**, and his own "Little Jim," weighed for
  the first time with her **hull finished and glassed** and cabin work
  just starting, at **just under 600 lb** (the archive's only weigh-in at
  this exact build stage, and a fourth independent "Zoë"/Paradox weight
  data point alongside Don Elliott's and Glen Maxwell's earlier figures);
  this touched off the archive's fullest **weight-vs-displacement physics
  debate**, David LeBlanc arguing the two are distinct quantities (a
  boat "can displace more than she weighs") against "LJGeib" (Larry, the
  same correspondent from the earlier ballast/lee-helm threads — distinct
  from the separate "Larry Whited"), who called the distinction "silly"
  by Archimedes' principle, with Larry citing Chuck Leinweber's Duckworks
  Magazine ballast column and Paul V. adding one line siding with Larry —
  no consensus figure reached. Folded into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md)
  (main record), [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md),
  [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md), and
  [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md). No new concepts, promotions,
  or prunings — both threads matched existing homes well; Larry ("LJGeib")
  remains correctly below the people threshold (recurring but always
  folded into other people's pages, never his own boat/build). Verified no
  new citation-number collisions were introduced across the nine files
  touched (`design/weight-and-ballast.md`, `boats/little-jim.md`,
  `people/paul-v.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-leblanc.md`,
  `people/nels.md`, `people/index.md`, `boats/index.md`, `design/index.md`).

* **Batch 80 (pages 791–800, msgnum 805–814, 18 December 2002 – 6 January
  2003).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely
  distinct; re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header.
  Pages 791–800 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run (805–814)** —
  page_791→805, 792→806, 793→807, 794→808, 795→809, 796→810, 797→811,
  798→812, 799→813, 800→814 — with no missing msgnums and no gaps, but a
  real **date jump at the very end**: 805–812 fell on a single day
  (18 December 2002), 813 was 28 December, and **814 (page_800) is dated
  6 January 2003 — the archive's first message of that year.** A light
  batch, entirely follow-up/coda threads, no new concepts or promotions.
  **(1) "Chine runners" thread continuation** (18–28 Dec) — the plans-less
  newcomer "dfwsfug," having received Alastair's and Glen Maxwell's
  from-scratch explanation in batch 79, came back confused about whether
  the runners were a "modification" and asked for a percentage
  improvement figure; Glen clarified "Zoë" is plans-built with no
  modifications bar the topping lift, and Alastair corrected the deeper
  misunderstanding — Paradox was designed from the start to use chine
  runners (with the rudder providing all lateral resistance), not as a
  modification over a "plans keel," with heeling countered by hull shape
  and ballast rather than the runners; dfwsfug thanked both and signed off
  with season's greetings; a separate one-off correspondent, "light369369,"
  added a thin thank-you/New Year note in the same thread ten days later —
  folded into
  [chine runners and windward performance](/chine-runners/windward-performance.md),
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), and
  [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md). **(2) "Gimble stove mount"** (18 Dec) —
  the same newcomer asked whether Glen's stove gimbal only handled roll;
  Glen confirmed "Zoë"'s stove is not gimbaled in the pitch plane, since
  there is very little pitching, with a half-joking aside about appetite
  in a real storm — folded into
  [cabin heating for cold-weather sailing](/operations/cabin-heating.md).
  **(3) "Jeff Please"** (20 Dec) — a new one-off correspondent, Eric
  ("ericarmer"), asked whether anyone had heard from "Jeff" about his 16
  ft Paradox plans (evidently Jeff (UK)'s "Paramour" design, last heard
  from in February 2002) — unanswered in this batch, folded into
  [Paramour](/design/variants/paramour-design.md)
  and [Jeff (UK, "doryman_uk")](/people/jeff-doryman.md). **(4) "Build
  Book"** (6 Jan 2003) — Paul V. (posting as "sohaven2002") reported
  gearing up to start cutting out bulkheads at last, pointing to his
  updated BuildBook files folder and looking ahead to spring epoxy
  weather — folded into
  [Paul V. (cruisenewsnet / sohaven2002)](/people/paul-v.md).
  No new concepts, files, or promotions this batch — all four threads
  matched existing homes well. No promotions: "dfwsfug," "light369369,"
  "ericarmer," each a one-off poster with no boat, all stay inline per the
  people threshold. Verified no new citation-number collisions were
  introduced across the seven files touched
  (`design/chine-runners-and-windward-performance.md`,
  `operations/cabin-heating.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `design/variants/paramour-design.md`,
  `people/jeff-doryman.md`, `people/paul-v.md`) — confirmed the standing
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision in `glen-maxwell.md` remains untouched and
  unchanged, this batch's one new citation there (`[158]`) appended at the
  file's end.

* **Batch 79 (pages 781–790, msgnum 795–804, 10–14 December 2002).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 781–790 mapped to a
  **clean, consecutive run (795–804)** — page_781→795, 782→796, 783→797,
  784→798, 785→799, 786→800, 787→801, 788→802, 789→803, 790→804 — with no
  missing msgnums and no gaps, all within a tight five-day window
  (10–14 December 2002), the same "Building encouragement" trip report and
  its reader follow-ups carried over from batch 78. A light batch: four small,
  well-matched follow-up threads, all slotted into existing homes — no new
  concepts, no promotions. **(1) Window condensation** (10-11 Dec) — Alastair
  asked about a window-frame drain channel after reading Glen's cold-weekend
  report; Paul V. contributed Sven Yrvind's BRIS-boat condensation-to-drinking-
  water technique; Glen explained "Zoë"'s uninsulated cabin relies on a
  condensation-collecting lip and through-ventilation — folded into
  [cabin heating for cold-weather sailing](/operations/cabin-heating.md),
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md),
  and [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md). **(2) CE/CLR quantified, and a safety
  warning** (12-13 Dec) — a new one-off Australian correspondent, Lothar
  Schwarz, asked Glen to quantify CE/CLR for "Zoë" specifically; Glen admitted
  he never had, and gave the archive's fullest low-tech hand-method
  explanation (balance a folded hull-profile drawing on a knife edge for CLR;
  hang a weighted sail drawing from a pinned string for CE), plus a
  single-hander lee-helm safety warning (a man overboard's lost weight aft
  jibes the boat away from him) — folded into
  [sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md)
