  as a continuation of the still-unresolved "Sail balance" thread from batch
  78. **(3) A third Paradox weight estimate** (13 Dec) — a new one-off
  correspondent, Ole Friis, asked the Paradox's empty/loaded weight for a
  trailer-limit check; Glen gave "Zoë"'s own estimate (650-750 lb empty with
  ballast, ~900 lb loaded, 1,400 lb designed displacement) — folded into
  [weight, ballast, and displacement](/design/weight-and-ballast.md)
  as a third independent data point alongside Don Elliott's and the January
  2002 trailering figures. **(4) Chine runners explained to a plans-less
  newcomer** (13-14 Dec) — a new one-off correspondent, "dfwsfug," asked the
  theory behind the chine runners without owning plans; Alastair answered with
  a disc-vs-cup physical analogy (a hemispherical cup feels roughly twice a
  flat disc's force in a fluid flow, since it deflects flow back rather than
  just stopping it); Glen followed with a fuller technical answer including
  Paradox's heeled-draft figures (9 in upright to ~23 in at 30°) and Matt
  Layden's RC-model development process — folded into
  [chine runners and windward performance](/chine-runners/windward-performance.md).
  All four threads cross-linked into [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)
  and [boats/zoe.md](/boats/zoe.md) as a single "follow-up questions" cluster
  answering the prior batch's trip report. No promotions: Lothar Schwarz, Ole
  Friis ("palander2"), and "dfwsfug" all stay inline — one message each, no
  boat, no follow-up. Verified no new citation-number collisions were
  introduced across the eight files touched
  (`operations/sailing-characteristics.md`, `operations/cabin-heating.md`,
  `design/weight-and-ballast.md`,
  `design/chine-runners-and-windward-performance.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/paul-v.md`,
  `boats/zoe.md`); re-confirmed the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]`
  collisions in `glen-maxwell.md` are unchanged (this batch's four new
  citations, `[154]`-`[157]`, were appended well clear of that area) and still
  await the post-1000 renumbering pass. **Flagged for the orchestrator: we are
  now at page 790 (msgnum 804) — the post-1000 holistic-review checkpoint is
  keyed to page/post number, so it remains roughly 210 pages away (~21
  batches), not yet close; batch 80 should continue business as usual.**

* **Batch 78 (pages 771–780, msgnum 785–794, 21 November–10 December 2002).**
  Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  771–780 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run (785–794)** —
  page_771→785, 772→786, 773→787, 774→788, 775→789, 776→790, 777→791,
  778→792, 779→793, 780→794 — with no missing msgnums, but with two real
  date gaps: 785 (21 Nov) to 786 (23 Nov, a 2-day jump), and 786/787-792
  (23-28 Nov) to 793/794 (10 Dec, a 12-day jump). **(1) The side-deck plans-error
  dispute resolved — RESOLVED the standing backlog item.** The very first
  page of this batch is Glen Maxwell's follow-up to the dispute left
  unresolved at the end of batch 77: re-measuring his own build against
  sheet #3, Glen found the foredeck's true max width is 3'11" and, with the
  deck crown added, confirmed a builder "probably" will exceed 4 ft — Alastair
  was right. Glen recalled he had in fact scabbed in an extra piece of ply on
  each side of his own boat to reach the gunwale edge. Updated
  [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md),
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md),
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md),
  and [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) to reflect the resolution — the
  "left unresolved" framing in both files is now replaced. **(2) A
  polyurethane-glue deck beam** (23 November) — Paul V. tried PL
  polyurethane adhesive in place of epoxy for a deck beam, judged "a good
  part," not yet load-tested — folded into
  [deck and cabin beam lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md)
  and [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md). **(3) "Sail balance" — a new lee-helm
  thread on "Whisper"** (27-28 November): David Beard reported needing
  constant negative rudder, drawing Glen Maxwell's textbook CE/CLR
  diagnosis and four fixes, a motor-weight tangent with Larry Whited over
  David's 55 lb outboard, and a bare-poles ballast-placement test from a
  second, distinct "Larry" (LJGeib) — left unresolved. Folded into
  [sailing characteristics and rig balance](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md),
  [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md), and
  [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md). **(4) "Building encouragement" — Glen
  Maxwell's long Cumberland Sound trip report** (10 December), the batch's
  richest single message: a real-world builder's field confirmation of the
  chine runners' windward performance under hard short-tacking; a newly
  discovered mast-heel play problem (~3/16 in, ~4 in at the masthead) linked
  to lost pointing and a broken anchor-light filament; a halyard-position
  experiment that let Glen balance the helm, answering the "Sail balance"
  thread above; the flower-pot cabin-heat technique confirmed in actual use;
  the skylight and chart table (previously only shown in photos) reported
  in practical use; a trailer-tire blowout with no spare aboard; and a
  halyard-knot failure that dropped the rig overboard, compounded by a
  furling-line jam, both recovered from underway. A brief reply two weeks
  later from Paul V. (`sohaven2002`) thanked Glen for the morale boost while
  working through a cold-climate winter build. Folded into
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md)
  (the main record),
  [chine runners and windward performance](/chine-runners/windward-performance.md),
  [mast interior sealing and anchor-light fit](/construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md),
  [cabin heating](/operations/cabin-heating.md),
  [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md),
  [trailering](/operations/trailering.md),
  [furling system](/components/rig/furling.md),
  and [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md).
  No new concepts or boat/person pages needed — all four threads matched
  existing homes well, though the trip report is now cross-linked from six
  different files given its unusually wide topical spread. No promotions:
  Larry Whited and "LJGeib" (a separate, distinct "Larry") both stay inline
  per the people threshold, consistent with their existing status. Verified
  no new citation-number collisions were introduced across the eleven files
  touched (`construction/panel-layout.md`, `boats/little-jim.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md`, `people/paul-v.md`,
  `operations/sailing-characteristics.md`, `boats/whisper.md`,
  `people/david-beard.md`, `construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md`,
  `design/chine-runners-and-windward-performance.md`, `boats/zoe.md`,
  `operations/trailering.md`, `design/furling-system.md`,
  `operations/cabin-heating.md`) — re-checked the pre-existing
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area in `glen-maxwell.md` and reconfirmed
  it is unchanged, still awaiting the post-1000 renumbering pass.

* **Batch 77 (pages 761–770, msgnum 775–784, 5–20 November 2002).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages 761–770 mapped to
  a **clean, consecutive run (775–784)** — page_761→775, 762→776, 763→777,
  764→778, 765→779, 766→780, 767→781, 768→782, 769→783, 770→784 — but with
  two real date gaps inside an otherwise-tidy numeric sequence: 775 (5 Nov)
  to 776/777 (10 Nov, a 5-day jump), and 777/778 (10 Nov) to 779 (16 Nov, a
  6-day jump); the remaining 779–784 run within a single 16–20 November
  window. No missing msgnums this batch. Three threads. **(1) A "Room for 2"
  coda.** The day after batch 76's three-photo "Room for 2" series, Dave
  Bolduc posted one further lighthearted photo, "Paradox at a dinghy dock in
  North Carolina" ("Note the size of Paradox versus the dinghies. Yikes!") —
  folded into
  [Matt Layden (biography)](/matt-layden/biography.md)
  and [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) as a coda, not new biographical
  content. Separately, on 17 November, Dave shared a first-person "Skidless
  decks" account of his own PETTIT-Skidless-Compound non-skid technique for
  Little Cruiser — a technique already known from microcruising.com but now
  independently confirmed from the archive itself — folded into
  [non-skid deck finish](/construction/non-skid-deck.md) and
  [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md). **(2) A thirteenth "where to buy
  plans" recurrence** (10 November): a new UK "Pete" (`widcoo`) asked the
  now-familiar question, answered within the hour by Glen Maxwell with the
  same Dave-Bolduc/$35 pointer — folded into
  [Paradox building plans](/references/building-plans.md) and
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md). **(3) "Progress, a question and
  a problem" — Little Jim's deck goes on (16–20 November), the batch's main
  Matt-Layden-adjacent build content.** Alastair reported his fore and aft
  decks fitted, side decks next, and raised two questions: mast protection
  at the deck partners (resolved — David Beard reported no wear on
  "Whisper," a one-off poster "dclark52001" suggested a leather washer, and
  Glen Maxwell's own epoxied-leather-collar fix, "worked well," carried the
  day) — folded into
  [mast interior sealing and anchor-light fit](/construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md);
  and a possible **plans error** in the side-deck/cabin-end-panel layout
  (Alastair: the two side decks and two cabin end panels can't fit one 4
  ft-wide sheet as drawn; Glen: no problem, recalling the end pieces aren't
  a full 4 ft wide; Alastair's fuller re-measurement narrowed it to 2 of 6
  end pieces and proposed a jointed-piece workaround) — **left unresolved at
  the end of this batch**, folded into
  [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md),
  with the build-progress side updated in
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md).
  No new concepts or files: all three threads were folded into existing,
  well-matched homes. No promotions: "Pete" (`widcoo`) and "dclark52001"
  both stay inline (one message each, no boat, no follow-up) per the people
  threshold. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the ten files touched
  (`matt-layden/biography.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`,
  `construction/non-skid-deck.md`, `references/building-plans.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `construction/mast-sealing-and-fit.md`,
  `construction/panel-layout.md`, `boats/little-jim.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `boats/whisper.md`) — and
  reconfirmed the standing `people/glen-maxwell.md` `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]`
  collisions are pre-existing and untouched by this batch's new `[142]`/
  `[143]` citations, correctly deferred to the post-1000 holistic review.

* **Batch 76 (pages 751–760, msgnum 765–774, 21 October – 4 November 2002).**
  Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Pages
  751–757 mapped to a **clean, consecutive run (765–771)**, all within two
  days (21–23 October 2002) — page_751→765, 752→766, 753→767, 754→768,
  755→769, 756→770, 757→771. Then a **large, genuine gap**: pages 758–760
  mapped to msgnum 772–774, dated **4 November 2002** — a nearly two-week
  jump with no archive captures for whatever msgnums (if any) fall between
  771 and 772; batch 77 should expect this gap rather than a clean
  continuation from 774/775. Two threads this batch, one of them
  significant for both the Nels backlog item and Matt Layden biography.
  **(1) New "Deck Beam" thread.** Paul V. reported pulling his first
  plywood-laminated deck beam (six layers of 1/4 in ply); Alastair flagged
  a beam-count mismatch (6 vs. Paul's 7); **Nels** ("~Edwin~," Canada)
  raised a structural doubt about plywood-vs-solid-wood lamination
  stiffness, which Paul answered while explaining his "Build Book"
  documentation philosophy (informal builder's notes, not a definitive
  method, drawing on his past experience at S2 Yachts) — folded into
  [deck and cabin beam lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md)
  and [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md). In the same exchange, Nels revealed his
  own planned build for the first time ("If I build one it will follow much
  the same order as you... I will be simplifying it as well. No water
  tanks and a few other changes") — combined with his earlier motorsailer
  thread (batch 73), this **resolved the standing backlog item**: Nels now
  has a second, distinct thread plus his own build plans, meeting the
  people-threshold promotion criteria. **Promoted Nels to
  [people/nels.md](/people/nels.md)**, cross-linked from `paul-v.md`,
  `alastair-law.md`, `design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md`,
  `deck-and-cabin-beams.md`, and `people/index.md`. **(2) "Room for 2"
  thread — new Matt Layden biographical content.** After the archive gap
  above, Dave Bolduc answered a frequently-asked question — is there room
  for two aboard Paradox? — with a direct "yes, but not spacious" verdict,
  reiterating Matt's boats were designed primarily for solo sailing with
  short-cruise accommodation for an extra crew member, and adding a
  firsthand **three-up** data point: cruising in company with Matt in the
  Bahamas, the group cooked aboard Little Cruiser and all three (Dave,
  Mindy, Matt) ate aboard Paradox. Three photos followed (Mindy and Matt
  aboard Paradox; Karen and Matt in the Bahamas aboard Paradox; Matt and
  Karen sailing Paradox in Georgetown, Bahamas) — the archive's **first
  confirmation that Matt's then-girlfriend Karen sailed aboard Paradox
  herself**, not only the earlier Balboa 20. Folded into
  [Matt Layden (biography)](/matt-layden/biography.md),
  [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md),
  and [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md); noted as updating/superseding
  an earlier textless September 2001 "Room for two" post (citation 6 in
  `dave-bolduc.md`) that survives only as a bare attachment. Verified no
  new citation-number collisions were introduced across the eight files
  touched (`construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md`, `people/paul-v.md`,
  `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/nels.md` [new], `people/index.md`,
  `design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md`, `matt-layden/biography.md`,
  `matt-layden/index.md`, `operations/crew-size-and-fit.md`,
  `people/dave-bolduc.md`, `construction/index.md`).

* **Batch 75 (pages 741–750, msgnum 755–764, 5–15 October 2002).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Mapping this batch was a
  **clean, consecutive run (755–764) with no gaps** — page_741→755,
  page_742→756, page_743→757, page_744→758, page_745→759, page_746→760,
  page_747→761, page_748→762, page_749→763, page_750→764 — the third
  gap-free ten-page batch in several rounds, and msgnum order matched
  `Date:`-header order throughout with no reordering quirks this time. Nine
  of the ten messages fell within two days (5–6 October 2002); the tenth
  (page_750, msgnum 764) was a delayed reply arriving a week later, on 15
  October 2002. A light batch, three small threads, no new concepts needed:
  **(1) "Little Jim progresses" coda.** A new Quebec, Canada correspondent
  ("rej leves," awaiting his own plans, ex-Bolger-Weekender/sea-kayak
  builder) welcomed Alastair's build with praise; separately Glen Maxwell
  answered Alastair's rope-size question from the tail of batch 74 (1/8 in
  lifting portion, 1/4 in hauling portion, soft yacht braid throughout for
  easier handling) — folded into
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md)
  and cross-linked from
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md).
  **(2) New "BuildBook" thread.** Reading Paul V.'s newly posted building
  notes, Alastair caught a premature-cutting error in Paul's own chine/clamp
  log notching plan (horizontal cut only until the sides are on) and, once
  Paul confirmed he had "Finally started with some woodworking" (a
  deck-beam mold, laminated from 1/4 in ply), volunteered an unprompted and
  strongly worded warning: **laminate the chine log** rather than bend a
  solid one, as Alastair's own solid-log bend had been the hardest and most
  physically risky part of his build so far — folded into
  [notching the stem for chine logs and sheer clamps](/construction/stem-notching.md)
  as a direct continuation of the March 2002 notching thread, rather than a
  new file, and cross-linked from `people/paul-v.md` and `people/alastair-law.md`.
  **(3) New "Little Jim Photos?" thread.** A new UK visitor ("barnacleid" /
  Bill Serjeant, `smallsailboats.co.uk` — distinct from, and not confirmed
  as the same person as, the "Bill Serjant" cited secondhand in
  `paul-v.md`'s February 2002 correspondence) asked Alastair to post his
  construction photos to the group's own files/photos sections; Alastair
  pointed to his existing website and extended an open invitation to visit
  in person, which Bill hoped to take up "next spring" — folded into
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md).
  No promotions or prunings: rej leves and Bill Serjeant both stay inline
  (single message each, no boat, per the people threshold); Nels does not
  appear anywhere in this batch, so the standing "Nels approaching the
  people threshold" backlog item remains open and untouched, its trigger
  not met. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced
  across the five files touched (`boats/little-jim.md`,
  `construction/stem-notching.md`, `people/paul-v.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`), and confirmed the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]`
  collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md` (flagged since batch 63) are
  unrelated to this batch's new `[141]` citation.

* **Batch 74 (pages 731–740, msgnum 745–754, 3–5 October 2002).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Mapping this batch was a
  **clean, consecutive run (745–754) with no gaps** — page_731→745,
  page_732→746, page_733→747, page_734→748, page_735→749, page_736→750,
  page_737→751, page_738→752, page_739→753, page_740→754 — the second
  gap-free ten-page batch in several rounds. All ten messages fell within
  three days (3–5 October 2002); msgnum order matched `Date:`-header order
  except for a same-evening cluster (750/751/752, all Alastair one-line
  replies sent within minutes of each other on 4 October) where msgnum 752's
  timestamp (22:00:03) actually precedes 750's (22:02:27) and 751's
  (22:09:08) — noted but not reordered, since all three are trivial,
  independent one-liners with no narrative dependency on strict ordering.
  Three small threads plus one substantial coda: **(1) "Motorsailer" thread
  coda.** Nels asked Alastair how sanitation works on a boat with no room for
  a portapotti at all (as on Paradox), and separately gave a firsthand
  "real chicken about big water" gloss on his own planned Lake
  Michigan/sheltered-water shakedown; Alastair answered in three words,
  **"Bucket & chuck it!"**, matching Dave Bolduc's/Matt Layden's own
  informal practice already on record, and separately pointed to
  [Jeff (UK)](/people/jeff-doryman.md)'s own longer/lighter Paradox-based
  redesign ("Paramour") as already underway, confirming Nels's "scaled up
  without becoming a sow's ear" assumption. Folded into
  [Motorsailer Paradox](/design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md) (new sections
  plus citations 6–9) and cross-linked from
  [head and sanitation arrangements](/operations/head-and-sanitation.md).
  **(2) New "Bulkhead side curves" thread.** Paul V. asked about the small
  curved offset (5/7/5 mm) in three of the four bulkheads' side edges and
  whether builders had trouble bending the ply to match; Alastair answered
  he had none. Slotted into
  [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md) alongside the
  existing bulkhead/nesting content, rather than a new file. **(3) New
  "Metric system? what metric system?" thread.** Paul V.'s wry aside on
  measuring in metric against nominally-imperial US lumber stock, answered
  by Alastair (sawn vs. planed distinction; the plans' mm values are
  conversions of Matt Layden's own inch-based purchasing) and a new
  one-off poster, **Barend Migchelsen** (pocket-calculator-as-metric aside,
  kept inline per the people threshold). Also folded into
  [panel layout and nesting](/construction/panel-layout.md). **(4) "Little
  Jim progresses."** Alastair collected his sail and gave "Little Jim" her
  first hands-on rigging trial — mast stepped, sail hoisted — and found, like
  Glen Maxwell and David Beard before him, that furling without a topping
  lift was impractical (3 in steps, blisters); he fitted his own topping
  lift (masthead block, split to each boom end) and asked the list what rope
  size to use, judging the plans' 6 mm too thin — this is the **third
  independent builder** to hit and fix the same furling problem. Updated
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md), cross-linked into
  [furling system](/components/rig/furling.md) (new section plus citation
  29a). No new concepts, no promotions or prunings: Nels (now five messages
  across two batches, one sustained thread, still no boat) and Barend
  Migchelsen (one-off) both correctly stay below the people threshold;
  flagged Nels in the backlog as approaching the threshold for batch 75+ to
  watch. Verified no new citation-number collisions were introduced across
  the eight files touched
  (`design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md`,
  `operations/head-and-sanitation.md`, `construction/panel-layout.md`,
  `boats/little-jim.md`, `design/furling-system.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `people/paul-v.md`, plus index-file description touch-ups in
  `design/index.md`, `boats/index.md`, `construction/index.md`,
  `design/variants/index.md`, and `people/index.md`).

* **Batch 73 (pages 721–730, msgnum 733–744, 28 September – 3 October 2002).**
  Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct;
  re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header. Mapping this
  batch: page_721→733, page_722→734, page_723→735, page_724→736,
  page_725→737, page_726→740, page_727→741, page_728→742, page_729→743,
  page_730→744 — a genuine **gap at msgnum 738–739** (no `page_NNN.eml` maps
  to either number) between page_725 and page_726, and a **five-day jump**
  in `Date:` headers at the same point (28 September → 2 October 2002); all
  ten messages were otherwise in clean chronological order, so no
  reordering was needed. Four small threads plus one substantial new one:
  **(1) Plywood thread's coda.** Glen Maxwell finally answered David
  Beard's original ply-species request with two supplier links
  (`boulterplywood.com`, `hardwoodint.com`); Larry Whited closed the
  tanks-vs-containers aside with a use-case-dependent verdict (day
  sailor/short cruiser: permanent ballast; long cruiser like Dave Bolduc:
  save every container for food/water) — both folded into
  [plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md).
  **(2) "Re: Spam" coda.** David LeBlanc volunteered as a third moderator
  after the shortage was already resolved by Dave Bolduc and Paul V.; Glen
  thanked him and floated adding him once he and Dave Bolduc left on a
  **planned joint winter sailing trip** (the first specific mention of that
  trip) — added to
  [mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md).
  **(3) "Little Jim" pictures thread's coda.** Larry Whited added a
  one-line compliment, folded into
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md).
  **(4) New "Building a Paradox (motorsailer?)" thread.** After the msgnum
  gap and five-day jump, Canadian prospective builder **Nels** (previously
  only a thin, inline mention from the building-sequence thread in earlier
  batches) replied to the Little Jim announcement with a substantially modified
  "motorsailer" Paradox proposal — a 3 ft slipper stern enclosing an
  outboard, a relocated aft bulkhead/extended cabin, a pram hood, and a
  pedestal-mounted, forestay-raised mast for clearing bridges, aimed at
  Canadian inland-lake/canal-adjacent cruising. **Alastair** objected on
  **designer-fidelity** grounds ("why make a sows ear out of a silk
  purse?"), flagging the harder moment-of-inertia problem behind Nels's
  weight-rebalancing plan and doubting a CAD rendering would reveal
  anything about real performance. **Paul V.** then gave the thread's
  fullest reply ("a boat is far more than the sum of its parts"), judging
  Nels's changes would need a full independent redesign — and, in the same
  message, **revealed his own planned modifications** to his personal
  Paradox for cold-climate use: a hinged, dog-down watertight hatch and
  lead ballast in place of the water tank, plus insulation and bug
  protection, for a trip toward a remote Canadian location (`mandow.ca`).
  Nels's reply gave a firsthand account of the dangerously shoal Hudson Bay
  coastline from his own weather-service flying days. This was substantial
  and novel enough (a new modification proposal, a new design-philosophy
  statement from two different members, and Paul V.'s first-ever reveal of
  his own build plans) to warrant a new concept,
  [Motorsailer Paradox (speculative)](/design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md),
  filed under `design/variants/` alongside the archive's other
  enlarged/modified-Paradox proposals. Nels himself stays an inline mention
  (three messages, one thread, no boat) per the people threshold, cross-linked
  from [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md) where he first appeared. Verified no new
  citation-number collisions were introduced across the nine files touched
  (`construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`,
  `references/mailing-list.md`, `boats/little-jim.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`,
  `people/david-leblanc.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/paul-v.md`,
  `design/variants/motorsailer-idea.md` [new], `design/variants/index.md`).

* **Batch 72 (pages 711–720, msgnum 723–732, 28 September 2002).** Read all
  ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct; re-derived
  every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header per the orchestrator's
  standing instruction. Mapping this batch: page_711→723, page_712→724,
  page_713→725, page_714→726, page_715→727, page_716→728, page_717→729,
  page_718→730, page_719→731, page_720→732 — a clean, consecutive run with
  **no gaps**, unlike the last several batches; all ten messages sent within
  a single day (28 September 2002), and `Date:`/msgnum order matched
  throughout, so no reordering was needed. Four threads: **(1) "Bulkhead 3
  side cleats? Deck Beam dims and BuildBook."** Paul V. asked two
  independent construction questions in one message — whether bulkhead #3
  really has no side cleats (reviving, without realizing it, a question a
  different member had asked and left unanswered in January 2002), and what
  the deck beam dimensions/radius are, not fully specified on the drawings.
  **Alastair** answered both same-morning: no cleats needed (the side panel
  glues/nails directly to bulkhead #3's edge), and the deck is a **parabola,
  not a radius** — laminate all deck beams on one mold built from sheet 7's
  dimensions, then cut each to length; roof beams the same way from sheet
  10. This **finally answered** the archive's eight-month-old open question,
  so [bulkhead #3 side cleats](/construction/bulkhead-3-cleats-question.md)
  was retitled from "(unanswered question)" to "— answered eight months
  later" and given a new answer section (citations 2–3); the deck-beam
  question was slotted into the existing
  [deck and cabin beam lamination](/construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md)
  (new section, citations 3–4) rather than a new file. **(2) The "Vent
  Baffle" thread closes.** Two more replies to Paul V.'s water-slapping
  cross-examination (from batch 71): **Alastair** explained the baffle is
  high enough to keep out normal water while the tiller hole sits low
  enough to stay above the waterline even upside-down (untested, by his own
  admission); **Dave Bolduc** reaffirmed ten trouble-free years with the
  rope-tiller/boot arrangement on Little Cruiser, plus an 18-month-old
  double-braided-line upgrade and a "mostly splashing, not real leaking"
  verdict. Neither directly answered Paul's original cross-examination,
  which remains open. Extended
  [transom deck plate and vent baffle](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md)
  (new closing section, citations 11–12). **(3) The "Plywood" thread
  continues.** David Beard answered Paul V.'s overnight question (why drop
  the water tanks?): he prefers small water containers, freeing the tanks
  as storage since he already has permanent ballast sited there, and
  confirmed he'd already glassed the hull's outside but judged the many
  small interior pieces impractical to glass, sticking with his
  plywood-species-change plan over Paul's glass-cloth fix. Extended
  [plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md)
  (new section, citation 18). **(4) "Building a Paradox (pictures)" — a new
  boat is named.** Alastair announced, for the first time in the archive,
  that his own long-tracked Paradox build (documented in detail since July
  2001) has a name: **"little Jim."** He posted a construction photo
  gallery after "over a year" of building, expecting to launch the
  following summer. **David Beard** replied warmly and recommended a
  topping lift before decking over, drawing from his own furling struggles
  on "Whisper"; **Alastair** answered that his sail was arriving the
  following week and offered his own untested **theory** for how designer
  Matt Layden furls without a topping lift at all (large furling-drum
  radius vs. small rolled-sail radius on the boom, so the boom
  self-supports when both lines are tight) — a question the archive had
  left open since Bert Vercauteren's December 2001 remark. He also asked an
  unanswered question about tight aft-deck/side-deck clearance on sheet 6.
  **Glen Maxwell** approved the site, adding it to the group's links area.
  Given its own new boat page,
  [Little Jim (Alastair)](/boats/little-jim.md), cross-linked from
  [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md); the topping-lift theory also
  cross-linked into
  [furling system](/components/rig/furling.md).
  **Separately, "Re: Spam" resolved the moderator-shortage question left
  open at the end of batch 71**: Glen Maxwell reported the very next
  morning that **Dave Bolduc and Paul V.** (the thread's original proposer)
  had both volunteered as co-moderators, making it practical to close the
  group to unapproved new members — the fastest thread resolution recorded
  in the archive's spam saga. Extended
  [Paradox builders mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md) (new
  "resolved the next day" section, citation 13),
  [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (new section, citation 138),
  [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (new section, citation 46), and
  [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md) (new paragraph, citation 22). **Citation-
  collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were
  introduced across the twelve touched files
  (`construction/bulkhead-3-cleats-question.md`,
  `construction/deck-and-cabin-beams.md`,
  `construction/transom-deck-plate.md`,
  `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`,
  `people/paul-v.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`,
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `references/mailing-list.md`,
  `design/furling-system.md`, `boats/little-jim.md` [new]); the pre-existing
  `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already
  tracked in the backlog, are untouched by this batch's new citations
  (`[136]`–`[138]`, added at the file's end). Updated index descriptions in
  [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, Alastair, Dave Bolduc,
  David Beard, Paul V. entries), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (new
  Little Jim entry, Whisper entry extended),
  [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (bulkhead-3-cleats
  retitled, deck-and-cabin-beams, transom-deck-plate, plywood-grades
  entries), and [references/index.md](/references/index.md) (mailing-list
  entry). **One promotion**: Alastair's boat, previously unnamed, is now
  [Little Jim](/boats/little-jim.md) — a genuinely new boat page (not a
  restructure), since the name and a real construction-photo gallery
  crossed the boats threshold this batch. No prunings. No new backlog items
  triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch
  did not reach post-1000, and while it did add citations to
  `people/glen-maxwell.md`, they were appended at the file's end and did
  not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to
  **720**.

* **Batch 71 (pages 701–710, msgnum 712–722 with one further gap, 27–28 September 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct (no duplicates); re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header per the orchestrator's standing instruction. Mapping: page_701→712, page_702→713 (already flagged by batch 70 as jumping ahead of its neighbors), **gap at 714** (no corresponding `page_NNN.eml`, a genuine missing archive capture), page_703→715, page_704→716, page_705→717 (also already flagged by batch 70), page_706→718, page_707→719, page_708→720, page_709→721, page_710→722 — all ten distinct and in ascending msgnum order, sent within the same roughly 30-hour window (27–28 September 2002); no further reordering needed since `Date:` order matched msgnum order throughout. Two threads: **(1) The "Stopping spammers" thread's tail.** Continuing directly from batch 70's newly opened moderator-approval proposal, four more replies arrived, all favoring Glen Maxwell's status quo over any structural fix: **Alastair** judged spam a delete-key-level nuisance, worse on digest-read groups (trash bundled with wanted posts); **David Beard** seconded David LeBlanc's objection with a terse "I agree !!"; **Ron Eike** favored keeping the group open so members can choose their own remedy (digest, or just ignoring obvious-spam senders); and **David LeBlanc**, in a separate "Spam" message, gave the thread's only concrete anti-spam mechanism — forward spam with full headers to `abuse@` at the sending network. No resolution (approval requirement, added moderators, or digest switch) is recorded by the end of this batch. Extended [Paradox builders mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md) (new "thread's tail" subsection, citations 9–12), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 51), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 109), [Ron Eike](/people/ron-eike.md) (new section, citation 7), and [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md) (citation 32). **(2) Two new construction threads from David Beard and Paul V.** David Beard opened **"Plywood,"** revealing that buying "Whisper" had led him to pause his own from-scratch build to learn the design first, with two settled changes for that build: no water tanks, and a checking-resistant ply other than Fir. **David LeBlanc** answered with the archive's most detailed species recommendation yet — Meranti to BS1088, lighter/cheaper than Okoume and slightly more rot-resistant per a "wood expert friend," both needing religious epoxy encapsulation; BS6065 usable but a step down; uncertified "marine" ply warned against; Brunzeel (Dutch) named as the top US-east-coast choice. **Paul V.** asked why David wanted to drop the tanks (unanswered) and offered a cheaper, competing fix for the checking problem itself — a thin glass/epoxy layer over Fir. Slotted into the existing [plywood grade and foam flotation debate](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md) (new "a third builder revives the question" section, citations 15–17) rather than a new file, since it is the same underlying checking question raised in October 2001 and January 2002. Separately, Paul V. opened **"Vent Baffle,"** questioning whether the transom's tiller-rope opening could be eliminated via drip boxes and a dorade box; **Dave Bolduc** answered that the arrangement works well on Little Cruiser and described her vinyl tiller-boot fix; Paul followed up with unanswered questions about water-slapping and the ventilation trade-off, adding he wasn't sure he'd trust "the rope tiller as the main steering system." Slotted into the existing [transom deck plate](/construction/transom-deck-plate.md) (retitled "Transom deck plate and vent baffle," new section, citations 8–10), since Alastair's original January 2002 answer in that file already described the same tiller-vent-baffle drain path. Extended [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (new section, citation 110), [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (new bullet, citation 43), [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md) (new section, citations 14–16), and [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (new section, citation 44). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced across the ten touched files (`references/mailing-list.md`, `construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md`, `construction/transom-deck-plate.md`, `people/david-leblanc.md`, `people/ron-eike.md`, `people/alastair-law.md`, `people/david-beard.md`, `people/paul-v.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`, `boats/whisper.md`); the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched (Glen does not appear as a poster in this batch). Updated index descriptions in [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Alastair, Dave Bolduc, David Beard, David LeBlanc, Ron Eike, Paul V. entries), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (Whisper entry), and [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (plywood-grades and transom-deck-plate entries). No new concept files created — both new threads fit well-matched existing homes. No promotions or prunings: all six participants already have person pages; no new one-off contributors appeared this batch. No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **710**.

* **Batch 70 (pages 691–700, msgnum 700–711 with two further gaps, 25–27 September 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct (no duplicates); re-derived every msgnum from its own `X-Yahoo-Msgnum` header per the orchestrator's instruction, since batch 69 had flagged the offset as irregular. Confirmed the mapping is **not** a clean continuation: page_691→700, page_692→701, **gap at 702** (belongs to a later page file, page_702.eml→msgnum713, confirmed by direct check), page_693→703, page_694→704, **gap at 705** (page_705.eml→msgnum717), page_695→706, page_696→707, page_697→708, page_698→709, page_699→710, page_700→711 — all ten distinct, with msgnum 702 and 705 confirmed as missing archive captures rather than mislabelings (flagged for batch 71). One major thread plus one substantial administrative thread, both centered on **Paul V.**: **(1) "THIS IS A REAL POST...Paradox building sequence."** Continuing from batch 69's flagged, unconfirmed second "Paul V." handle (`sohaven2002`), this batch **resolves the identity question**: replying to Charles Wilson's offer of a spare plans CD, Paul V. signed off **"Paul V / aka sohaven2002,"** then immediately resumed posting under his usual `cruisenewsnet`/`admin@` identity for the rest of the batch — confirming both handles are the same person. Dave Bolduc pointed to Don Elliott's paid 16-part articles as the substantive answer; Charles Wilson offered his own spare plans CD; a new one-off Canadian contributor, **Nels** (`arvent@...`/`recree8`), asked for a copy of Don's articles too (twice failing to reach Don directly), was redirected to Charles, and confirmed days later that Charles had followed through — "What a great bunch of folks!" Updated [Paul V. (cruisenewsnet / sohaven2002)](/people/paul-v.md) (retitled from "(cruisenewsnet)," rewritten identity section, citations 3–10), [hull assembly sequence](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md) (corrected the prior "unanswered" framing now that Dave Bolduc's/Charles's replies are captured, citation 9 added), [Charles Wilson](/people/charles-wilson.md) (new section, citation 5), and [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (new one-line section, citation 43). Nels stays a below-threshold inline mention (two messages, one thread, no boat), cross-linked from all four files. **(2) "Stopping spammers — require confirmation before adding new users?"** The next day, Paul V. (back under his usual identity) opened the archive's first proposal of a *structural* anti-spam fix — requiring new members to be approved by a moderator — prompted by the six-post bare-link spam pattern tracked since batch 68. Glen Maxwell (the list's sole moderator/founder) replied he could enable it but would need volunteer co-moderators, since he hoped to be away sailing for a couple of months, and floated reading posts via the Yahoo website as an alternative; he then suggested trying daily-digest mode first, citing another group's experience. David LeBlanc objected to the Yahoo-website idea, citing Yahoo's own on-site advertising. No resolution recorded. New section added to [Paradox builders mailing list](/references/mailing-list.md) (citations 5–8, extending its History section into a "Spam and a moderator-approval proposal" section) and to [Paul V.](/people/paul-v.md), [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citations 136–137), and [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md) (citation 31). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced across the seven touched files (`people/paul-v.md`, `construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md`, `people/charles-wilson.md`, `people/dave-bolduc.md`, `references/mailing-list.md`, `people/glen-maxwell.md`, `people/david-leblanc.md`); the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits. Updated index descriptions in [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Paul V., Charles Wilson, David LeBlanc, Glen Maxwell entries), [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (hull-assembly-sequence entry), and [references/index.md](/references/index.md) (mailing-list entry). No new concept files created — the spam-moderation content extended the existing `references/mailing-list.md` rather than spawning a new page, since it is genuinely list-administration history rather than boat/build content. No promotions or prunings: Nels stays inline per the people threshold, consistent with past newcomer handling. No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **700**.

* **Batch 69 (pages 681–690, msgnum 683–699, offset widening from +2 to a gap, 7–25 September 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each genuinely distinct (no duplicates); noted the file→msgnum offset, steady at +2 through batch 68's page_680/msgnum682, **widens partway through this batch**: page_681–687 continue msgnum 683–689 (+2), but page_688–690 jump to msgnum **691, 695, 699** — confirming genuine archive gaps (msgnum 690, 692–694, 696–698 are simply missing `page_NNN.eml` captures, not mislabelings) rather than a batch-worker error; flagged for the orchestrator's awareness before batch 70. Four threads/clusters: **(1) The "Size matters??" thread's tail.** Continuing from batch 68's newcomer **Ernie** ("whittlinfool") headroom question, David Beard offered to take measurements from "Whisper"; **Alastair** asked Ernie where in the world he was building ("Are we to see building in new territory?"); Ernie replied with his location (Sebastopol, CA) and thanks; David then revealed a detail not previously stated so plainly — he is **also building his own Paradox from scratch**, in addition to owning "Whisper" — and this in turn prompted **Larry Whited** to ask directly whether Ernie/David meant **Bob Archibald's** boat, which David confirmed in plain words ("yes, I did buy Mr. Archibald's boat"), the archive's clearest own confirmation of Whisper's original-builder identity. Extended [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) (citations 21–24), [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (citations 39–42), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citations 105–108), and [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 50). **(2) GPS thread coda.** Glen Maxwell closed out Dave Bolduc's eTrex Mariner follow-up (batch 68) with a brief "Great Post Dave, Thanks" reply. Extended [GPS and navigation instruments](/operations/gps-navigation.md) (citation 6) and [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citation 134). **(3) The tripod-spam pattern's sixth occurrence.** A new one-off handle, "fkdbovtfgjct," posted a **sixth** instance of the recurring bare-link spam pattern — but this time even more degraded, with **no link at all**, only the literal placeholder text `<message3>`, suggesting a malfunctioning spam-generation template; unanswered, like the fourth and fifth occurrences. Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citation 135). **(4) Two new one-off threads.** **Steven Lewis** ("lewisboats"), new to the group, proposed an unrelated general small-boat **"messabout"** at Lake Okoboji, NW Iowa, for summer 2003 — a third, geographically distinct gathering point alongside Apalachicola Bay and Cedar Key, added as a new section + citation [16] to [group cruise planning and firsthand trip reports](/operations/group-cruise-reports.md) (renumbering this addition from a first-attempted `[15]` after discovering it collided with a pre-existing microcruising.com citation already using that number — fixed before committing). Separately, a poster signing himself **"Paul V."** under a new handle ("sohaven2002") asked, prefaced with "No link to porn here!" and "THIS IS A REAL POST..." (pre-empting being mistaken for the ongoing spam nuisance), whether anyone had documented the Paradox **building sequence** beyond the plans — unanswered in this batch; added as a new section + citation [8] to [hull assembly sequence](/construction/hull-assembly-sequence.md), with a cross-link (not a merge) added to the existing [Paul V. (cruisenewsnet)](/people/paul-v.md) page flagging the shared name as unconfirmed rather than assuming identity. Steven Lewis and "fkdbovtfgjct" both stay one-off inline mentions per the people threshold (no boat, no follow-up). **Citation-collision check**: caught and fixed the `group-cruise-reports.md` `[15]` collision described above; verified no other new duplicate citation numbers were introduced across the nine touched files (the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits). Updated index descriptions in [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, David Beard, Dave Bolduc, Alastair entries), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (Whisper entry), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) (crew-size-and-fit, GPS, and group-cruise-reports entries), and [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (hull-assembly-sequence entry). No promotions or prunings — Larry Whited remains correctly below the people threshold (thin/inline, consistent with his existing status); Steven Lewis and the new spam handle are likewise inline-only. No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **690**.

* **Batch 68 (pages 671–680, msgnum 673–682, offset +2 holding steady, 31 August – 7 September 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each distinct (no gaps/duplicates against batch 67's msgnum 672 endpoint); noted that same-day msgnum order does not always match message timestamp order at second-level resolution — page_671 (msgnum 673, sent 22:09 UTC) actually arrived chronologically **after** page_672/673 (msgnum 674/675, sent 22:55 UTC/02:11 UTC), confirmed by converting each message's own `Date:` header to UTC; content was drafted in true chronological order regardless of msgnum. Two small threads plus the ongoing spam nuisance and its GPS-purchase coda: **(1) The "New Pictures" spam thread's tail, plus two further occurrences.** The third occurrence's pile-on (already logged in batch 67, ending at Glen Maxwell's "If I knew how to Spam the originator I would") continued with **Larry Whited** joking he'd "go to my Linux box and try to use sendmail to blow his box full," and Glen's one-word reply "GREAT!!!!" — closing that exchange without any real counter-spam action. Separately, **two further bare tripod-link posts** arrived from two new one-off handles — "ljuiuktjvh" ("Just Found The Best Site!") and "aqzufgzoxlox" ("New Website Here") — a **fourth and fifth** occurrence of the pattern, both unanswered, confirming the nuisance persisted into September with no moderation beyond the list's informal "it's spam" replies. Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citations 130–133). **(2) New: "Size matters?" — a 6'4" newcomer's headroom question.** A new prospective builder, **Ernie** ("whittlinfool"), inspired by Don Elliott's articles, found after buying the plans that at 6'4" the berth and sitting headroom were too short, and asked whether to modify the design or give up looking; **David Beard** answered from firsthand experience aboard "Whisper" — cut a little out of the bulkhead #2 hatchway and lower the floor storage bin's top between bulkheads #1 and #2, "I sleep like that on Whisper" — a real **as-built** fix distinct from the archive's earlier, more invasive mockup-based proposals (Pete Hodges's roof-raising/ballast-swap plan, javelin3155's outright rejection). Ernie thanked David and resolved to start building. Added as a new section + citations [18]-[20] to [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) (the best-matched existing home — no new file needed), with matching cross-linked sections in [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 104) and [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (citation 38). Ernie ("whittlinfool") stays a one-off inline mention (two messages, single thread, no boat yet) per the people threshold. **(3) Dave Bolduc's GPS follow-up.** About a week after Glen Maxwell's Garmin recommendation (batch 67), **Dave Bolduc** reported buying a **Garmin eTrex Mariner** and posted three same-morning follow-up messages: detailed cheaper-alternative sourcing for the power/data cable adaptor (down from West Marine's $37 to a $22.50 DIY combo), a pointer to the free **EasyGPS** waypoint-upload program, a **MeMap** firmware hack replacing the unit's startup warning with his own contact info as an anti-theft measure, and a pointer to a free `smallboatgps.com` PDF usage guide. Added as new sections + citations [3]-[5] to [GPS and navigation instruments](/operations/gps-navigation.md), with a matching cross-linked section in [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (citations 40-42). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced in any of the six touched files (the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits — the new Glen Maxwell citations `[130]`-`[133]` sit well clear of them). Updated index descriptions in [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) (crew-size-and-fit and GPS entries), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (Whisper entry), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, David Beard, Dave Bolduc entries). No promotions or prunings — Ernie and the two new one-off spam-poster handles all stay correctly below the people threshold (thin/single-purpose, no boat, no follow-up). No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **680**.

* **Batch 67 (pages 661–670, msgnum 663–672, offset +2 holding steady, 27–31 August 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each distinct (no gaps/duplicates against batch 66's msgnum 662 endpoint); noted one genuine near-duplicate within the batch itself — Larry Whited sent two separate short "thanks" replies (page_663/664, msgnum 665/666) less than 15 minutes apart, both preserved as distinct citations rather than merged, consistent with the archive's past-established double-post handling. Two threads plus a recurring nuisance and a thin new equipment topic: **(1) The "Pipe Stowage rail" thread's coda — where the yuloh plans come from.** Larry Whited (a one-off, inline-only list member previously tracked for the Cedar Key meetup) asked whether the Paradox plans include yuloh-building instructions or where builders sourced them; Dave Bolduc confirmed **page 12 of the Paradox plans has the yuloh diagram** itself, pointing to two external construction/sculling-technique sites as further reading. Added as a new section + citations [12]-[15] to [yuloh and stowed-rig retaining loops](/components/yuloh.md), with matching cross-linked sections in [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (citation 39). **(2) The "New Pictures" spam thread — a third occurrence, now identified by name.** The same recurring poster ("ztqo6mcoaq," confirmed by matching Yahoo profile handle across this and batch 66's near-identical unanswered repeat) posted a third bare tripod link; this time it drew a pile-on of replies — Larry Whited (java-popup complaint), **David LeBlanc** (rejoining the list after his last recorded appearance to state flatly "It was spam and not a legit post"), and Glen Maxwell ("If I knew how to Spam the originator I would") — the sharpest response yet, though still no moderation action taken. Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citations 124-127) and [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md) (citation 30, his first appearance since his 5 August 2002 "Lovebird" aside). **(3) New: GPS and navigation instruments.** David Beard asked what people thought of the entry-level Garmin GPS ($129.99, West Marine) for marine use; Glen Maxwell gave a strong recommendation from eight-plus years' experience with his own Garmin GPS 45, explaining his Mariner unit's pre-programmed US navigational aids. This is the archive's only recorded discussion of **buying** a GPS (as opposed to using one to log speed/position data, already covered in [hull speed](/operations/hull-speed.md) and [Gulf Stream crossing](/operations/gulf-stream-crossing.md)) — thin (one Q, one A) but a genuinely new topic with no existing home, so given a compact **new concept**, [GPS and navigation instruments](/operations/gps-navigation.md), rather than forced into an unrelated file. Extended [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citations 128-129) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 103). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced in any of the six touched files (the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits). Updated index descriptions in [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (yuloh entry), [operations/index.md](/operations/index.md) (new GPS entry), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, Dave Bolduc, David Beard, David LeBlanc entries). No promotions or prunings — Larry Whited remains correctly below the people threshold despite five messages this batch (all thin/inline in nature, consistent with his existing status). No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **670**.

* **Batch 66 (pages 651–660, msgnum 653–662, offset +2 holding steady, 22–27 August 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each distinct (no gaps/duplicates against batch 65's msgnum 652 endpoint). Two threads: **(1) The "Sail" thread's final exchange** — [Ron Eike](/people/ron-eike.md) asked Glen Maxwell who had actually built his sail; Glen answered with his fullest account yet — **Sails Ect.**, St. Augustine FL (904-810-1966), 85-90% satisfied, and a **second price correction** (his true cost, per his receipt, was **$420**, not the $450 he had given days earlier), plus fit/performance detail (tack-fitting liberties, a light-air wrinkle off the wind, 4.5 oz British cloth). Added as a new closing section + citations [32]-[33] to [rig and sail](/components/rig/index.md), plus matching sections in [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citation 119) and the newly-promoted [Ron Eike](/people/ron-eike.md) (below). A near-identical, **unanswered** repeat of the "spammed pictures" incident from batch 65 also landed here (one-off poster "ztqo6mcoaq," a bare tripod link, no reply at all this time) — recorded only as a one-line addition to Glen Maxwell's existing spam-aside section. **(2) A new "Pipe Stowage rail" thread** — David Beard asked how to install the Paradox's port-and-starboard pipe stowage rails (the fittings that retain the [yuloh and stowed rig](/components/yuloh.md) on deck), drawing three distinct techniques: Glen Maxwell's detailed epoxy-and-filler method (3/4 in hole, 60-grit scuff, 404-filler/cabosil fill — with a same-day correction to 5/8 in) plus a later torch-removal tip; **Dave Bolduc**'s removable bolt-on alternative from **Little Cruiser** (prompted by his own project of reinstalling worn rails to carry their yuloh on the Bolducs' next trip); and **Alastair**'s speculative U-bolt/studding idea (Glen judged it workable but more labor). This is the archive's most detailed deck-hardware installation write-up to date; added as new sections + citations [3]-[9] to [yuloh and stowed-rig retaining loops](/components/yuloh.md) (renumbering its two pre-existing microcruising.com citations from [3]-[4] to [10]-[11] to keep the new archive citations grouped with the related existing thread), with matching cross-linked sections in [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md) (citations 120-123), [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (citation 38), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 49), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 102), and [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (citation 37). **Promotion**: **[Ron Eike](/people/ron-eike.md)** promoted from inline mention to a full person page — he crossed the people threshold on sustained recurrence (KakWorm virus-scare confirmation Nov 2001, the December 2001 census, and now four sailmaking messages spanning 15-22 August 2002, including naming and vouching for two of the thread's three sailmakers) despite never building or owning a Paradox, the same basis as the existing [David LeBlanc](/people/david-leblanc.md) page; cross-linked from [Chris Becker](/people/chris-becker.md), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (both virus-thread mentions), and [rig and sail](/components/rig/index.md) (replacing a stale `/people/index.md` link and an outdated "still below the people threshold" note). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced in any of the nine touched files (the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits). Updated index descriptions in [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) (Whisper), [design/index.md](/design/index.md) (yuloh entry), and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Glen Maxwell, Dave Bolduc, Alastair, David Beard entries, and the new Ron Eike entry). No new backlog items triggered; both existing Open items remain correctly deferred (this batch did not reach post-1000 and did not touch the `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collision area). Cursor advanced to **660**.
* **Batch 65 (pages 641–650, msgnum 643–652, offset +2 holding steady, 17–22 August 2002).** Read all ten source `.eml` files and confirmed each distinct (no gaps/duplicates against batch 64's msgnum 642 endpoint). Two threads this batch, both continuations of threads batch 64 had left open, plus one thin administrative aside: **(1) "To seal or not?" answered** — [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) replied the same day to David Beard's bulkhead-#3 epoxy-sealing question (page_640/batch 64), citing his own [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) repair technique (dry, soak with unthickened epoxy, fill with wood flour/microballoons, then seal with epoxy, paint, or varnish) — added as a new section + citation [15] to [checking a used Paradox for rot](/construction/rot-inspection-ice-pick.md), closing out the long bulkhead-#3 saga on an answered note (was previously unanswered). **(2) The "Sail" thread continued and closed out** — a week after his original sailmaker request (batch 64), David Beard reported a **$550 quote from Dabbler sails** (5 oz Dacron with bag), drawing a round of price-comparison replies: Dave Bolduc (Sailrite kit ~$223-$323 vs. ~$250 DIY-materials), Glen Maxwell ($450, tanbark — an update to his earlier $420 figure), and Alastair (~$500/£300, tan, 6 oz — the archive's only UK sail price). Added a new "A price comparison..." section + citations [24]-[27] (renumbered the file's four pre-existing microcruising.com citations to [28]-[31] to avoid a collision) to [rig and sail](/components/rig/index.md). **(3) A thin administrative aside**: a one-off poster ("wuvs2uzw") posted a broken tripod-hosted picture link; Glen Maxwell redirected posters to the files section, and newcomer **Bill Turnbull** identified it as likely spam (matching a near-identical post on another Yahoo group); recorded only as a brief aside in [Glen C. Maxwell](/people/glen-maxwell.md), comparable in weight to the earlier JS/Kak virus incident — no dedicated page warranted (Bill Turnbull and "wuvs2uzw" stay unlinked one-off mentions, below the people threshold). **Wired through**: [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (both threads, citations 35-36), [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citations 101, both threads), [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (citations 36-37), [Alastair](/people/alastair-law.md) (citation 48), and index-description updates to `boats/index.md`, `construction/index.md`, `design/index.md`, and `people/index.md` (Glen Maxwell, David Beard, Dave Bolduc, Alastair entries). **Citation-collision check**: verified no new duplicate citation numbers were introduced in any of the seven touched files (the pre-existing `[71]`/`[72]`/`[84]` collisions in `people/glen-maxwell.md`, already tracked in the backlog, are untouched and unrelated to this batch's edits). No new concepts, promotions, prunings, or renames — all content slotted into existing, well-matched homes. Cursor advanced to **650**.
* **RECONCILIATION (batch 64, page_631–page_640) — completes a batch left incomplete earlier today.** An earlier batch-64 run partially landed content for pages 631–639 (in `design/rig.md`, `design/rudder.md`, and `boats/zoe.md`) but never finished: its `bundle/log.md` entry was lost when a concurrent microcruising.com site-ingestion pass also prepended to `log.md` at the same time (see the "single writer only" standing rule this incident prompted, now recorded in `.okf-progress/backlog.md`), and downstream cross-links/index updates were left incomplete. This pass read all ten source `.eml` files (page_631–page_640, Yahoo msgnum 633–642, 14–17 August 2002, offset +2 holding steady from batch 63) and reconciled rather than re-drafted: **nine of the ten pages' content was already correctly present** — the "Sail" thread (Mike Westfield's "make it yourself," Ron Eike's three sailmaker names — Dabbler, Douglas Fowler, Todd Bradshaw — and his follow-up, Glen Maxwell's decline-to-recommend and Messing About in Boats anecdote, Alastair's yard-fastening question and Glen's grommets answer) correctly cited in [rig and sail](/components/rig/index.md) citations 17–23; Dave Bolduc's "Paradox rudder" essay (why the rudder was built large, the Sven Yrvind/Bris comparison, the Little Cruiser rudder tracing and measured offsets) and his offsets-delivery follow-up correctly cited in [rudder and chine experiments](/components/rudder.md) citations 11–12; and Glen Maxwell's "New Files" interior/chart-table photo announcement present in [Zoë](/boats/zoe.md) — though there mislabeled with a placeholder citation number **`[X1]`** instead of a proper sequential number, now **fixed to `[20]`** and moved to its correct chronological position at the end of the citations list. **Page 640 — "To seal or not?"** (David Beard, redoing "Whisper"'s interior, asking whether to epoxy-seal over the bulkhead-#3 area previously rot-treated per Dave Carnell) was in fact already present in [checking a used Paradox for rot](/construction/rot-inspection-ice-pick.md) (citation 14, the "five-months-later coda" section) from the earlier incomplete run, but had **not been cross-linked** from the boat/person pages a normal batch would touch. Filled those gaps: added a "To seal or not?" section + citation to [Whisper](/boats/whisper.md) (citation 34) and [David Beard](/people/david-beard.md) (citation 100), a short cross-reference addition to [Dave Carnell's boat-building articles](/references/dave-carnell-articles.md), and updated the stale index descriptions in [construction/index.md](/construction/index.md) (rot-inspection entry), [boats/index.md](/boats/index.md) and [people/index.md](/people/index.md) (Whisper/David Beard entries) to mention the August coda — none of which had been updated by the incomplete run. No content was lost or duplicated; no new concepts, promotions, or renames were needed — this batch's one new thread (the rot-reseal question) slots into the already-promoted `rot-inspection-ice-pick.md` leaf. Resolved the "RECONCILE batch 64" and contributed to the "verify log.md integrity" backlog items (see `.okf-progress/backlog.md`); cursor advanced to **640**.
* **New source — Dave's Wiki (aiki.pbworks.com, Dave Raftery), two pages + images**: On user request, scraped two pages from the PBworks wiki of **Dave Raftery** (a prospective Enigma builder; distinct from Dave Bolduc) — "**Advantages of Matt Layden mini cruisers**" and "**Enigma sailboat**" — and **added their contents to the bundle**. Both pages, plus **all 30 wiki-hosted images** (Paradox/Enigma photos; ~2.2 MB), were fetched via curl and **mirrored to `source-web/daves-wiki/`** with a `MANIFEST.tsv`. Added a new source concept, [Dave's Wiki](/references/daves-wiki.md), with citation form `Dave's Wiki (aiki.pbworks.com) — "Page Title" (URL), Dave Raftery — <detail>`; it is treated as a **secondary/compiling source** (lower authority than microcruising.com), valued mainly for the **primary quotations** it carries (Matt Layden c. 2006, George Van Sickle, Sven Yrvind, Don Elliott, "Al"/Alastair, the Bolducs) and for detailed Enigma technical data; pages dated ~2010 and ~2012 (PBworks edit stamps), content 2005–2012. **Chiefly a major enrichment of [Enigma](/matt-layden/enigma.md)**: added fuller Enigma 360 particulars and a construction schedule, and — new to the bundle — the **Enigma 460**, a 2007 4.6 m plywood-hardtop-cabin variant (LOA 15'1", beam 3'9", draft 7⅜", disp 1,010 lb, SA ~95 sq ft, three watertight compartments) whose prototype was built by **George Van Sickle** (sea trials summer 2010), with its own construction schedule and ballast; plus a **Paradox / Enigma-360 / Enigma-460 comparison table**, the light-displacement "crew-weight-as-ballast … could even plane" contrast, a **chine-runner evolution** note ("higher-aspect foils … an improvement over Paradox's runners"), and Matt Layden's own **April 2006 statements that Enigma building plans were being prepared** (relayed also via Don Elliott). **Also enriched**: [chine runners](/chine-runners/how-they-work.md) (the wiki's airfoil/heel/big-rudder definition; the Enigma higher-aspect-foil evolution; and Sven Yrvind's claim that Matt created the chine-runner "in 1982"); [weight and ballast](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (ballast-material densities sand/steel/lead, and Enigma's sand/steel/lead ballast choices); [crew size and cabin fit](/operations/crew-size-and-fit.md) (usable-beam geometry — Paradox 4' minus side shelves ≈ 3'6" vs. Enigma 460's 3'9"; 7'7" crew compartment; sleeps-one/two-snug; George Van Sickle's headroom quote); [furling system](/components/rig/furling.md) (a clear third-source description of the boom-rotating drum mechanism); and [sailing characteristics](/operations/sailing-characteristics.md) (sailing/reading standing in the hatch, with Alastair's quote). **Discrepancies flagged rather than smoothed**: the Enigma runner shape is "shorter and **deeper**/higher-aspect" here vs. microcruising.com's "shorter and **wider**"; the chine-runner concept dated to **1982** (Sven) vs. the 1985 first-boat Swamp Thing; and Enigma 360 length given as **12'3"** here vs. 11'10"/3.6 m on microcruising.com. **George Van Sickle** (Enigma 460 prototype builder) and the Paradox "**Enuf**" (a hull with a lift-out rear window) are kept as inline mentions per the people/boats thresholds. Updated the [matt-layden](/matt-layden/index.md) (Enigma entry) and [references](/references/index.md) indexes; no pruning or renames.
* **New source — microcruising.com (David & Mindy Bolduc's website), full site pass**: On user request ("plow the contents of https://www.microcruising.com/ … read page by page in a subagent as we would read the forum contents … add to the okf bundle"), ingested the **entire microcruising.com website** — the first **non-mailing-list** source added to the bundle. The live site (a static site frozen ~Dec 2019) was **crawled and mirrored locally** to `source-web/microcruising/` via a curl-based BFS crawler (WebFetch was 403-blocked; curl worked), then converted to clean text for reading. A **follow-up image scrape** then downloaded **all 1,643 referenced images** (inline photos, gallery thumbnails and the full-size photos they link to, and design-particular diagrams — ~57 MB), preserving the site's directory layout; `MANIFEST.tsv` was regenerated as a full file→URL map (`relpath / kind / bytes / url`) covering all **135 pages + 1,643 images = 1,778 files**. **Reading was done page-by-page by seven parallel Sonnet subagents** (batches A–G: Layden boats; long-form articles; Bolduc's guide/forum commentary; projects/how-tos; the multi-part refit + inventory; trips/blog/news; reviews/famous-boats/misc), each producing a structured findings report (verbatim quotes + suggested destinations) under `scratchpad/mc/findings/`; **integration into the tree was done by the main agent**, with well-scoped new-page drafting and append-only enrichment further parallelized across Sonnet subagents. Established a **website citation scheme** — `microcruising.com — "Page Title" (URL), David & Mindy Bolduc — <detail/quote>` — anchored by a new site-level reference concept. Because the site is authored first-person by Dave Bolduc (the plans seller, who knew Matt Layden personally, owns Little Cruiser, and later Enigma), it is treated as a high-quality **primary source**; opinions are attributed, and content is dated/past-tense per the historical-archive convention. **12 new concepts created**: four Matt Layden **designs** filed under `matt-layden/` (not `boats/`, per the designer-branch rule) — [Terrapin](/matt-layden/terrapin.md) (c.1985, the Maine-knockdown origin of chine runners), [Enigma](/matt-layden/enigma.md) (2005, "Enigma 360"), [Sand Flea](/matt-layden/sand-flea.md) (8' pram), [Elusion](/matt-layden/elusion.md) (9', stand-up-paddle auxiliary); a new person [Mindy Bolduc](/people/bolduc/mindy.md); four new operations/construction leaves — [Gulf Stream crossing](/operations/gulf-stream-crossing.md), [provisioning — food and water](/operations/provisioning-and-water.md), [sculling and yuloh technique](/operations/sculling-and-yuloh-technique.md), [sailmaking](/construction/sailmaking.md), [folding dinghy](/construction/folding-dinghy.md); and two new references — [microcruising.com](/references/microcruising-website.md) and the book [Beach Cruising and Coastal Camping](/references/beachcruising-book.md) (Ida Little & Michael Walsh, which features Swamp Thing). **The whole `matt-layden/` branch was substantially enriched**: [biography](/matt-layden/biography.md) gained a full **design-lineage section** (Terrapin→Swamp Thing→GJAC→Little Cruiser→[20' Balboa interlude with future wife **Karen**]→Paradox→Enigma→Sand Flea→Elusion, with dates, wintering-aboard, Watertribe field-testing, and Matt's own 2019 Cedar Key talk with author Ida Little present); [original Paradox](/boats/original-paradox.md) gained **canonical design particulars** and a cruising record (built 1993; CT→ICW→Ragged Islands; the 1994 tandem-with-Little-Cruiser Bahamas cruise; ~3,000 mi; **the 300-mile Everglades Challenge win now dated to 2003**); [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md) gained particulars, the **Gulf Stream freighter-wake rollover-and-self-right** anecdote, and the **Dave Gatan → 2004 hurricane rescue → 2005–2008 rebuild** provenance; [GJAC](/matt-layden/gjac.md) gained its 14'10" lineage and the correction that **its leeboards were added by a later owner** (as designed she used chine runners); [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) gained particulars, a 10,000-mile cruising record, scantlings, the 2005 epoxy/glass sheathing refit, and its sail history. **Design pages enriched**: [chine runners](/chine-runners/origin-and-evolution.md) (origin, "hydrodynamic fence," runner-vs-centerboard boat map and firsthand verdict), [weight and ballast](/design/weight-and-ballast.md) (the designer's **layered ballast** — water tanks + stores + lead, with a claimed 165° self-righting), [blue-water suitability](/design/blue-water-suitability.md) (Dave Bolduc's firsthand "**tough coastal cruisers, not offshore boats**" verdict, which largely settles the old February-2002 debate and answers its open knockdown question), plus [rig](/components/rig/index.md), [rudder](/components/rudder.md), [furling](/components/rig/furling.md), and [yuloh](/components/yuloh.md). **Operations enriched**: ground-tackle, heavy-weather-tactics, sailing-characteristics, hull-speed, cabin-heating, auxiliary-outboard-power, trailering, crew-size-and-fit, first-boat-suitability, and group-cruise-reports (the Bolducs' 1993–2013 voyage log, the 1994 buddy-boat cruise with Matt, and the Cedar Key/WCTSS gatherings). **Construction enriched**: bottom-painting, topside-painting, non-skid-deck, epoxy-coating-technique, hull-planking, fastening-and-gluing (paint/epoxy/rot-repair/glass-schedule specs from the Bolduc refits). **People/references enriched**: [Dave Bolduc](/people/bolduc/dave.md) (origin story, Enigma ownership, 2013 solo Bahamas crossing), [Don Elliott](/people/don-elliott.md) (**confirmed deceased** — "the late Don Elliott" — and his 16-article compilation corroborated), [Chris Morejohn](/people/chris-morejohn.md) (the Hogfish sharpies), and the plans/articles/study-plans references. **Key reconciliations/corrections**: an initial subagent over-correction ("ballast is water, not lead") was caught and integrated correctly as a **layered lead + water + stores** scheme (the SCA interview explicitly gives "several hundred pounds of lead"); the Sketch1.htm "16 part construction article" **corroborates** (does not contradict) the bundle's existing 16-article-compilation vs. 5-part-MAIB-serialization finding; the `physics.bgsu.edu/~layden/…` construction-photo link was **left flagged as unverified** (no university affiliation asserted); minor per-source spec discrepancies (e.g. Enigma beam 43" vs 3'4"; the "sixth micro-cruiser / five during the full-time decade" nuance) were preserved rather than smoothed over. Updated the `matt-layden`, `boats`, `design`, `operations`, `construction`, `people`, and `references` indexes. A bundle-wide internal-link integrity check (2,856 links) confirmed **this pass introduced zero broken links**; also fixed one **pre-existing** broken link surfaced by the check ([plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md](/construction/plywood-grades-and-foam-flotation.md)'s `/non-skid-deck.md` → `/construction/non-skid-deck.md`). No pruning or renames; all new Layden boats slot into the already-promoted designer branch, and all site material extends already-promoted, trunk-adjacent concepts rather than fragmenting the tree. The local `source-web/microcruising/` mirror makes every website citation reproducible even if the live site changes.
* **Promotion (structural, no new source read)**: On user request, promoted **GJAC** — one of Matt Layden's older boats, previously an inline mention only (one Sept-2001 rudder photo, one June-2002 resale listing, judged below the boats threshold) — to its own concept at [GJAC](/matt-layden/gjac.md). Filed under `matt-layden/` as a distinct older Layden **design** (a sibling of [Little Cruiser](/matt-layden/little-cruiser.md) and [Swamp Thing](/matt-layden/swamp-thing.md)), **not** a `boats/` Paradox hull, since GJAC is a different design (14.5 ft x 4 ft x 9 in, no cabin top, chine runners) rather than a build of the Paradox design. The new concept consolidates both existing data points (the half-immersed rudder photo from page_060 and the full "GJAC is for sail" listing from page_513) with owner Don Musante, dimensions, the no-cabin-top difference, and Dave Bolduc's leeboards-vs-ballast opinion. Added GJAC to [matt-layden/index.md](/matt-layden/index.md)'s Designs list; rewired the inline mentions in [operations/secondhand-market.md](/operations/secondhand-market.md) (now the canonical home for the market/listing side only, its "remains an inline mention" note softened), [people/dave-bolduc.md](/people/bolduc/dave.md), and [design/rudder.md](/components/rudder.md) to link to the new concept. No facts or citations lost; no other renames or pruning. Also introduced a new process file, `.okf-progress/backlog.md`, so deferred "promote/consolidate later if X surfaces" hints (like this one originally was) have a durable home that batch workers read and append to.
* **Update (batch page_621–page_630)**: Ten archived pages (Yahoo Msgnum 622–630 and 632, 8–14 August 2002, offset +1 confirmed for page_621→622 through page_629→630). All ten confirmed genuinely distinct by `X-Yahoo-Msgnum`/Message-ID/body — no duplicates. **One numbering gap confirmed**: page_630 is msgnum **632**, not 631 — checked `source/page_631.eml` directly (outside this batch's scope) and confirmed it is msgnum **633** ("Re: Sail," a reply to this batch's final message, from `n6pfk`), so the file-to-msgnum offset of +1/+2 continues consistently and msgnum 631 is simply a page genuinely missing from the archive's `page_NNN.eml` capture (not a mislabeling or a page owed to this batch) — flagged for awareness only, no action needed.
